meta.executiveSummary:
A 13-category sweep (~570 candidate finds) over a very mature zero-dep vault converges on one strategic thesis: the vault's moat is its headless-Chrome SAVE-GATE, and that gate is the single most under-built, least-duplicable, highest-leverage surface remaining. The gate today scores only static-regex slop (hex/duration/radius/left-border-card), an imagery-PRESENCE floor, and a binary "did it draw" — it is blind to accessibility (zero ARIA/contrast-on-gradient/focus-order/reduced-motion-honoring), to typography line-setting (widows/measure/clamp truncation), to overlay/open-state correctness (top-layer/occlusion/clip), to motion budget, to determinism (same-seed=same-pixels), and to WebGPU/context-loss. Almost every category independently nominated a verification gate as its #1 pick. So the plan front-loads ~10 gate capabilities that compound on infrastructure already present (lib/inspect.js already computes WCAG luminance but GIVES UP on gradient backgrounds; render-smoke already reads pixel variance but never checks context-loss/animation-liveness/determinism).

Second thesis: one missing PRIMITIVE unlocks an entire effect class. The shaders runner is single-pass-only (fluid.glsl.js's own header admits it fakes fluid "without ping-pong"), so a ~120-line vanilla PingPong FBO primitive unlocks reaction-diffusion, true fluid, bloom/tonemap chains, and LUT grading — none fakeable today. Likewise a seedable-PRNG + real-CPU-noise pair is the determinism substrate the generative-art skill demands but never shipped.

Third thesis: the platform itself is deleting JS in 2025-26 — CSS anchor positioning, Popover+Invoker, scroll-driven timelines, scroll-state() queries, native carousels, customizable <select>, light-dark(), contrast-color(), field-sizing — and the vault's floating/scroll/theming layers are still 100% getBoundingClientRect/IntersectionObserver/duplicated-token JS. These are net-new, license-free (web standards), and ship behind @supports with the existing JS as the graceful fallback.

Fourth: genuinely NET-NEW capability gaps (not effects) — DTCG token export (the vault's first interchange format; everything is already CSS-var-driven), an accessibility skill (30 skills, zero on a11y), photoreal 3D defaults (scene-runner sets no toneMapping/environment — the #1 flat-metal tell), and runtime imagery (ThumbHash/responsive-<picture> emitter; the vault has skeletons but zero per-image placeholder/srcset). License terrain is clean; the recurring traps to AVOID-as-dep are axe-core (MPL-2.0 — inject unmodified via CDN at test time only, never bundle), LYGIA/paper-design (Prosperity/PolyForm), apca-w3 (restricted — reimplement the formula), and SaaS sources (Mobbin/Gamma/Lovable — inspiration only).

topToBuild.name: PingPong FBO compute primitive (companion to ShaderRunner)
topToBuild.why: Single highest-leverage ADD in the sweep — an enabling primitive, not one effect. Verified: runner.js is single-pass-to-default-framebuffer only; fluid.glsl.js's header admits it fakes fluid 'without ping-pong'. ~120 lines vanilla WebGL2 (guard EXT_color_buffer_float, degrade to static) unlocks a whole net-new CLASS: reaction-diffusion, true Stam fluid, bloom/tonemap chains, LUT grade, slime-mold, erosion — none fakeable today.
topToBuild.license: Public domain / MIT (reimplement from nullprogram CC0 + tdhooper/webgl-grayscott MIT)

topToBuild.name: Contrast gate that samples pixels BEHIND glyphs (worst-case fg/effective-bg)
topToBuild.why: Flagship gap: lib/inspect.js bgOf() literally returns null and SKIPS the contrast check the moment text sits on a gradient/image/backdrop-filter — i.e. blind on exactly the mesh/aurora surfaces the vault composes by default. The WCAG luminance math already exists in-repo (lum/parseRgb); only swap the give-up path for sampling the rendered --screenshot PNG under each glyph rect. Makes an already-scored check TRUE. No new dep.
topToBuild.license: N/A (own vanilla technique; reuses existing WCAG math)

topToBuild.name: axe-core ARIA/role/focus-order audit injected into the headless gate
topToBuild.why: Turns the ~6-check heuristic gate into a real WCAG audit. Net-new vs the in-repo contrast/alt checks: ARIA validity, role-vs-allowed-attribute, required-children, accessible-name, positive-tabindex focus-order — the biggest bug class a pixel-blind composer emits. CRITICAL: MPL-2.0 is file-level copyleft — inject the UNMODIFIED CDN bundle at test time, behind need()-guard, never fork or bundle into emitted pages.
topToBuild.license: MPL-2.0 (FLAG: unmodified CDN inject only; do not fork/bundle)

topToBuild.name: elementFromPoint occlusion + clip-rect open-state gate
topToBuild.why: Catches the #1 AI-compose failure: a decorative hero / leftover scrim / ::after band covering the primary CTA, and clipped/off-screen dropdowns. The gate only ever asserts the CLOSED state today. Combine CDP DOM.getTopLayerElements (open-overlay oracle) + an elementsFromPoint hit-test sweep (already partially scaffolded in inspect.js:549) + ancestor clip-rect walk. Pure moat no snippet library can copy.
topToBuild.license: N/A (CDP BSD-style via existing playwright/chrome harness)

topToBuild.name: Reduced-motion HONORING gate (render-twice diff under emulated reduce)
topToBuild.why: The vault ships 920+ motion snippets and CLAIMS each has a guard; nothing verifies it. inspect.js has ZERO media-feature emulation. Render once normal, once with prefers-reduced-motion forced, assert getAnimations() empties / animated selectors quiet. Also catches scroll-scrub.js freezing at blank frame 0. Upgrades audit.js's string-presence check into a real semantic gate.

topToBuild.name: DTCG 2025.10 token export/import (round-trip .tokens.json)
topToBuild.why: The vault's FIRST machine-interop artifact and its single highest-leverage handoff. Everything is already CSS-var-driven yet it emits only a Markdown DESIGN.md. ~150 lines: walk cssRules, classify --* by $type, emit 2025.10-correct OBJECT color {colorSpace,components,hex} + {value,unit} dims (bare hex fails strict validators), preserve scales→palette→semantic as {alias} refs. Opens Figma/Penpot/Style Dictionary/Tokens Studio with zero build.

topToBuild.name: Seedable PRNG + real CPU noise pair (prng.js + noise.js)
topToBuild.why: The determinism substrate everything generative sits on. The generative-art skill says 'always seed the PRNG' but ships NO primitive; the vault has only sin-wave fake noise (vortex/wavy) and GLSL-locked noise. Ship sfc32+mulberry32+xmur3 (public-domain, byte-identical across engines) + a vendored ~40-line simplex core (v4 takes an injected PRNG). Underpins flow fields, stippling, terrain, and the reproducible-render gate.

topToBuild.name: Reproducible-render gate (same-seed=same-pixels, render-twice)
topToBuild.why: The generative moat and a clean extension of the save-gate. Render a seeded composition twice in one run, hash the PNG, FAIL on diff — proving determinism with NO committed baselines, GPU-portably. Also statically flags Math.random/Date.now/devicePixelRatio in generation. Enforces the PRNG primitive is actually used. Carries the WebGL preserveDrawingBuffer readback caveat from MEMORY.

topToBuild.name: Photoreal 3D render config + procedural IBL (scene-runner defaults)
topToBuild.why: Highest design-impact-per-line for 3D and the #1 documented AI tell (flat default metal). scene-runner.js sets NO toneMapping/outputColorSpace/environment. ~4 renderer lines (ACESFilmic/AgX + sRGB) + a ~12-line RoomEnvironment+PMREM helper lift ALL 10 existing scenes at once to cinematic, roughness-aware reflections. Zero external asset, screenshot-stable, prerequisite for native transmission glass.

topToBuild.name: ThumbHash placeholder + responsive-<picture> emitter + per-img quality gate
topToBuild.why: Closes the imagery seam end-to-end. Vault has skeleton shimmer but ZERO runtime per-image placeholder and NO srcset/ emitter (images.skill says 'serve via picture/srcset' but ships none). ThumbHash (MIT, ~120 lines, carries aspect→kills CLS) + a vanilla picture emitter (AVIF/WebP/JPEG, width/height, fetchpriority) + a per- Lighthouse-grounded gate (LCP-not-lazy, modern-format, alt). Turns the vague imagery floor into an enforceable 0-100 subscore.
topToBuild.license: MIT (ThumbHash); emitter rules web.dev Apache-2.0 reimplemented

topToBuild.name: a11y.skill.md decision tree
topToBuild.why: 30 skills, ZERO on accessibility — the missing judgment layer. Routes the four generation-time choices: content-vs-decorative image→alt/aria-hidden; dynamic update→live-region politeness; reduced-motion→remove/substitute/keep + flat fallback; forced-colors safety. Sits beside taste/color/art-direction skills (which actively produce gradient-heavy pages that fail HCM/contrast). The doc the new a11y gates and primitives hang off.

topToBuild.name: FocusTrap + inert shared primitive
topToBuild.why: drawer.js comments 'focus trap basics' but ships NO trap; dialog.js traps without inert (background stays in a11y tree); 5+ overlays each hand-roll partial buggy logic. One ~60-line primitive (inert + re-query-on-Tab + focus restore) retrofits all of them and deletes duplication. inert is Baseline since 2023.

topToBuild.name: Native floating primitive: Popover API + CSS Anchor Positioning + Invoker Commands
topToBuild.why: Deletes the most JS in the vault: every floating panel (popover/tooltip/dropdown/context-menu/command-palette) positions via getBoundingClientRect and toggles via custom JS. Native top-layer kills the z-index/clipped-dropdown bug class. Ship ONE primitive + thin recipes (the 4 anchor-positioning candidates are one feature). Behind @supports with existing JS positioners as fallback; emit only renamed props (position-area, never inset-area).

topToBuild.name: Native scroll-driven primitives: sticky-pin scenes + stacking cards + horizontal pin
topToBuild.why: The vault's scroll layer is 100% JS listeners; ZERO named timelines/view()-ranges/@property step-state. Ship sticky-pinned scrollytelling scenes (@property state machine), the dominant 2025-26 sticky scale-down stacking-card pattern (net-new — card-stack.css is a timed rotating deck), and a pure-CSS horizontal pin (retires wheel-hijack horizontal-scroll.js). Compositor-driven, off-main-thread, @supports-guarded + reduced-motion.

topToBuild.name: Directional View Transitions via native types + Navigation API (+ VT-name save-gate)
topToBuild.why: view-transitions.js has a homegrown data-vt-type with ZERO direction concept. Upgrade run() with types + direction:'auto' reading the Navigation API so back-nav auto-reverses. Pair with the VT-name gate (catches the spec-defined silent killer: duplicate view-transition-name at capture → InvalidStateError, morph never plays, no devtools signal). Native, no dep.

topToBuild.name: Bayer + blue-noise dithering post-FX pass (dither.glsl.js)
topToBuild.why: The dominant 2024-26 retro/print web look and a verified true gap: the shaders skill's 'dithering' line points ONLY at restricted sources (paper-design PolyForm, LYGIA Prosperity); halftone.glsl is a dot-grid; CSS .dither-* is SVG turbulence. Bayer is public-domain recurrence, texture-free, sub-0.2ms at 4K; blue-noise variant via inlined CC0 64x64 texture. Mirrors HalftoneShader's shape for drop-in. Replaces the skill's restricted pointers with an owned snippet.
topToBuild.license: Reimplement vanilla; hughsk/glsl-dither MIT + public-domain Bayer + CC0 blue-noise

topToBuild.name: Generalized 8-sector Kuwahara + Lottes CRT shaders (photo-to-painterly + retro)
topToBuild.why: Two cleanest-license net-new shaders serving the authenticity law (turn any stock/local photo into bespoke art). Vault has zero edge-preserving painterly filter and NO GPU CRT/scanline/aperture-grille. Kuwahara is single-pass (fits 60fps budget, MIT GarrettGunnell port); Lottes CRT is header-declared PUBLIC DOMAIN with trinitron/arcade/amber presets. Both drop onto ShaderRunner's imageUniforms.

topToBuild.name: Roving-tabindex + accessible-reorder + live-region Announcer primitives
topToBuild.why: Three foundational a11y primitives the visuals-heavy vault lacks. tabs/menu/select/combobox each re-roll the arrow-key loop while toolbars/radio-groups have NO keyboard JS (2.1.1 fail). sortable/kanban are drag-ONLY (2.5.7 fail — needs a single-pointer move alt, keyboard isn't enough). toasts.js has no aria-live (silent to SR). One controller + one reorder wrapper + one Announcer singleton fix dozens of components.
topToBuild.license: W3C APG / MDN patterns, reimplemented vanilla

topToBuild.name: forced-colors / Windows High Contrast token layer
topToBuild.why: Near-absent (one component touches forced-colors). Rescues the vault's signature gradient/glass/mesh aesthetic — exactly the properties (background-image, box-shadow, backdrop-filter, background-clip:text) that revert in HCM, leaving invisible borderless UI. Drop-in @media(forced-colors:active) mapping roles to system-color keywords. Satisfies WCAG 1.4.3/1.4.11.

topToBuild.name: WebGL context-loss + shader-compile + WebGPU-adapter probes
topToBuild.why: render-smoke's variance gate is fooled by lost contexts (stale buffer = false green), silent shader-compile failures (colored clear passes vacuously), and is fully BLIND to WebGPU contexts. isContextLost()/getError() at read time, COMPILE/LINK_STATUS probes, and a navigator.gpu/device.lost probe are native-API zero-dep gate upgrades closing real holes as the vault follows its own WebGPU/TSL direction.

topToBuild.name: Typography line-census gate (widows/orphans/measure/clamp-truncation)
topToBuild.why: The gate scores type-scale ratio/rhythm but NEVER measures how text wraps — the #2 AI tell (bad line-setting), invisible today. One Range.getClientRects() census yields widows/orphans, measure (45-80ch floor+ceiling, upgrading the crude >95ch heuristic), runaway lines, AND vertical line-clamp truncation (scrollHeight>clientHeight — Playwright itself has no native solution). Pure Web API in the existing probe.

topToBuild.name: Native CSS color modernization: oklch(from --accent) theming + light-dark() + contrast-color()
topToBuild.why: All ~61 palettes are hand-authored static token blocks and dark-mode.css duplicates every token across light/[data-theme] (3x maintenance). Relative-color derives an ENTIRE token system from one --accent at runtime (hover/surface/accent-2/on-accent); light-dark() collapses the dual tables into one declaration; contrast-color() auto-picks legible text. Zero-JS, Baseline, @supports-guarded with static .pal-* as fallback. Unlocks white-label/theme-from-logo-color.

topToBuild.name: Vanilla scale + nice-ticks + data-color-scale dataviz foundation
topToBuild.why: Every chart hand-rolls v/maxVal math with a niceMax() that ceilings to the next power of 10 (axis 0/25/50/75/100 for max 47, half wasted) and no .invert(). Reimplement d3-scale/d3-array core (scaleLinear/Time/Band + {1,2,5}×10ⁿ ticks + nice()) — visibly upgrades EVERY existing y-axis and is the literal prerequisite for crosshair/brush/annotation. Add a value→color interpolator + viridis/cividis (CC0) + ColorBrewer diverging for real magnitude encoding (charts use decorative hex + Math.random fills today).

topToBuild.name: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion + bloom/AgX-tonemap post-FX chain (on PingPong)
topToBuild.why: The first concrete payoffs on the PingPong primitive. Reaction-diffusion gives the on-trend coral/leopard/fingerprint 'alive texture' backgrounds the vault structurally cannot fake (all 18 shaders are single-pass). A bright-pass→mip-blur→AgX tonemap chain lifts EVERY emissive shader (godrays/plasma/raymarch/voronoi) at once — the vault has zero real bloom outside three.js EffectComposer and zero HDR tonemap. Ships named presets like the existing palette tables.
topToBuild.license: MIT (tdhooper/piellardj RD; Froyok bloom + dmnsgn/glsl-tone-map AgX)

verification.count: ~10 in topToBuild (the dominant moat form; ~80 across the full sweep)
examples: Contrast gate sampling pixels behind glyphs (fixes inspect.js bgOf() giving up on gradients)
axe-core ARIA/role/focus-order injected at test time (MPL-2.0 — unmodified CDN only)
elementFromPoint occlusion + CDP top-layer open-state gate

vanilla-snippet.count: ~11 in topToBuild (largest build form; ~200 across the sweep)
examples: Bayer+blue-noise dither, Kuwahara, Lottes CRT, reaction-diffusion, bloom/AgX
forced-colors token layer, DTCG exporter, print.css @page master

skill.count: ~12 across the sweep; ~3 high-priority in topToBuild
examples: chart-chooser.skill.md (FT Visual Vocabulary intent→snippet router; FLAG: don't reproduce FT poster art)

cdn-snippet.count: ~9 across the sweep (each behind a need()-guard, degrades gracefully)
examples: Iconify runtime icon resolver (vault has NO icon engine — emoji only)
MiniSearch/FlexSearch Cmd-K full-text overlay (palette is static-array fuzzy; FlexSearch is Apache-2.0 not MIT)
MediaPipe FaceLandmarker face-react (same Apache-2.0 dep already vetted)

compose-genre.count: ~30 across the sweep (the biggest archetype-expansion opportunity)
examples: api-reference, email-newsletter, link-in-bio, case-study, changelog, glossary, comparison X-vs-Y, conference-landing, resume (print)
Box-layout-spec front-door (region IR → deterministic snippet fill)
Nippon Traditional Colors / Werner 1814 / Sanzo Wada / Dutch Masters (PD/MIT)
Neo-brutalist clash palette + blessed --sp-* spacing scale
Maxime Heckel dither/NPR series (no OSS license on code)
Sonification (Highcharts proprietary — reimplement vanilla Web Audio)
Layout-tree-fingerprint gate (premised on infra the vault lacks — defer)
cult-ui Dynamic Island state vocabulary (React/Framer — port taxonomy only)

trends: Verification-as-moat: every category independently nominated a save-gate diagnostic as its top pick. The durable competitive surface is rendered-FACT verification (computed style + geometry + pixels sampled under glyphs), NOT pixel-diff baselines — and the vault already runs headless Chrome with half the math in-repo.

Native CSS is eating JS: anchor positioning, Popover+Invoker Commands, scroll-driven timelines + scroll-state() container queries, native carousels (::scroll-marker/::scroll-button), customizable <select>, light-dark(), contrast-color(), field-sizing, text-box-trim, interpolate-size. All net-new to the vault, license-free, ship behind @supports with the existing JS as the fallback.

Determinism as a first-class concern (fxhash/Art Blocks 'same hash = same pixels'): seedable PRNG (sfc32) + real CPU noise + a render-twice gate are now table-stakes for generative work and map onto the vault's existing seed-driven compose semantics.

Stateful/iterative GPU is back: ping-pong FBO reaction-diffusion, neural-CA, compute particles — the 'alive texture' looks the single-pass shader era skipped. The PingPong primitive is the gate to all of it.

WebGPU/TSL migration is production (Chrome/FF/Safari late-2025) but compute() is WebGPU-only and does NOT fall back — a real WebGPU→WebGL2-transform-feedback→CPU ladder plus WebGPU-aware verification is mandatory, not optional.

Anti-AI-slop / tactile-print reaction: risograph overprint, CSS halftone, dithering, deckle edges, neo-brutalism, lit-emboss paper texture, true baseline grids — physical/human surfaces that directly counter the 'gradients + no images = AI tell' authenticity law.

Perceptual color by construction: APCA/reverse-contrast solvers compose to HIT a target Lc instead of checking after; OKLCH relative-color derives whole token systems from one hue; P3 wide-gamut as progressive enhancement. (License trap: apca-w3 is restricted — reimplement the formula.)

Accessibility graduated from advice to enforceable baseline: WCAG 2.2 (ISO Oct 2025) added mechanically-verifiable target-size/dragging/focus-not-obscured; forced-colors replaced -ms-high-contrast; APG behaviors are expected. The vault is thin here.

Compact-placeholder + Core-Web-Vitals discipline matured: ThumbHash/CSS-LQIP + explicit width/height + fetchpriority + AVIF/WebP <picture> are now codifiable as a gate dimension, not just authoring advice.

DTCG 2025.10 is the first real token-interchange standard (Figma/Penpot/Style Dictionary/Tokens Studio read it) — and a token-driven vault is the perfect producer. Note the breaking change: color $value is now a structured object, not flat hex.
meta.gaps: Even after this plan, Design Book retains notable capability gaps. INFRASTRUCTURE: no FBO/multi-pass primitive yet (PingPong is the #1 fix, but until it lands the entire stateful-shader class is impossible); no seedable-PRNG/CPU-noise substrate (generative-art skill demands it, ships nothing); no auto-layout engine at all (no Sugiyama/dagre/ELK) so diagrams can only fill fixed templates, never lay out an arbitrary graph; no machine-readable param-manifest so the composer cannot enumerate/drive generative knobs headlessly (Tweakpane is human-only). 3D: scene-runner is WebGLRenderer-only with no WebGPU/TSL sibling, no orbit/damping/fit camera, no GLTFLoader with Draco/KTX2 decoders (compressed .glb fails to load entirely), no Gaussian-splat support, no vanilla physics, no point-cloud/LiDAR path, no postprocessing stack beyond single bloom. ICONS: the vault has NO icon-rendering engine — icon-picker is emoji chars; there is no resolver for the 200k+ permissive icon corpus. VERIFICATION still-missing: no full-text search engine (palette is static-array fuzzy); no print/PDF page-fit or email-compat (caniemail) gate; no JSON-LD/structured-data emit-or-validate; no internal-link/dead-anchor resolution; no motion-budget/effect-stacking gate; no GPU-frametime budget probe. A11y depth: no dismiss-stack manager (nested overlays all collapse on one Escape), no type-ahead listbox search, no focus-appearance pixel gate. ARCHETYPE breadth: ~15+ uncomposable page genres (api-reference, email-newsletter, changelog page, glossary, comparison, conference-landing, resume, scrollytelling-explainer, link-in-bio, case-study) and no box/wireframe-spec front-door (composer is genre-driven only — no region-IR input). DATAVIZ: missing whole FT-taxonomy buckets (boxplot, violin, diverging-bar, bullet, chord, beeswarm), no canvas/streaming data path, no real map engine or projection (only a hardcoded Greece bake), no big-data downsampling (LTTB). FAN-OUT: book_variants re-runs seeds with NO measured pairwise distinctness — variants can be cosmetic reshuffles.

categories.trends: The 2024-2026 shader-fx wave is dominated by (1) retro/print stylization — ordered Bayer + blue-noise dithering, CRT scanline/aperture-grille/barrel passes, and NPR "post-processing as a medium" (luminance cross-hatch, Kuwahara oil-paint, edge-detect ASCII), all popularized by Maxime Heckel's 2024-25 series and Codrops; (2) iOS-26 "Liquid Glass" — true background/lens refraction via SVG feDisplacementMap as backdrop-filter (Chromium-only) with WebGL screen-space-refraction upgrades; and (3) the WebGPU + TSL transition, where compute-shader GPGPU particle fields (1M+ points) are the new ceiling, with the critical gotcha that TSL/WebGPURenderer auto-falls-back only the RENDER path to WebGL2 — compute itself is WebGPU-only, so a real fallback ladder (WebGPU compute -> WebGL2 transform-feedback -> CPU) is mandatory. Cross-cutting all of this: the vault's single-pass screen-only runner.js is the bottleneck — almost every high-value effect (reaction-diffusion, true fluid, bloom/tonemap/DoF, LUT grade) actually needs an FBO ping-pong primitive the vault lacks, making that primitive the highest-leverage enabling add. License hygiene is the recurring trap: LYGIA (Prosperity), paper-design/shaders (PolyForm), particlesGL (paid-commercial), and most cinematic LUT/.cube packs (redistribution-forbidden or CC-BY-SA) are inspiration-only; the clean path is vanilla reimplementation from public-domain math (Bayer recurrence, Blinn metaballs, Kuwahara 1976, Lottes CRT public-domain) plus CC0 assets (blue-noise texture, self-baked HaldCLUTs) and confirmed-MIT references (three.js, dmnsgn/glsl-tone-map, GarrettGunnell, tdhooper). The emerging moat is verification: context-loss survival (WEBGL_lose_context), GPU frametime budgets (EXT_disjoint_timer_query), and NaN/black-frame correctness gates — none of which any competing design tool ships.

topPicks.name: PingPongRunner — vanilla two-texture FBO compute primitive (companion to ShaderRunner)
topPicks.why: The single highest-leverage add: an enabling primitive, not one effect. Verified gap — shaders/runner.js draws single-pass to the default framebuffer only (no createFramebuffer), and fluid.glsl.js's own header admits it fakes fluid 'without multi-pass FBO ping-pong.' Landing this unlocks an entire net-new effect CLASS (reaction-diffusion, cellular automata, slime-mold, true Stam fluid, heat/erosion) that the vault structurally cannot fake today. Zero-dep vanilla WebGL2 (~120 lines), public-domain technique, guards on EXT_color_buffer_float and degrades to static/CSS. Moat-adjacent because it's reusable infrastructure every other multi-pass candidate (bloom chain, LUT, grayscott) builds on.
topPicks.license: Public domain / MIT (reimplemented from nullprogram CC0 + Jim Fisher + tdhooper/webgl-grayscott MIT)

topPicks.name: WEBGL_lose_context survival probe (context-loss recovery gate)
topPicks.why: The strongest moat add and directly extends the vault's existing 'screenshot is truth for WebGL draw' memory + render-smoke.mjs measure() reader. Fires loseContext()/restoreContext() then re-runs the pixel-variance draw check — PASS only if the snippet redraws after a forced GPU reset. This is exactly the production failure (driver hiccup, tab-backgrounding, GPU reset) that takes real shader pages black, and render-smoke proves only first-frame draw, never recovery. No competing design tool gates on context-loss survival. Web-standard, zero-dep, degrades cleanly when the extension is absent (matches harness KNOWN_ISSUES discipline).

topPicks.name: Ordered (Bayer) dithering post-FX pass — shaders/dither.glsl.js
topPicks.why: The dominant 2024-2026 retro/print web look and a verified true gap: the shaders skill's 'dithering' line currently points ONLY at restricted sources (paper-design PolyForm, LYGIA Prosperity), halftone.glsl.js is a rotated dot-grid (continuous radius), and the CSS .dither-* is SVG turbulence noise — none does ordered per-pixel threshold quantization. Bayer is canonical, texture-free, sub-0.2ms at 4K, public-domain recurrence math. Mirrors HalftoneShader's {fragment, proceduralFragment, defaults} so it drops onto ShaderRunner identically. Reimplemented vanilla from hughsk/glsl-dither (MIT) matrix funcs — cleanest possible license path and replaces the skill's restricted pointers with an owned snippet.
topPicks.license: Reimplement vanilla; basis hughsk/glsl-dither MIT + public-domain Bayer recurrence

topPicks.name: Timothy Lottes CRT shader (FixingPixelArt) — vanilla crt.glsl.js
topPicks.why: Public-domain (header-declared by Lottes) = the cleanest possible license for inlining, and a verified gap: the vault has plasma/voronoi/aurora + a ShaderRunner with imageUniforms and CSS post-processing.css, but NO GPU CRT/scanline/aperture-grille/barrel pass. ~123 lines covering scanline beam, RGB shadow-mask, and barrel warp; reimplement as CrtShader.{fragment, defaults, presets} (trinitron/arcade/amber-terminal/pvm/green-phosphor) mirroring PlasmaShader's shape. Pairs directly with the existing demoscene/terminal/retro-grid cluster. Round out with a small CC0 phosphor-glow + chromatic step (Lottes ships no bloom). Highest-value of the retro cluster on license + drop-in fit.

topPicks.name: Liquid-glass card (SVG feDisplacementMap as backdrop-filter + feSpecularLighting rim)
topPicks.why: The canonical iOS-26 Liquid Glass recreation and a TRUE background/lens-refraction capability the vault completely lacks — verified: glass-refraction.css is only chromatic RGB-split text + edge-bleed blob, glassmorphism.css is plain blur, buttons-glass.css is CSS-only. High-value and on-trend. The kube.io pipeline (Snell-refraction RGB-encoded displacement map + feSpecularLighting rim + 3-pass chromatic aberration) is the best pedagogical source; ship original vault code. CRITICAL caveat to bake in: backdrop-filter:url(#svg) is Chromium-only, so it MUST pair with the @supports + prefers-reduced-transparency gate below to degrade to plain blur in FF/Safari. Article-only source, so reimplement.
topPicks.license: No code released (article walkthrough) — reimplement original; inspiration-grade source

topPicks.name: prefers-reduced-transparency + @supports(backdrop-filter:url) fallback gate
topPicks.why: The moat that makes ANY glass component ship-safe, and grep-confirmed net-new (no vault coverage of prefers-reduced-transparency or backdrop-filter:url @supports anywhere). Catches the two ways glass breaks: Chromium-only backdrop-filter:url() failing silently in FF/Safari, and reduced-transparency users getting unreadable translucent surfaces. Headless-Chrome-checkable (emulate the media feature, assert surface goes opaque + text contrast passes). Naturally extends the existing verify save-gate and is the required companion to the liquid-glass card. Web-standard, no license constraint.

topPicks.name: Acerola-style ASCII edge-detect post-pass (font-atlas + DoG + Sobel directional glyphs)
topPicks.why: The recognizable 2025 terminal/dev-tool aesthetic and verified net-new: vault ASCII = hard-coded three.js torus (scenes-pack.js) + block-font text banner (ascii-banner.js) + halftone — NONE is a generic image-input ASCII pass and none do edge detection. The differentiator over a bare luminance ramp is the font-atlas-baked-from-canvas + Difference-of-Gaussians -> Sobel directional-glyph layer (| _ / \). Drops onto the existing ShaderRunner (already accepts sampler2D imageUniforms + fullscreen quad). Reference repo is Go/CLI (spec only); the algorithm is public-domain ShaderToy lineage, so reimplement clean as MIT vanilla GLSL.
topPicks.license: Algorithm public-domain (Acerola/ShaderToy lineage) — reimplement MIT vanilla; reference repo inspiration-only

topPicks.name: 2D screen-space metaball field shader — shaders/metaballs.glsl.js
topPicks.why: The canonical flat lava-lamp/organic-blobs/liquid-nav GPU background, verified net-new at the GPU tier: lava-lamp.css and canvas-fx.js both fake metaballs with the CSS blur(20px)+contrast(20) trick (blurry, no per-blob color/threshold control, no pointer-charge injection) and raymarch-sdf.glsl.js is 3D. A real sum-of-charges + isosurface + screen-space smin field is sharper, cheaper than blur+contrast on large canvases, and tunable. Public-domain math (Blinn 1982) so pure vanilla reimplement, drops onto ShaderRunner.create() like the other 18 *.glsl.js with cosine-palette presets and u_mouse charge injection. CSS trick stays as the lightweight fallback.

topPicks.name: Luminance cross-hatch + pencil-ink editorial shader — npr-hatch.glsl.js
topPicks.why: The most on-brand NPR gap and directly serves the vault's authenticity-law ('bespoke > generic gradient'): verified distinct from halftone.glsl.js (dots), stepped-gradient.css .dither-* (SVG-noise), and watercolor.css (CSS paint). Luminance + modulo diagonal/cross stripes at 3-4 thresholds + optional Sobel ink outline = the editorial/illustrative hand-drawn look the brief wants. Trivially reimplementable from first principles with ZERO dependency and no license entanglement (Heckel/clicktorelease are technique references only). Ships runner.js-compatible {fragment, proceduralFragment, defaults} with palette/line-density/edge-strength uniforms.
topPicks.license: Public-domain math (luminance + modulo stripes + Sobel); reimplement clean

topPicks.name: runner.js post-FX chain: HDR-threshold mip bloom + ACES/AgX tonemap (vanilla)
topPicks.why: Load-bearing: the vault's emissive shaders (godrays/plasma/raymarch/fluid/voronoi) all run single-pass to the canvas with NO real bloom or tonemap — the only UnrealBloom is hard-bound to three.js EffectComposer and cannot layer over runner.js (anti-dup confirmed: only fluid.glsl.js uses an FBO, for its own sim). One offscreen ping-pong chain (bright-pass -> CoD/Froyok 13-tap mip down + 3x3-tent up -> tonemap) lifts EVERY emissive shader at once. Algorithm from CC-BY LearnOpenGL + Froyok/Bloom (MIT) — reimplement from the math. Depends on the PingPongRunner primitive landing first, which is why that ranks above it.
topPicks.license: Algorithm CC-BY-4.0 (LearnOpenGL prose) + Froyok/Bloom MIT — reimplement; resulting snippet original

topPicks.name: dmnsgn/glsl-tone-map (AgX + ACES + Neutral + Filmic + Reinhard, raw GLSL)
topPicks.why: Confirmed-MIT and the cleanest single source for the tonemap pass of the post-FX chain. The vault has ZERO HDR tone mapping — color-grade.css is LDR CSS filter() only and lut-grade.glsl.js is unshipped backlog. AgX (the 2024-25 Blender default) desaturates blown highlights instead of hard-clipping to white at 1.0, which is exactly what fixes over-bright godrays/plasma. Each operator is ~5-15 lines, trivially vendored behind a need()-guard or inlined. Complements color-grade.skill.md as the HDR display-transform tier it lacks. Pairs with and completes the bloom chain above.

topPicks.name: Generalized 8-sector Kuwahara (Acerola / GarrettGunnell Post-Processing)
topPicks.why: The sweet-spot painterly tier: confirmed-MIT (verified at repo level via gh api, 806 stars, pushed 2024), and crucially SINGLE-PASS (8 circular sectors + polynomial weighting, no separate structure-tensor pass) so it fits the vanilla-snippet 60fps budget while killing the blocky artifacts of the classic 4-quadrant filter. The vault has zero edge-preserving/painterly photo filters — directly serves the 'turn any stock/local photo into bespoke art' authenticity goal. HLSL but ports 1:1 to GLSL for runner.js (imageUniforms/sampler2D already exposed). Keep radius<=6, N=8; expose hardness/sharpness/zeta. The anisotropic variant in the same repo is multi-pass — flag as a future tier.

topPicks.name: Canvas-sampled particle-text/image (vanilla WebGL instanced-points, assemble/disperse)
topPicks.why: The signature 2024-2026 hero reveal and verified net-new: the vault's particles (particles.js network/snow/dust, particles-galaxy.js spiral, vortex.js curl, GPGPU flow-field) are ALL background/abstract and NEVER form text or an image from sampled pixels. draw text/image to a hidden canvas -> getImageData -> threshold -> map kept pixels to target XY+color -> instanced points lerp from scatter to target (assemble) and push on cursor (disperse). MIT-style demo basis (Bruno Imbrizi); technique is unencumbered and three is already a need()-guarded vault dep. Clean reduced-motion path (render at targets immediately, no RAF). Pairs with the dissolve disperse mode as a matched reveal/conceal pair.
topPicks.license: MIT-style demo basis; technique vanilla-reimplementable (three.js already a need()-guarded vault dep)

topPicks.name: gkjohnson three.js LUTPass — trilinear 3D-LUT color-grade pass (.cube/.3dl + manual trilinear GLSL)
topPicks.why: Closes a gap the vault's own color-grade.skill.md already lists as backlog (shaders/lut-grade.glsl.js over runner.js) but never shipped — the host exists, the shader doesn't. MIT loader+trilinear algorithm (Garret Johnson); reimplement vanilla per the skill's own 'hand-roll the sampler, no Prosperity/LYGIA' guidance. True 3D color remaps CSS filters cannot express — the GPU sibling of the .grade-* CSS looks. WebGL2 sampler3D with a Hald-strip WebGL1 fallback. Only the LUT DATA carries licensing (see the CC0 HaldCLUT bake item); the shader+loader code is MIT-clean.
topPicks.license: MIT (three.js; trilinear algo Garret Johnson MIT) — reimplement vanilla

topPicks.name: three.js WebGPU TSL compute particles (webgpu_tsl_compute_attractors_particles)
topPicks.why: Gives Design Book the 'compute tier' it completely lacks (vault is fragment-quad + vertex-only point clouds). Canonical MIT, self-contained GPGPU compute particle field (instancedArray storage buffers, renderer.compute() TSL advection, ~262k stock scaling to 1M+) with swappable kernels (curl-noise/attractor/flow-field). Best as a CDN-guarded snippet feature-detecting navigator.gpu. CRITICAL caveat that lowers it below the vanilla primitives: compute does NOT run under WebGPURenderer's WebGL2 fallback (compute is WebGPU-only; only the RENDER path auto-falls-back), so it MUST pair with the transform-feedback fallback below and screenshot-verify the chosen tier actually drew. Also ESM/import-map only (breaks the vault's window.THREE need()-guard convention).

topPicks.name: Compute-tier 'GPU particle field' skill (WebGPU->WebGL2->CPU fallback ladder)
topPicks.why: The decision-tree that ties compute together: feature-detect navigator.gpu -> WebGPU compute advection at 1M+ -> WebGL2 transform-feedback GPU advection -> capped CPU/2D-canvas (reuse particles.js/vortex.js as the floor), honoring prefers-reduced-motion at every tier. Net-new: the vault's 3d-motion/shaders skills only LINK GPGPU/WebGPU as 'inspiration' and flag it future — no skill encodes the compute tier or the compute-doesn't-survive-WebGL2-fallback gotcha. Must state the verification angle: headless Chrome runs WebGPU under SwiftShader inconsistently, so the save-gate screenshot-verifies the tier actually drew rather than trusting navigator.gpu (echoes the vault's WebGL-draw memory). MIT basis (three.js + reimplemented vanilla WebGL2).
topPicks.license: MIT (three.js + reimplemented vanilla WebGL2; Heckel blog code open/educational)

topPicks.name: WebGL2 Transform Feedback GPGPU particles (the no-compute fallback path)
topPicks.why: The load-bearing middle rung of the compute degradation ladder, answering the brief's 'how should it fall back' half. WebGL2 has no compute shaders, so particle state (position/velocity VBOs) is advanced by a transform-feedback vertex shader with rasterization disabled, ping-ponging two buffer sets — still fully on-GPU, still 1M particles, no compute API. This is the correct fallback for the ~20-30% of mid-2026 browsers (default-off Firefox + older devices) without WebGPU, rather than collapsing straight to slow CPU canvas. WebGL2 is ~96% supported. Zero-dep vanilla; port curl from stegu/webgl-noise (MIT), NOT the no-license cabbibo/glsl-curl-noise flagged in the skill.
topPicks.license: Public-domain/BSD-style technique (webgl2fundamentals) — reimplement; curl from stegu/webgl-noise MIT

topPicks.name: Blue-noise dithering variant + CC0 tileable blue-noise texture
topPicks.why: The perceptual middle ground between structured Bayer (visible cross-hatch) and white noise (clumpy) — Maxime Heckel's 2025-26 retro-shading look and the canonical gradient-banding killer, optionally per-frame-shifted for near-invisible animated grain. Ships as a u_mode/u_noise branch INSIDE the same dither.glsl.js so Bayer and blue-noise share one snippet. The enabling asset is Christoph Peters' blue-noise set — CC0 public domain, no attribution — inline the 64x64 PNG as a base64 data-URI so the snippet stays zero-network. Only piece needing an external asset, and CC0 makes it clean. Ranks just below the Bayer pass it extends.
topPicks.license: CC0 / public domain (texture, no attribution); shader reimplemented vanilla

topPicks.name: Classic single-pass Kuwahara filter (4-quadrant) as a vault shader
topPicks.why: The cheap baseline painterly tier below the generalized 8-sector version: public-domain math (Kuwahara 1976, ships in ImageMagick/Blender) so fully reimplementable as zero-dep GLSL for runner.js (imageUniforms/sampler2D already exposed). Highest-impact 'turn any photo into bespoke art' transform serving the imagery-floor + authenticity-law goals. Net-new: vault has duotone/halftone/color-grade/watercolor-CSS but NO photo-to-painterly GPU filter. Budget is explicit: cap MAX_SIZE at radius 6 (~196 fetches single-pass ceiling), expose radius as the abstraction knob. Reimplement the public-domain algorithm, not the unlicensed reference .frag.
topPicks.license: Algorithm public-domain (Kuwahara 1976); reference repo unlicensed — reimplement from the math

topPicks.name: EXT_disjoint_timer_query_webgl2 GPU frame-time budget probe
topPicks.why: A frametime-budget moat the brief explicitly asks for: wrap one render frame in beginQuery(TIME_ELAPSED_EXT)/endQuery, read QUERY_RESULT nanoseconds, check GPU_DISJOINT_EXT to discard throttled samples — flag snippets blowing a budget (>8-12ms on the harness GPU), i.e. heavy raymarch/SDF/fluid shaders that jank on real hardware. The vault ships those today with NO performance floor. GPU timer is desktop-Chrome-only and disjoint-noisy, so it MUST degrade to a rAF-delta CPU jank counter (count frames over 16ms) when QUERY_RESULT is unavailable, matching render-smoke's HARNESS_SOFTWARE discipline. Figma's MIT webgl-profiler is a compact reference. Below the context-loss gate because it's noisier and platform-limited.
topPicks.license: Web standard; reference impl figma/webgl-profiler MIT

topPicks.name: Black-frame / NaN-output correctness probe (frame-content sanity gate)
topPicks.why: Upgrades render-smoke's binary 'drew = variance>5' into a shader-aware verdict: flag all-black/transparent output (the #1 shader failure — a NaN time/resolution uniform or missing texture collapses every fragment to black) and degenerate >99%-one-color frames despite a claimed animated effect, using per-family expected-animation. Reuses the existing offscreen measure() reader in render-smoke.mjs — pure addition, no dep. Scoped honestly as 'sharpen the existing canvas-drew assertion for shaders/' since it partially overlaps the variance check, not a wholly separate gate — which is why it ranks below the two novel gates.

topPicks.name: grayscott.glsl.js — Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion shader pair (state + display)
topPicks.why: The first concrete payoff on top of PingPongRunner: a state-update fragment (3x3 Laplacian + Gray-Scott feed/kill into A,B channels) + a display fragment mapping B to a palette, producing the iconic coral/leopard/fingerprint/mitosis patterns that are on-trend for 2025-26 hero backgrounds and impossible in the vault today (all current shaders are single-pass). Ships named presets (coral/mitosis/fingerprint/spots-and-loops/worms) as feed/kill tuples, mirroring how gradient-flow/mesh-gradient already ship preset tables. MIT references (tdhooper/webgl-grayscott + piellardj, both MIT); ~30-line kernel. Ranks here because it strictly depends on the PingPongRunner primitive landing first.
topPicks.license: MIT (reimplemented from tdhooper/webgl-grayscott + piellardj, both MIT)

topPicks.name: liquidGL (naughtyduk)
topPicks.why: The cross-browser WebGL upgrade path the SVG liquid-glass card degrades to/from: snapshots the page background with html2canvas, uploads it as a texture, and UV-displaces it in a fragment shader (refraction/bevel/frost/magnify/tilt) — works in Chrome/FF/Safari/Edge because it never relies on backdrop-filter:url(). Most mature option in the space (628 stars, ~221 commits, vanilla, syncs with Lenis/GSAP), MIT. Best as a need()-guarded cdn-snippet. Real caveats keep it below the SVG vanilla path: hard html2canvas dep (two guarded scripts, throws if missing), CORS-clean images only, Safari unstable when glass >50% viewport, very long docs exceed GPU texture limits — fallback is CSS frost.
topPicks.license: MIT (+ hard html2canvas dep, also MIT)

topPicks.name: Liquid / metaball nav indicator (merging active-pill that oozes between items)
topPicks.why: A distinct high-demand interaction (Awwwards 'liquid nav') verified net-new: the vault has SVG goo FILTERS (gooey-menu.css, gooey-input.css, blob-cursor) and a static tab-bar active-indicator LINE, but no indicator that gooey-MERGES between targets as it travels. Vanilla reimplement: a .liquid-nav JS animating an absolutely-positioned blob between item rects inside an SVG goo filter (FLIP measure + spring) with an optional click particle burst. React Bits GooeyNav (MIT) is the reference; reimplement without React. Can upgrade to the metaball shader for a true screen-space merge. Ranks here because it partly overlaps existing SVG-goo primitives — the moving/merging behavior is the net-new part.

topPicks.name: Dual-Kawase blur (downsample/upsample) as the bloom blur kernel
topPicks.why: The cheapest high-quality bloom blur (Marius Bjorge/ARM, Kawase 2003): a downsample chain with ~4-tap kernels then upsample, near-Gaussian quality at a fraction of the texture taps — ideal for the vault's 60fps mobile constraint. Offered as a selectable kernel (dual-Kawase vs the mip 13/9-tap) inside the bloom pass so the chain trades softness vs cost. No separable/Kawase/multipass blur exists in shaders/ today. INSPIRATION not a dep: the frost.kiwi article code is unlicensed — reimplement from the documented 4-tap-down/interpolated-up pattern, cross-checking against clean MIT impls (JujuAdams/Kawase). Below the bloom chain it plugs into.
topPicks.license: frost.kiwi article unlicensed — reimplement from the math; MIT refs exist to cross-check

topPicks.name: Named in-house CC0 HaldCLUT set (bake-your-own film-look palette)
topPicks.why: The 'palette' for the GPU LUT tier, extending the .grade-* CSS looks into true 3D color remaps. Worth flagging because there is STILL no cleanly bundleable cinematic .cube pack: FilterGrade/RocketStock/IWLTBAP forbid redistribution, Pat David/Natron HaldCLUTs are CC-BY-SA (attribution+ShareAlike), even MIT-labeled GitHub collections carry a copyright caveat on the LUT data. The only vault-safe path (exactly as color-grade.skill.md prescribes) is BAKING your own neutral-identity HaldCLUT, pushing CSS-filter/ffmpeg transforms through it, and shipping CC0 — owing nobody attribution and keeping the GPU looks byte-aligned with the CSS .grade-* tier. Pairs with the LUTPass item.
topPicks.license: CC0 (self-baked). External packs FLAGGED: redistribution-forbidden / CC-BY-SA / MIT-with-data-caveat

topPicks.name: TSL-vs-raw-GLSL decision skill (when to author in Three Shader Language)
topPicks.why: Net-new DECISION LOGIC the vault lacks: shaders.skill.md has only a short 'WebGPU/TSL note (2026 direction)' stub pointing at an external skill, with no rule for choosing TSL vs the vault's own raw-GLSL runner.js. The encodable insight: raw GLSL via runner.js still wins for tiny zero-dep fullscreen-quad effects (the whole shaders/ corpus); TSL wins for material-integrated effects (displacement, custom lighting, postFX nodes) and WebGPU future-proofing, because one node graph auto-lowers to WGSL on WebGPU and falls back to GLSL/WebGL2. Must flag the friction: await renderer.init() is mandatory and TSL is ESM-only. Best as an EXPANSION of shaders.skill.md plus a cross-link, not a new file — which is why it's a skill-tier add, not an effect.

topPicks.name: Bokeh depth-of-field pass (hexagonal/circular CoC) for the post-FX chain
topPicks.why: DoF is grep-confirmed completely absent from the vault. A screen-space bokeh pass (circle-of-confusion driving a disc/hexagonal-tap blur) adds the cinematic focus-falloff that reads as 'rendered, not flat' on raymarch-SDF and 3d scenes; for the pure-2D runner.js shaders that lack depth it degrades to a tilt-shift radial/linear-gradient CoC. Lowest of the four post-FX passes because the biggest payoff needs a depth buffer (only the 3d/ three.js scenes have one) — ship it third, after bloom + tonemap land. Reimplement from documented gather-bokeh/hexagonal-DoF references (pmndrs DepthOfFieldEffect is Zlib if the three.js path is wanted).
topPicks.license: Reimplement from documented gather-bokeh technique; pmndrs DepthOfFieldEffect Zlib alt

topPicks.name: Maxime Heckel — 'The Art of Dithering and Retro Shading for the Web'
topPicks.why: The single best 2025 reference for the dither aesthetic and the skill-doc decision source: walks white-noise vs ordered-Bayer vs blue-noise tradeoffs, gives the 4x4 Bayer GLSL + texture-based blue-noise, and explicitly notes error-diffusion/Floyd-Steinberg is NOT fragment-shader-friendly (sequential) — which justifies shipping ordered+blue-noise and SKIPPING true error diffusion. Fold the decision tree into shaders.skill.md alongside the new dither snippet. Inspiration-grade (no OSS license on the code), and it also surfaces the sibling-source license flags (LYGIA dither = Prosperity, paper-design = PolyForm — both inspiration-only, do not vendor).
topPicks.license: Blog/tutorial — illustrative code, no explicit OSS license; treat as technique reference

topPicks.name: Codrops 'Creating an ASCII Shader Using OGL' (render-to-target -> fullscreen ASCII pass)
topPicks.why: The 'how to wire it' companion to the ASCII edge-detect technique: the canonical two-pass pattern (render scene to an OGL RenderTarget, sample it in a second fullscreen ASCII frag), depending only on libs the vault ALREADY uses — OGL (drag gallery) + Tweakpane (live-controls) — so it slots in with near-zero new surface behind a need('ogl') guard. Establishes reusable render-to-FBO -> post-pass plumbing the vault has never documented for shaders. But it uses PROCEDURAL bitwise glyphs (no font atlas) and NO edge detection, so it's the wiring half only — combine with the font-atlas + Sobel/DoG layer for the full effect. Hence inspiration/pattern-grade, below the vanilla edge-detect ship.
topPicks.license: OGL MIT; Codrops tutorial code permissive/educational; Tweakpane MIT (Lygia permissive-attribution)

topPicks.name: nikdelvin/liquid-glass (pure CSS+SVG components)
topPicks.why: A clean MIT cross-check that the liquid-glass technique generalizes beyond a single card to text and buttons (LiquidGlass container / LiquidText / LiquidButton, feDisplacementMap + feGaussianBlur + feColorMatrix, no WebGL, documented Safari fallback). Reference-grade: pull the SVG filter + CSS structure, ignore the Astro/Tailwind/Anime.js site wrapper; same Chromium backdrop-filter:url() caveat applies. Lower priority than the kube.io technique (the better pedagogical source) — use it to validate the vault's own snippet, not as the primary build.

topPicks.name: Painterly / post-processing-as-a-medium NPR skill reference (Maxime Heckel series)
topPicks.why: The decision-tree distillate for stylized screen-space NPR: when to use edge-preserving smoothing (Kuwahara) vs luminance-threshold hatching vs ordered dithering vs Sobel ink outlines, plus the shared structure-tensor/multi-pass plumbing. Worth folding into a short 'npr-stylize' note in shaders.skill.md/art-direction.skill.md so the engine routes a brief (hand-drawn-ink vs oil-painterly vs newsprint-zine) to the right new shader and reuses one shared luminance/sobel base. Inspiration-grade technique reference, not a dep — captures the brief's core hatch-vs-Kuwahara routing question.
topPicks.license: Editorial blog content (no code license) — decision/technique reference, reimplement clean

topPicks.name: Anisotropic Kuwahara painterly pipeline (Kyprianidis structure-tensor method)
topPicks.why: The top-tier painterly look (structure-tensor -> orientation/anisotropy -> elliptical kernel rotated to local feature direction = directional 'brush flowing along edges' rather than smeared). Maxime Heckel's R3F deep-dive walks the exact GLSL. But it is genuinely MULTI-PASS (3 render targets) so it does NOT fit the single-pass vanilla-snippet budget — ship as a documented upgrade path / skill note ABOVE the single-pass classic + generalized tiers, not the first ship. Academic method (Kyprianidis et al., GPU Pro) is freely implementable; Heckel publishes code. Inspiration/future-tier.
topPicks.license: Academic method freely implementable; author publishes code

topPicks.name: Noise-threshold dissolve-to-particles (disperse half) — Codrops 2025
topPicks.why: The matched DISPERSE to the particle-text assemble snippet: a uProgress uniform sweeps a Perlin-noise threshold across the surface/text, discarding fragments below it while a uEdge band glows and emits particles at the dissolving boundary — the 'text/logo dissolves into particles' exit. Verified not duplicative: vault 'morph' is geometry/clip-path/cube only and text effects are DOM-character reveal/scramble — no noise-dissolve-to-particles. Fold in as the disperse MODE of the particle-text snippet rather than a separate dep, tied to scroll progress. Reduced-motion: instant opacity crossfade, no emitted particles. Codrops article unlicensed — reimplement the short noise-threshold+edge-emit shader.
topPicks.license: Codrops demo, no explicit license — reimplement (short original shader), not a copied dep

topPicks.name: gpu-io
topPicks.why: The heavy-duty ESCALATION path for complex multi-buffer sims (full Stam fluid, agent/Physarum systems) where hand-rolling the vanilla PingPongRunner gets unwieldy — MIT, abstracts the ping-pong FBO bookkeeping (GPULayer state, GPUProgram passes, auto WebGL2/WebGL1 fallback, float16 handling), ships a UMD build (window.GPUIO) on jsDelivr fitting the vault's cdn-snippet + need()-guard pattern. Official examples cover the whole missing class (reaction-diffusion, fluid, Conway, Physarum). Ranks LOW despite MIT because the author announced 'switching gears' in Oct-2022 with no tagged releases since — stable-but-dormant. Recommend the vanilla PingPongRunner as default and gpu-io only as the 'when vanilla isn't enough' escalation.
topPicks.license: MIT (clean; not GPL/AGPL) — but flagged stable-but-dormant maintenance

topPicks.name: mattdesl/glsl-lut — 2D-strip lookup-table color transform
topPicks.why: The lightweight WebGL1-safe FALLBACK branch inside the LUT-grade snippet: transform(vec4 color, sampler2D lookup) samples a 512x512 PNG strip LUT (classic Instagram approach), MIT, ~few lines, trivially inlined — needs no 3D textures so it works everywhere WebGL1. Inferior to the trilinear 3D path (effectively bilinear within 2D tile slices, so it bands on smooth gradients), so it's the cheap/compat degradation rung, not the primary. Use as the WebGL1 fallback inside the same lut-grade snippet alongside gkjohnson's trilinear default.

topPicks.name: crt-fx (stefanlegg) — MIT WebGL CRT effect set + presets
topPicks.why: The FEATURE+PRESET reference to round out the public-domain Lottes crt.glsl.js: MIT, zero-dep, covering scanlines + 10 phosphor masks + chromatic aberration + bloom + barrel + noise/flicker/vignette/color-bleed plus 10 named presets (trinitron/arcade/pvm/vhs/terminal/amberTerminal/etc) whose parameterizations map cleanly to uniform presets and give good genre vocabulary. But it is multi-pass framebuffer + image-only input and lightly maintained, so LIFT the preset parameter tables and mask taxonomy into the vanilla snippet rather than vendoring the pipeline class. Inspiration/parameter-source, below the Lottes ship it enriches.

categories.trends: 2026's scroll-motion story is the maturing of native CSS scroll-driven primitives moving real work off the main thread. The stable, build-on-it core is animation-timeline: scroll()/view() + animation-range (Baseline-ish, Chrome 115+) — the vault already uses it for reveals/parallax/progress, but never for the named-timeline (view-timeline-name + timeline-scope), exit/contain ranges, or @property-state-machine patterns that the wider community now uses for scroll-spy, sticky stacking cards, cover flow, and horizontal pins with zero JS. The genuinely new 2025-2026 layer is four Chromium-first primitives, all still gated behind @supports: scroll-state() container queries (stuck/snapped/scrollable) that delete whole classes of IntersectionObserver/scroll-listener code; the CSS Carousel pseudo-elements (::scroll-marker / ::scroll-button / :target-current) for markup-free accessible dots+arrows; animation-trigger/timeline-trigger (Chrome 145+) as the declarative replacement for fire-once IntersectionObserver reveals; and Scroll Snap Events (scrollsnapchange). On the page-transition side, cross-document (MPA) View Transitions hit cross-browser interop in Q1 2026 (Chrome 126+, Safari 18.2+), and the directional types mechanism — startViewTransition({types}) / @view-transition{types} + :active-view-transition-type() driven by the Navigation API — plus view-transition-name: match-element (Baseline Oct 2025) are now the canonical way to do intent-aware forward/back motion and N-item list reorders without hand-naming. The throughline for a verification-moat product: native scroll-driven animations only stay 60fps if they animate transform/opacity/filter (Chrome's own trace emits compositeFailed + unsupportedProperties as ground truth), and VT silently skips when two elements share a name at capture (InvalidStateError while the DOM still mutates) — both are deterministic, Chrome-native save-gate signals nothing in the vault currently catches. Net: native-first snippets behind @supports with JS fallbacks (the vault's house style), plus headless-Chrome gates, are exactly where this category is heading; the only non-native dep worth keeping is Scrollama for the Firefox/Safari scrollytelling fallback, and the only spring-physics need (linear() token + WAAPI FLIP) is now reimplementable vanilla, retiring GSAP-Flip routing.

topPicks.name: VT-name save-gate diagnostic (duplicate / unset / silent-skip detector)
topPicks.why: Highest moat-fit in the set. Pair with the vault's dataviz/charts for the graphic (Scrollama does steps, not layout). License-clean and CDN-safe.

categories.trends: 2025-2026 kinetic-type is splitting into four currents, and the vault is under-covered on three of them. (1) Zero-JS scroll-driven CSS is the headline shift: animation-timeline: scroll()/view() now ships in Chrome 115+/FF/Safari 18, so scroll-scrubbed variable-font axis morphs run on the compositor with a clean @supports static fallback — the vault has ZERO animation-timeline usage (every scroll effect is a JS listener), making this the single highest-leverage net-new primitive. (2) Variable fonts are moving past wght/wdth/slnt into purposeful axis use: opsz coupled to size (optical sizing), GRAD for reflow-free numeric weight-surge (no advance-width change = no tabular jitter), and @property-registered axis customs so each axis animates from an independent trigger — with the honest caveat that font-variation-settings is NOT composited (Chromium #1411864 open), so these win on zero-JS + reduced-motion cleanliness, not GPU offload. (3) Color fonts (COLRv1 + font-palette/@font-palette-values) finally give resolution-independent gradient/3D display type as plain selectable text — a total vault gap — gated behind @supports font-tech(color-COLRv1) because Safari still silently mono-falls-back (the exact 'looks fine but lost all color' trap that suits the vault's screenshot-is-truth moat). (4) Native typesetting CSS matured: text-box-trim/text-box-edge (Chrome 133+) kills the cap-height half-leading 'amateur tell', and text-wrap: balance/pretty have documented silent-fallback caps (6 lines Chromium / 10 Firefox) that pages apply blindly. Across all four, the biggest moat fit is VERIFICATION: the vault scores type-scale ratio and rhythm but never measures rendered lines, so a Range.getClientRects() line census (widows/measure/runaway + balance-cap + pretty-orphan detection) plus a COLRv1-painted pixel-hue gate and a leading-gap detector are net-new diagnostics that catch the #2 AI tell (bad line-setting) invisibly to today's gate. Library-wise the safe, CDN-clean picks are troika-three-text (MIT, vanilla SDF 3D text — fills the gap drei's React-only Text leaves), opentype.js (MIT, glyph→SVG path engine that finally extends the vault's stroke-dasharray draw moat from icons to any font), and NumberFlow (MIT custom element). Intl.Segmenter is the correctness base-primitive every existing splitter should delegate to (all currently mojibake emoji/CJK). License climate is clean throughout: every font is OFL, every lib MIT, the rest native CSS — no GPL/SaaS traps. Drop/demote: @formatjs/intl-segmenter polyfill (contradicts zero-dep ethos), GlitchedWriter (non-seeded, superseded), and treat Capsize + Utopia Core as build-time metric/math sources, not runtime deps.

topPicks.name: CSS scroll-driven font-variation-settings axis morph (animation-timeline: scroll()/view())
topPicks.why: Highest-leverage net-new: confirmed the vault has ZERO animation-timeline/scroll()/view() anywhere — every scroll effect (scroll-scrub.js, scroll-animations.js, scroll-fx-pack) is a JS listener. This is pure-CSS, compositor-interpolated scroll-scrubbed axis morphing with a built-in @supports static fallback that doubles as the reduced-motion variant, so it clears the save-gate by construction. Load-bearing primitive for the type-poster genre AND a general zero-JS scroll capability beyond it. House-style perfect (degrades gracefully, accessible).

topPicks.why: Confirmed real archetype gap: structure.skill.md ships exactly 5 archetypes (SaaS, Agency, Local/restaurant, E-commerce, Local service) and none is type-as-the-product; Agency only mentions a 'huge type statement' but is portfolio-goal-driven. Every primitive already exists (marquee, footers-pro giant-wordmark, scroll-text-zoom, heading-pack oversized-hero, big-stretch, variable-fonts) but nothing bundles them into one composable, verification-passing archetype. Ships a reduced-motion static variant (morph→fixed instance, marquee freezes) so the oversized hero can't overflow and type counts toward the imagery floor. Highest assembly leverage in the set.
topPicks.license: n/a (assembled from MIT/permissive vault parts + OFL/Apache fonts)

topPicks.name: Typography coherence gate — headless line-level type-setting diagnostic (Range.getClientRects line census)
topPicks.why: Net-new diagnostic that fits the vault's verification moat exactly. Confirmed the gate scores type-scale ratio/rhythm/hierarchy but NEVER measures how text actually wraps. One Range.getClientRects() incremental-expansion census yields widows/orphans, measure (45-90ch / WCAG ≤80ch), and runaway lines — catching the #2 AI tell (bad line-setting) that is invisible to every current check. Pure standard Web API, reimplementable in the existing headless page.evaluate(). The anchor for a whole new 'typeset' sub-score.
topPicks.license: Standard Web API algorithm (reimplement; reference post only)

topPicks.name: COLRv1 color-font auto-recolor (@font-palette-values + override-colors)
topPicks.why: Confirmed total vault gap: search returns only mesh/gradient and mask-fill image-gradient — zero color-font/CPAL coverage. A single ~30KB OFL WOFF2 (Nabla/Bungee Spice) re-themes to ANY pal-* by string-generating a @font-palette-values rule. Crucial pre-solved trap: CSS custom properties silently no-op inside @font-palette-values, so the rule MUST be string-built from the active palette — exactly the kind of gotcha a snippet should bake in. Guard with @supports font-tech(color-COLRv1) + a mono fallback for Safari. Native CSS, CDN-loadable via Google Fonts css2.
topPicks.license: Fonts SIL OFL 1.1 (Nabla, Bungee Spice); technique native CSS (permissive)

topPicks.name: Container-relative fluid type scale (cqi + modular-scale clamp)
topPicks.why: Confirmed fluid-type.css is vw-only and container-queries.css has no cqi-driven scale: a card heading in a 360px sidebar and a 900px column get identical vw sizes today, which is component-incorrect for a drop-in snippet library. cqi + modular ratio sizes each instance to its OWN container. Ships @supports(font-size:1cqi) with a vw fallback so it never collapses text in the save-gate. Purely additive (parallel .ct-* classes), pairs with existing container-queries.css. High design correctness, zero risk.

topPicks.name: SVG textPath running-type engine (curved headline / circular badge / wavy sine ticker)
topPicks.why: Confirmed the vault places NO type on a path — it has SVG draw/morph + getTotalLength scroll-draw and flat marquee, but textPath is an explicit scope gap. One primitive yields three high-impact presets (arched editorial headline, rotating circular seal, sine ticker). Gotchas are well-scoped and pre-solved: startOffset is a presentation attribute (CSS scroll-driven animation can't target it → use rAF), set both href+xlink:href, dodge the WebKit startOffset jitter, use path getTotalLength() not getComputedTextLength() for the loop modulo, avoid the 'side' attr (reverse path instead). Native SVG, degrades to static curved text.

topPicks.name: Pure-CSS odometer / rolling-digit reel (@property <integer> + tabular-nums + per-digit translateY)
topPicks.why: Confirmed all four numeric snippets (count-up.js, gsap/counter.js, components Stats, micro.countUp) only tween textContent (numbers blur/jump) and text-flipping-board is whole-glyph split-flap snap — none give a continuous mechanical mileage roll where only changed digits carry. Distinct 'data updated' feel for hero stats / tickers / dashboards. Ship two coordinated variants (pure-CSS @property and a JS stacked-digit translateY column) for broad support. tabular-nums keeps columns from jittering.

topPicks.name: GraphemeSplit — Intl.Segmenter grapheme-cluster text splitter (a11y-safe primitive)
topPicks.why: Confirmed every vanilla splitter (SplitReveal, TextReveal, text-wave/ripple/encrypted) uses text.split('')/text[i], which mojibake-breaks emoji ZWJ/flags/CJK and has no copy-paste guarantee; grep for Intl.Segmenter returns zero. This is the correctness base-primitive they should all delegate to: grapheme-cluster spans + aria-label-on-host/aria-hidden-on-spans + contiguous real text nodes (copy/paste intact), with a [...str]/split('') fallback. Foundational — it makes the whole kinetic-type family correct, not just prettier.
topPicks.license: Intl.Segmenter is a platform API (no license); snippet original (MIT-style)

topPicks.name: opentype.js (runtime glyph→SVG path effect engine)
topPicks.why: Confirmed net-new as a RUNTIME use: print-typography.skill.md names opentype.js only as a print/font-FILE tool (subset/instance), and svg-animations.js getTotalLength+stroke-dasharray only works on hand-authored paths. Wrapping opentype.parse → getPath → toPathData behind a need()-guard converts the vault's path-draw moat from icons-only to ANY uploaded/Google font: per-letter stroke draw-on, outline-to-fill, fill-sweep, liquid-fill. MIT, CDN-safe, degrades to plain rendered text on failure. Single dependency that unlocks a whole effect class on the EXISTING pipeline.

topPicks.name: Seedable multi-variant text-decode (mulberry32-driven decode.js)
topPicks.why: Confirmed all four vault decoders (text-scramble.js, text-reveal.scramble, encrypted-text.css skins, buttons-text-fx) call Math.random() directly — none reproduce the same frame sequence twice, which conflicts with the engine's documented reproducible-compose seed semantics (in memory). A ~5-line mulberry32 PRNG makes the decode deterministic from a seed and adds sweep/cascade variants the vault lacks, so the screenshot/verify gate is stable for any decode-bearing hero. Public-domain PRNG, zero-dep. Directly serves the moat.

topPicks.name: Optical (size-aware) letter-spacing — tracking that auto-tightens as type grows
topPicks.why: Confirmed missing coupling: variable-fonts.css animates wght/wdth/slnt on hover but never controls opsz or couples tracking to size, and tracking-* classes are fixed manual steps. One zero-dep rule (letter-spacing: clamp(-0.05em, calc((1em - 1rem) / -10), 0em)) tightens display type while body stays at 0 — em-vs-rem trick, no JS, no per-class tokens. Pair with font-optical-sizing:auto for true display-vs-text treatment. Strongly on-scope ('size-aware tracking', 'opsz coupling'); a natural single fluid-display snippet with the cqi scale.

topPicks.name: text-box-trim / text-box-edge cap-height trim utility (+ @supports fallback)
topPicks.why: Confirmed gap: vertical-rhythm.css controls line spacing, never the half-leading slack above caps. text-box: trim-both cap alphabetic is the single biggest amateur-vs-pro tell in big-type layout — flush headings, truly optically-centered button labels, baseline-aligned price+label. Bundle the @supports(text-box-trim) progressive-enhancement fallback (font-relative margin-block approximation canceled inside the support block) so Firefox/old-Safari degrade gracefully instead of breaking the save-gate. Two candidates merged into one safe snippet. Chrome 133+ native.

topPicks.name: font-palette-paints / COLRv1-painted verification (pixel-hue-count gate)
topPicks.why: Net-new verification fitting screenshot-is-truth precisely. Safari (still true May-2026) parses font-palette CSS but renders COLRv1 as a single flat CPAL color, so a page looks fine yet lost all color/gradient intent. Gate: render heading, sample pixels in glyph bounds, assert >1 distinct hue / measurable variance; flat → font-palette silently fell back, warn/fail. Mirrors the documented WebGL draw-verify lesson (pixel-sample to prove it painted). Makes the COLRv1 pick safe to ship. Diagnostic, no license.

topPicks.name: Untrimmed-leading-gap detector (composed-hero verification gate)
topPicks.why: Net-new diagnostic, not just a snippet — the moat capability the brief flagged: coherence scoring can't currently see optical misalignment. Measures the rendered gap between an element's content-box top and actual cap-height glyph top (getBoundingClientRect vs a cap-height probe) and warns when a flush-intended hero has untrimmed half-leading AND no text-box-trim; since the verifier runs Chrome 133+ it can also confirm trim took effect. Cap-height baseline can reuse Capsize metrics rather than per-glyph probing. Pairs with the trim utility.

topPicks.name: GRAD-axis weight-surge on value change (reflow-free numeric flux)
topPicks.why: Net-new axis + net-new trigger. Confirmed the vault's variable-font work (variable-fonts.css transitions, variable-font-cursor) is cursor/hover driven, never value-change driven, and never uses GRAD. GRAD increases stroke density WITHOUT changing glyph advance, so a tabular metric surges bolder on update and settles with zero layout reflow and zero column jitter (wght would shift neighbors). Pairs with the odometer + NumberFlow for a tactile 'data just updated' accent. Needs a GRAD font (Roboto Flex/Recursive, OFL); gate on font load, fall back to wght or color flash.

topPicks.name: OpenType feature animation snippet (font-feature-settings / font-variant reveals)
topPicks.why: Confirmed non-duplicative: no vault file references font-feature-settings, font-variant-ligatures/alternates/numeric, swsh, ssXX, calt, dlig, or frac — the vf files animate AXES only. An entirely distinct expressive dimension: swash-on-hover, ligature reveal, ss01-ss20 cycling, fractions. Key design insight baked in: features are an INSTANT glyph swap (not tweenable), so soften with a companion clip-path/fade/axis-tween so the swash 'blooms'. Prefer standardized font-variant-* first, fall back to font-feature-settings for ssXX/swsh. Zero-dep, reduced-motion guard.
topPicks.license: CSS spec technique — public; demo fonts OFL

topPicks.name: text-wrap:balance silent-cap detector (Chromium 6-line / Firefox 10-line)
topPicks.why: Targeted lab check folding into the typeset diagnostic. heading-pack.css and fluid-type.css both ship .text-balance, so composed pages routinely apply balance to multi-line leads where the browser silently reverts past the 6/10-line cap with no warning — false polish. Detection = computed text-wrap === 'balance' AND getClientRects line count > cap; also emit the inverse (heading/lead with text-wrap:normal → 'add balance'). Free per viewport in the existing headless render and catches the mobile-only break. Standard CSS behavior, no dep.

topPicks.name: @property-registered axis customs (--wght/--wdth/--opsz composed via font-variation-settings)
topPicks.why: Confirmed the vault has NO @property axis registration (only @property for conic angles/progress rings). Registering each axis as @property '<number>' lets you keyframe/transition each axis from an INDEPENDENT trigger and sidesteps the shorthand-reset gotcha (setting one axis wiping others). MUST ship with the honest caveat (engine confusion the brief named): registered customs are non-composited (Chromium #1411864 open) and font-variation-settings repaints/relayouts per frame — sell as zero-JS + reduced-motion-clean + auto-pause-offscreen, NOT GPU-cheap. Pair will-change + contain:layout. Cleaner default than raw-shorthand keyframes.

topPicks.name: CSS Motion Path per-character text-on-path (offset-path/offset-distance)
topPicks.why: Distinct CSS-first complement to the textPath engine: per-char spans with offset-path: path('...') and offset-distance from char index keep text REAL selectable/copyable DOM with individually hover/animatable glyphs (textPath text is selectable but not reflowable HTML, char-level coloring is awkward). Crucial differentiator: offset-distance IS animatable by CSS scroll-driven animations (unlike SVG startOffset), so this is the scroll-scrub-friendly variant. Gotcha documented: only path() is broadly supported (no shape()/ray() in 2025 Safari), so re-layout on resize. Complements, doesn't duplicate, the textPath snippet.
topPicks.license: Native CSS Motion Path + vanilla JS

topPicks.name: troika-three-text (vanilla three.js CDN SDF text)
topPicks.why: Confirmed gap: 3d/floating-text.js is extruded TextGeometry and shaders/sdf-text.glsl.js samples a pre-baked atlas whose builder is 'not included' — neither is a real, typeable, layout-correct SDF text object, and drei's Text/Text3D (named in 3d-web.skill.md) is React-only. troika parses .ttf/.otf/.woff directly, bakes an SDF atlas in a worker (kerning/ligatures/RTL/unicode), renders crisp at any zoom, one quad per glyph (far cheaper than extruded mesh). UMD build loads straight from jsDelivr behind need()-guard; only peer dep is three.js (already loaded). Exposes glyphGeometryDetail + createDerivedMaterial for the SDF-warp pairing.

topPicks.name: Fluid optical sizing — opsz axis bound to viewport via @property + clamp()/calc()
topPicks.why: Confirmed the one axis the vault completely ignores: variable-fonts.css only lists opsz in a comment and never binds/animates it; fluid-type.css scales size but not the optical axis. Registering --opsz as @property and driving it from rendered size (or a fluid curve) gives genuinely designed-for-this-size headlines — sturdier small, refined-high-contrast large — no JS, no media-query soup. This is the axis that most separates 'pro typography' from 'AI-default sans'. Low risk, degrades to a static value on non-opsz fonts. Best with Roboto Flex/Fraunces/Source Serif 4 (OFL). Note it's an intentional art-directed override beyond font-optical-sizing:auto.

topPicks.name: text-wrap:pretty orphan-window audit (last-4-lines, <1/3-width short-line)
topPicks.why: The correctness spec for the orphan branch of the typeset diagnostic so the gate's auto-fix is browser-accurate, not naive: Chromium's pretty is a limited Knuth-Plass pass on the LAST 4 LINES, acting only when the last line is ≤1/3 width. The gate should use that same '<1/3 width OR single word' violation definition AND distinguish the two fixes — balance for ≤6-line headings, pretty for long prose (flag prose>6/10 lines that mis-use balance). Makes the diagnostic's recommendations right. Standard CSS behavior, no dep. Folds into the line-census gate.

topPicks.name: Non-composited animation gate (DevTools 'not composited' detection in verify)
topPicks.why: Directly resolves the brief's own confusion: the gate can't currently tell composited (transform/opacity) from main-thread-repaint animations, so it can't warn when a 'cheap' headline effect (font-variation-settings, registered customs, box-shadow/filter on large areas) actually janks on a long page. Chrome's Animations panel marks non-composited animations and the reasons are queryable via the CDP Animation domain / trace events — already the gate's runtime. Surface as a coherence/perf WARNING (not a hard fail). Makes the variable-font snippets shippable with honest guidance instead of the compositor myth.

topPicks.name: NumberFlow (vanilla <number-flow> custom element)
topPicks.why: Premium 'value flux' upgrade behind a need()-guard: a true framework-free custom element (esm.sh) on WAAPI + Intl.NumberFormat (zero deps) doing per-digit spin/fade with real currency/percent/compact formatting, localization, a trend prop, respectMotionPreference (reduced-motion default-on), and a continuous plugin. Distinct from the vault's count-up (no per-digit motion) and split-flap. MIT, 6.3k stars, used by X/Dub. Pairs with the vanilla odometer (vanilla = always-on baseline, NumberFlow = premium formatted/localized layer); renders the literal number as no-JS fallback.

topPicks.name: color-font palette-binding + animation skill (palette interpolation vs variable-axis)
topPicks.why: Net-new decision content; print-typography.skill.md covers variable text but not COLR/CPAL. Encodes the often-confused split: (1) font-palette interpolation animates 'by computed value' so modern Chromium/FF cross-fade between two @font-palette-values but older engines flip discretely; (2) variable-axis animation (Nabla EDPT/EHLT, Rocher bevel) animates the gradient/3D look itself and is reliable cross-engine. Skill binds palette indices to pal-* tokens, chooses cross-fade vs axis-morph, and sets the discrete-flip fallback expectation. Routes the COLRv1 snippets correctly. Documentation/native CSS.

topPicks.name: text-on-path.skill.md (textPath vs offset-path vs canvas decision tree)
topPicks.why: The router the vault's two new text-on-path snippets need — fits the vault's decision-tree-skills + verification strength. Chooses textPath for badges/arched headlines/sine-ticker (best glyph spacing along true arc length), offset-path per-char when text must be selectable/individually-animated/scroll-scrubbed, canvas measureText only for thousands of glyphs. Encodes the verification-relevant rules (rAF not SMIL, getTotalLength modulo, both href+xlink:href, reverse path instead of 'side', freeze offset under reduced-motion, accessible label) so generated output dodges the Safari pitfalls that would otherwise fail coherence/overflow gates.

topPicks.name: Kinetic-text a11y decision tree (split-or-not, aria-label vs duplication)
topPicks.why: No existing skill covers kinetic-type a11y (print-typography is print, not motion), and the vault's many vanilla splitters currently skip the aria contract, shipping the 'T-I-T-L-E' screen-reader bug. Codifies: only split semantic hosts (h1-h6/p/button) where aria-label is announced; default aria-label-on-host + aria-hidden-on-spans; duplication fallback (visually-hidden real text + aria-hidden spans) where aria-label is unreliable; gate behind prefers-reduced-motion. SplitReveal already does aria-label+aria-hidden, so the skill blesses that and extends it to the vanilla snippets. Can be a section on a kinetic skill rather than a whole file.

topPicks.name: Fluid type & optical-tracking skill (vw vs cqi, ratio choice, opsz/tracking coupling)
topPicks.why: Ties the new cqi scale + optical-tracking snippets into one decision tree the engine can consult, sitting alongside print-typography/design-system skills which mention type scale but give no vw-vs-cqi guidance and no opsz/size-aware-tracking rule. Chooses vw clamp for page rhythm vs cqi for reusable components, picks a modular ratio by genre, turns on optical sizing + auto-tracking only on display/hero tiers (never body), and always wraps cqi in @supports with a vw fallback so the save-gate never sees collapsed text. Additive-only (directs use without altering fluid-type.css).
