topPicks.name: Exotic-axis OFL variable-font catalog (Fraunces SOFT/WONK, Recursive CASL/MONO/CRSV, Roboto Flex GRAD/YOPQ/YTAS, Sixtyfour BLED/SCAN)
topPicks.why: Net-new as a curated axis-tag+range+morph table (not the font names — Recursive/Roboto Flex/Fraunces are already referenced, but their exotic axes are never catalogued or animated; CASL appears only as a placeholder with no font behind it). Each entry gives the exact font-variation-settings tag + safe range + suggested kinetic morph + CDN path, turning the vault's existing axis-animation machinery loose on genuinely expressive axes with zero license risk. The 'palette-like curated list' the angle asks for. Powers the OpenType-feature and scroll-axis-morph snippets with fonts that actually fire.

topPicks.name: Glyph-set decode banks (katakana / binary / hex / symbols / braille)
topPicks.why: Confirmed encrypted-text.css has aesthetic skins (cyber/acid/matrix) but no alternate glyph alphabets — every JS decoder uses one Latin/symbol pool. Named swappable chars presets (katakana/binary/hex/box-drawing/braille) add cyberpunk/terminal/data-stream variety cheaply and pair with the seedable decode for reproducibility. Motion's ScrambleText ships these presets but is Motion+ paywalled/React-only (inspiration, reimplement). Glyph data, zero-dep.

topPicks.name: SDF glyph-warp shader (melt / ripple / bulge — Codrops 'Gommage' technique)
topPicks.why: Confirmed net-new: vault has pointer-displace/distortion/gl-transition warps and DOM text-wave/ripple, but nothing warps glyph SDF text, and sdf-text.glsl.js only has a trivial whole-texture sine. The hard part this adds is smoothstep-correct edge AA preserved THROUGH the warp (distance-field, not raster) — threshold MSDF vs animated noise for melt + vertex-displace the subdivided glyph quad for ripple/bulge. Inspiration only (Codrops demo is WebGPU/TSL) but the math is plain GLSL — reimplement as a glsl.js companion to troika or extend sdf-text.glsl.js. Atlas via MIT msdfgen/msdf-bmfont-xml when pre-baked.

topPicks.name: Sixtyfour (CRT variable font with BLED + SCAN axes)
topPicks.why: Confirmed the vault has CRT/scanline EFFECTS (text-flicker crt, loaders scanline, hologram overlay) but NO CRT font and nothing that animates per-glyph raster bleed — those composite lines OVER text; here the glyphs themselves bleed and gain scanlines. Animating SCAN (scanline size) + BLED (phosphor bleed) via font-variation-settings gives a real CRT-warmup / signal-degradation effect impossible to fake with overlays. Distinctive retro-display option behind a CDN link + need()-guard; degrades to monospace fallback. OFL.

topPicks.name: Glitch-block reveal (clip-path / mask mosaic decode)
topPicks.why: A structurally different decode flavor: text resolves through animated rectangular blocks/mosaic tiles rather than per-character glyph cycling. Confirmed distinct from effects/glitch.css + glitch-burst.css (RGB-split/chromatic jitter, not block reveal) and from transitions-pro page-level mosaic (full-screen overlay, not in-place text). Seedable via the mulberry32 decode for which blocks fire when. Adds the 'data-decrypt over a grid' aesthetic of 2025 cyberpunk UIs. Zero-dep reimplement.

topPicks.name: Editorial type-poster palette + taste preset ('big-statement / brutalist-editorial')
topPicks.why: Lower leverage than the genre/technique because the building blocks (Fraunces import, editorial aesthetic, font-pairing) already exist — but it's the named bundle + sparse 'ink+paper+1-accent' palette that makes the type-poster genre cohere at seed 0 instead of looking like a pile of effects, the same role TastePresets plays for other genres. Net-new as a preset+palette PAIR (existing presets don't target near-monochrome type-poster). Reuse blessed durations; one accent to clear anti-slop coherence checks.

categories.trends: Three buckets define 3D-on-the-web right now, all confirmed against the live vault. (1) The "stop shipping flat default-metal" correctness push: IBL via RoomEnvironment/PMREM + ACESFilmic/AgX tone mapping + scene.environment is the single highest-leverage realism fix, and r164+ MeshPhysicalMaterial now does true transmission/dispersion glass natively with no custom shader — both are pure-vanilla, zero-dep, and the vault's scene-runner currently sets NONE of toneMapping/outputColorSpace/environment, so this is the biggest cheap win available. (2) The WebGPU/TSL migration (production across Chrome 113+, Firefox 141+, Safari 26 as of late 2025): WebGPURenderer auto-falls-back to WebGL2, TSL render-materials compile to both backends, but compute() is WebGPU-only and does NOT degrade — Codrops/award studios ship these now. The vault has WebGPU/TSL AUTHORING docs but zero runnable snippet and, critically, zero WebGPU VERIFICATION. (3) Gaussian splats (.spz/.ply/.sog) overtook meshes as the 2025-26 photoreal-capture format; Spark (World Labs, MIT) is the renderer, and SplatMesh is a THREE.Object3D so it drops into any scene. Cross-cutting: the ecosystem's good parts (OrbitControls, GLTFLoader+Draco/KTX2, RoomEnvironment, postprocessing) are now ESM-addons-only — the old examples/js script-tag globals were removed — so every new snippet must ride the importmap pattern the vault's bloom snippet already documents, not the UMD window.THREE convention. License hygiene is the recurring trap: avoid cabbibo/glsl-curl-noise (no license) and LYGIA (Prosperity) for flow fields — reimplement curl from stegu/webgl-noise (MIT) or TSL mx_noise; Needle progressive-LOD and Spline/Potree pipelines are SaaS/preprocessing-bound (inspiration, not deps); Theatre studio is AGPL. The deepest moat is verification: today's render-smoke variance gate is fooled by lost contexts (stale buffer reads as a false green), silent shader-compile failures (clear-color passes vacuously), frozen rAF loops, and is fully blind to WebGPU contexts — isContextLost()/getError(), COMPILE/LINK_STATUS probes, and a navigator.gpu/device.lost/error-scope probe are native-API, zero-dep gate upgrades that close real holes.

topPicks.name: Photoreal render config: ACESFilmic/AgX tone mapping + outputColorSpace + environmentIntensity/Rotation (scene-runner defaults)
topPicks.why: Verified absent in scene-runner.js and every 3d/ snippet — no toneMapping, no outputColorSpace, no environmentIntensity anywhere. ~4 renderer lines baked into the shared bootstrap lifts ALL 10 existing scenes at once from washed-out/flat to cinematic contrast. Zero dependency, pure core three, fully save-gate compatible. Highest design-impact-per-line in the whole batch and the completing half of the IBL fix.

topPicks.name: Procedural IBL via RoomEnvironment + PMREMGenerator (scene.environment helper)
topPicks.why: The single highest-leverage fix for the documented #1 AI tell (flat default metal). ~12 lines, zero external asset, deterministic and screenshot-stable, threads through scene-runner so particles-galaxy/cube-morph/instanced-grid/raycast-hover all gain roughness-aware reflections + soft fill. Verified net-new for vanilla: vault's only IBL is React drei <Environment> and model-viewer's baked attribute, neither of which touches raw THREE.Scene materials. Prerequisite for the glass snippet. RoomEnvironment is a three/addons import = same importmap need()-guard the bloom snippet already documents.

topPicks.name: glass-material.js — vanilla MeshPhysicalMaterial transmission glass (native, zero-dep)
topPicks.why: The signature 'expensive' product-hero look (perfume bottles, glass logos, liquid orbs) with ZERO new dependency. As of r164 (before the vault's pin) MeshPhysicalMaterial natively supports transmission/thickness/ior/attenuation/dispersion — three auto-allocates the transmission render target. Verified non-dup: vault 'glass' is all 2D CSS backdrop-filter; 3d/ scenes use only MeshStandard/MeshBasic; skill only name-drops drei's React MeshTransmissionMaterial. A GlassMaterial.preset() factory drops into scenes-pack's material slot. Ranks just below IBL because it REQUIRES the env-map helper to read as glass instead of grey — ship them as a pair.

topPicks.name: camera-controls.js — vanilla orbit + critically-damped lerp + fitToBounds
topPicks.why: Grep-verified net-new: NO orbit/damping/fit logic in 3d/; scene-runner is static-camera only; model-viewer hides orbit inside Google's bundled component. ~120 lines of pure pointer+vector math, zero dep, no CDN, slots straight onto ctx.camera/ctx.renderer.domElement and serves postprocessing-bloom, scenes-pack, wave-plane, raycast-hover. fitToBounds via bounding-SPHERE (radius/sin(fov/2)) is rotation-invariant for orbit — the literal 'cleanest vanilla equivalent' of drei OrbitControls+Bounds the category was missing. Highest reuse surface of any single helper.

topPicks.name: Spark (@sparkjsdev/spark) — splat-viewer.js cdn-snippet
topPicks.why: Closes the vault's total absence of Gaussian-splat support — the dominant 2025-26 photoreal-capture format (.spz/.ply/.sog auto-detected). World Labs, MIT, actively maintained. SplatMesh extends THREE.Object3D so it composes with ctx.scene.add(), OrbitControls and every 3d/ helper unchanged. Mirrors model-viewer's exact need()-guard/poster/reduced-motion pattern. One honest friction encoded in the snippet+skill: Spark is ESM/importmap-only (not window.THREE), and capture splats need an upright flip option — both documentable, not blockers.

topPicks.name: gltf-load.js — vanilla GLTFLoader + Draco/Meshopt/KTX2 CDN-decoder pipeline (onProgress + poster)
topPicks.why: Production glTF is almost always Draco/Meshopt/KTX2-compressed; without decoders those assets fail to load entirely. Verified net-new: model-viewer bundles its own uncontrollable loader and ALL existing Draco/Meshopt guidance is R3F-only — there is no way today to load a real compressed .glb into the vault's own SceneRunner/scenes-pack. WASM decode runs in a worker pool = no main-thread freeze. Must encode two caveats: addons are ESM-only under three/addons (dynamic-import, not window.THREE), and decoder CDN path MUST be pinned to the exact same three@x.y.z (version drift is the #1 reported failure).

topPicks.name: WebGL context-lost + getError preflight probe (before the pixel-variance gate)
topPicks.why: Pure moat. Grep-confirmed NO isContextLost/getError usage in tools/ today. render-smoke forces preserveDrawingBuffer and reads RGB variance, but a context lost AFTER one good frame (GPU crash, VRAM exhaustion, the ~16-context/tab limit) leaves a STALE non-blank buffer that passes variance as a false green. isContextLost()/getError() at read time is the ONLY signal that closes the 'canvas present but context-lost' hole. Native API, zero dep, injected via addInitScript. Distinct from the existing variance check — do not re-add that.

topPicks.name: WebGPU render verification (adapter / device.lost / error-scope probe)
topPicks.why: The single biggest correctness gap as the vault follows its own documented 2026 WebGPU/TSL direction: full WebGPU AUTHORING exists (3d-web/shaders skills, vendored webgpu-threejs-tsl) but ZERO WebGPU verification — the harness only instruments webgl/webgl2/experimental-webgl and is blind to 'webgpu' contexts. A scene whose requestAdapter() returns null or whose device is lost would slip the gate. Probe navigator.gpu + non-null adapter + device.lost + pushErrorScope('validation'). Native API. Headless note: when WebGPU is genuinely unavailable, treat a graceful WebGL fallback as pass, not force-fail.

topPicks.name: GPUComputationRenderer curl-noise flow-field particles (WebGL FBO ping-pong)
topPicks.why: The dramatic net-new hero on the MAXIMALLY-compatible path: 100k-1M GPU-simulated points on the WebGL build the vault already pins (~98% device support), vs particles-galaxy's static CPU point cloud rotated as a rigid body with no simulation. GPUComputationRenderer is bundled in three (examples/jsm, MIT). Critical license discipline already verified in shaders.skill.md: reimplement curl clean-room from stegu/webgl-noise (MIT, approved) finite-difference gradients — NOT cabbibo/glsl-curl-noise (flagged no-license) or LYGIA (Prosperity). Degrades by capping count if FloatType targets are unsupported.

topPicks.name: scene-runner-webgpu.js — WebGPURenderer + TSL bootstrap with auto WebGL2 fallback
topPicks.why: Every current 3d/ snippet hardcodes the legacy WebGLRenderer (verified in scene-runner.js). This API-compatible sibling boots WebGPURenderer via the three/webgpu ESM entry, keeps the same SceneRunner.create contract so all callbacks port unchanged, and the value is the vault-shaped wrapper: async init(), setAnimationLoop, an explicit forceWebGL fallback branch and dispose. WebGPURenderer already falls back internally so the snippet stays small. It's the prerequisite that unlocks the TSL node-material and GPU-compute snippets. Ranks below GPUComputationRenderer (the safe default) because it's the heavier importmap path.

topPicks.name: rapier3d-compat (dimforge) — vanilla three-physics.js rigid-body helper
topPicks.why: Verified net-new: vault's ONLY physics is 2D DOM gravity.js + spring.js + force-directed network; every 3d/ snippet is purely visual; Rapier appears ONLY as a React @react-three/rapier recommendation. A tiny ThreePhysics helper (RAPIER.World + RigidBodyDesc/ColliderDesc auto-derived from geometry, world.step(), copy translation()/rotation() onto mesh) unlocks falling/stacking/colliding meshes and drag-to-throw — things the vault literally cannot do. Self-contained base64-inlined WASM (no separate .wasm fetch). Apache-2.0. Ranked lower than the visual/correctness picks because the bundle is ~0.8 MB gzip — async need()-guard, opt-in only, never a default include. Load via esm.sh/jsdelivr (avoid unpkg's CJS build).

topPicks.name: point-cloud-viewer.js — vanilla PLY/PCD scan viewer with color-by-height + Eye-Dome Lighting
topPicks.why: Directly serves Design Book's architecture-diagram/archviz/survey composing, none of which the vault can do: verified ZERO point-cloud DATA viewer, ZERO PLY/PCD load, ZERO EDL. PLYLoader/PCDLoader are official three addons (CDN, zero extra dep); EDL is the screen-space depth-edge pass (CloudCompare/Potree standard) that makes an unlit structureless gray scan read as crisp 3D geometry — the one feature distinguishing a DATA cloud from particles-galaxy's decorative confetti. Reuses the postprocessing-bloom EffectComposer pattern for the EDL pass. The bundled EDL technique generalizes to wireframes/voxel/dense particle scenes.
topPicks.license: MIT (three.js + PLY/PCD addons; EDL is public/reimplementable)

topPicks.name: pmndrs/postprocessing — vanilla effects-composer.js (DoF/CA/vignette/grain/SSAO/SMAA)
topPicks.why: The cinematic post-FX stack beyond the vault's bloom-only snippet and its CSS-filter fake — the look separating pro 3D from default-flat three. pmndrs merges multiple effects into ONE fullscreen pass (vs three addons' framebuffer-per-pass) so a 5-effect stack stays cheap. Zlib (permissive). ESM-only — rides the exact importmap header postprocessing-bloom.js already documents, pin three peer version, degrade to direct renderer.render() if unavailable. Verified net-new: no WebGL multi-pass composer in the vault beyond single-effect bloom.

topPicks.name: scroll-camera-rig.js — vanilla scroll-choreographed camera + reveal timeline
topPicks.why: Verified gap: vault's 3d-motion.skill.md routes EVERY scroll-camera request to React-only tools (drei ScrollControls, theatre-js) — there is no vanilla path. CatmullRomCurve3.getPointAt/getTangentAt + Vector3.lerp are all core three (MIT), so a damped scrollProgress drives camera.position along a waypoint spline with look-ahead aim, plus a keyframe table cross-fades object reveals. The canonical Apple-style scrollytelling product reveal as a vanilla rig. Reuses the existing --scroll-progress body-var convention. Distinct from instanced-grid scrollDriven (single wave scalar) and webgl-drag-gallery (Z image strip, no spline).

topPicks.name: Scene3D — vanilla declarative scene-graph mini-DSL (object-tree → THREE.Scene)
topPicks.why: The literal vanilla R3F/drei analogue the category is missing: the entire 3d/ dir is hand-coded imperative new THREE.X(); scene.add() — verified NO data→scene path. A ~150-line builder walks a plain JS/JSON tree via a geometry/material type registry and emits a real THREE.Scene that scene-runner mounts, turning 3D into composable, emittable, diffable, seedable data the same deterministic way Design Book composes pages. Verified NOT a dup of three's ObjectLoader/toJSON (UUID cross-refs + flat matrices, explicitly not for hand-authoring) while staying round-trippable to it. Keeps the need(window.THREE) guard; verify via the WebGL screenshot draw-check.

topPicks.name: WebGL shader compile/link instrumentation (silent-failure catcher)
topPicks.why: Moat upgrade pairing with the context-lost probe in the same addInitScript-wrapped getContext. A shader that fails to compile draws only the clear color — and if that clear is a colored/gradient clear (common in shader snippets) the per-channel variance check passes vacuously. Wrapping compileShader/linkProgram to read COMPILE_STATUS/LINK_STATUS + InfoLog gives a deterministic specific reason ('FRAGMENT_SHADER: ...') instead of an ambiguous low-variance number. Spector.js (stale, 2021) is inspiration for the wrap-getContext pattern only; vendor the ~30-line vanilla probe, not the lib.
topPicks.license: MIT (Spector.js reference only; probe is native API)

topPicks.name: TSL node-material snippet (e.g. tsl-animated-material.js)
topPicks.why: The JS-authored, zero-GLSL shader path the vault lacks as a runnable snippet (shaders/ is all hand-written GLSL string pairs). Drives material.colorNode/positionNode with TSL nodes (color/time/oscSine/uv/mix/positionLocal) and degrades automatically because TSL compiles to BOTH WGSL and GLSL. Sits on scene-runner-webgpu. The 2025 idiom Codrops/award studios use. Caveat to encode: keep to widely-supported nodes so it renders identically on the WebGL2 fallback; verify via forceWebGL.

topPicks.name: RGBELoader + PMREMGenerator.fromEquirectangular (.hdr IBL) with CC0 PolyHaven fallback
topPicks.why: The 'real photo' lighting tier above the assetless RoomEnvironment default, for outdoor/sunset/studio mood. Encodes a constantly-hit, non-obvious gotcha worth a snippet: roughness is BROKEN on a raw equirect .hdr envMap — it only works AFTER PMREM conversion. Verified gap: blender.skill.md lists PolyHaven/ambientCG CC0 HDRIs but only for the drei React path — no vanilla .hdr→PMREM loader. Degrades to RoomEnvironment if the .hdr fails. Ranks below the procedural default because it requires an asset fetch (determinism/imagery-floor considerations).

topPicks.name: N8AO (N8python) — SSAO pass for the composer
topPicks.why: The single biggest cheap realism win after IBL — grounds objects with contact shadows the flat renderer never produces, and does SSAO noticeably better than pmndrs' built-in (screenSpaceRadius mode, half-res + denoise, HBAO quality). Verified net-new: vault only name-drops n8ao in prose, no snippet. CC0 (zero restrictions). Ships a real UMD/CDN build (unpkg n8ao dist) so it's script-tag friendly unlike pmndrs core. Slots into effects-composer.js as the 'AO' toggle via N8AOPostPass; skip the toggle if the global is absent.

topPicks.name: TSL GPU-compute particle snippet (compute() storage-buffer particles)
topPicks.why: The payoff the WebGPU runner unlocks: per-vertex spring/friction/noise physics entirely on the GPU — the thing WebGLRenderer fundamentally cannot do without CPU round-trips, shipped by Codrops Jul 2025 as a current technique. Must encode the hard caveat that makes it lower than the WebGL flow-field hero: compute has NO WebGL2 fallback (unlike TSL render materials), so the snippet MUST feature-detect navigator.gpu and swap to a reduced CPU/instanced fallback (e.g. fall back to particles-galaxy.js) to honor the degrade-gracefully law and pass the gate. Flag prominently in the header.

topPicks.name: drei-vanilla MeshTransmissionMaterial (N8 backside-buffer glass)
topPicks.why: The genuine ceiling beyond native MeshPhysicalMaterial: ~324 self-contained lines (onBeforeCompile + FBO sample) that can SEE other transmissive objects (stacked glass, liquid-in-bottle) plus author-controlled chromaticAberration/anisotropicBlur/distortion and a backside pass for thick solids. MIT. Best as the OPTIONAL upgrade tier behind a need()-guard in glass-material.js — when drei-vanilla is present use it, else fall back to the native path (pick #3). Ranks here not #3 because it's ESM and costs an extra render pass per material; default to a tiny 256/512 buffer for the verification budget. Confirm it composes under window.THREE before shipping.

topPicks.name: drei-vanilla (pmndrs) — premium-look helpers as Scene3D registry hooks
topPicks.why: Official pmndrs vanilla port of drei's expensive-look abstractions (MeshTransmissionMaterial, MeshReflectorMaterial, AccumulativeShadows, Caustics, Outlines, Grid, soft PCSS shadows) — standalone functions the Scene3D mini-DSL can expose as optional material/staging node types behind a need()-guard that degrades to plain MeshStandardMaterial. Active (v1.25.0, Sept 2025), MIT. Verified: skill names drei-vanilla in passing — promote to a real registry hook. Add only the high-value helpers; ranked below the specific MeshTransmissionMaterial pick since it depends on Scene3D landing first.

topPicks.name: @loaders.gl/las — LAS/LAZ + COPC LiDAR loader (real survey data)
topPicks.why: The bridge from demo point cloud to real survey deliverable: framework-independent LAS/LAZ parser returning flat typed arrays (position/intensity/classification/color) that drop straight into BufferGeometry, enabling color-by-intensity and color-by-classification (ground/building/vegetation) — the standard archviz/GIS modes. MIT. Complements (not dups) the PLY/PCD viewer; behind a need()-guard degrades to PLY/PCD. Caveat to document: handles up to LAS 1.3; pair with copc.js for LAS 1.4 / COPC streaming.

topPicks.name: rAF liveness / FPS-floor sanity (frozen-animation detector)
topPicks.why: render-smoke samples 4 frames only to find the best pixel signal — it never checks the scene is still ANIMATING. A snippet that draws one good frame then throws inside (or never re-schedules) its rAF loop reads as a perfect pass on the static frame but is frozen for the user. A monotonic rAF-tick count over ~1s is the cheap liveness signal for self-declared-animated families (shaders/, 3d/). Native API. Ranked lowest of the verification picks: needs an animated-vs-intentionally-static classifier (reuse the existing INTERACTIVE_CANVAS exemption list) and is best added after the context-lost/compile/WebGPU probes. Launch with --disable-background-timer-throttling for stable headless counts.

topPicks.name: gpgpu-particles.skill.md — GPU-simulated particle decision tree
topPicks.why: Consolidates knowledge currently split across three skills and turns the dangling 'GPGPU flow fields: see 3d-motion.skill.md' pointer (no runnable target) into an actionable path. Routes WebGL GPUComputationRenderer (max compat, pinned build) vs WebGPU TSL compute (cleaner, needs fallback), captures the FBO ping-pong mental model + FloatType caveats + curl-from-gradient math, and crucially centralizes the license guardrails the vault states scattered (avoid glsl-curl-noise/LYGIA, use webgl-noise/mx_noise). Net-new value over the existing scattered mentions is the WebGL-vs-WebGPU routing + concrete TSL APIs tied to the two runnable snippets above.

topPicks.name: 3d-web.skill.md 'don't-ship-flat-PBR' decision branch (IBL-first staging rule + verification)
topPicks.why: Codifies the authenticity law for 3D into a verifiable rule: never light a PBR scene with bare DirectionalLight+AmbientLight — set scene.environment first (RoomEnvironment default; .hdr+PMREM for real-sky mood), then 1-2 accent lights. The defensible part is the optional verification form: a coherence check that flags any three.js scene rendering MeshStandardMaterial with metalness>0 but no scene.environment — the literal flat-metal AI tell. Extends the existing skill, turning the two IBL snippets into an enforceable contract.

topPicks.name: webxr-scene — vanilla WebXR immersive-ar/vr compose-genre + helper
topPicks.why: Rounds out the immersive lane without duplicating model-viewer (which only delegates one-tap AR to the OS and can't author a custom in-session experience). Uses three's own ESM webxr addons (ARButton/VRButton) with strict feature-detection and a graceful 2D fallback to the existing orbit/model-viewer scene. The canonical hit-test reticle-placement pattern. Verification reality encoded: navigator.xr is absent in headless Chrome, so the gate verifies the 2D-fallback path only — the genre's value is that the fallback IS the verified default and XR is progressive enhancement. iOS/macOS Safari + Firefox have no WebXR, making the mandatory fallback correct by design.

topPicks.name: TSL compute particles (WebGPU) — instancedArray + compute() + Fn
topPicks.why: The modern WebGPU particle path the vault names as 'the 2026 direction' but never operationalizes (verified: no instancedArray/compute/Fn/WebGPURenderer string anywhere — only pointers to external skills). instancedArray creates a persistent GPU storage buffer (no per-frame upload), a compute Fn updates per instanceIndex, the same buffer feeds the render node — no FBO texture-packing. Hits 1M+ points. Largely OVERLAPS the TSL GPU-compute snippet above and the GPUComputationRenderer hero is the safe default, so this ships as the sibling 'webgpu' variant with forceWebGL fallback, not a separate headline. Use mx_noise/clean-room curl, not LYGIA.

topPicks.name: Rapier transform-sync tick pattern (translation()/rotation() → mesh.position/.quaternion)
topPicks.why: Documents HOW the vanilla physics helper works rather than being a separate dep: after world.step(), copy each body's translation() onto mesh.position and rotation() onto mesh.quaternion, with the load-bearing gotcha that Rapier quaternions are plain {x,y,z,w} (use mesh.quaternion.set(...), not .copy of a THREE.Quaternion) and a fixed-timestep accumulator to avoid framerate-coupled sim. Belongs as a short section in 3d-web.skill.md alongside the existing React @react-three/rapier note, covering the vanilla path. Generalizes to any future solver.

topPicks.name: prefers-reduced-motion 'jump-to-end' fallback for scroll-driven 3D (verification + contract)
topPicks.why: The vault's signature anti-slop differentiator, net-new for the 3D-scroll case: under reduce, snap the camera to each waypoint as its section enters view (discrete IntersectionObserver step, no spline scrub), reveal objects at full opacity, stop the look-ahead aim — preserving the narrative while removing vestibular-triggering continuous motion. Every public scroll-camera tutorial omits this. As verification it extends the headless gate: assert the reduced-motion run renders the final/each composed frame (not a blank mid-spline frame) and no rAF camera tween runs. Bundle with scroll-camera-rig so the rig ships accessible by default.

topPicks.name: scrollytelling-3d-reveal compose-genre (pinned-canvas product-reveal archetype)
topPicks.why: The default 'expensive agency landing page' shape, currently uncomposable end-to-end in vanilla — the vault can pin sticky text/image panels but cannot choreograph a real 3D camera behind them. A position:sticky full-viewport canvas (the scroll-camera-rig scene) behind a tall scroll spacer whose sections reveal product states in lockstep. Composes from existing parts: structure.css spacer rhythm + scroll-camera-rig + SceneRunner + scroll-text-reveal, with the live 3D render satisfying the imagery floor. Depends on the scroll-camera-rig pick shipping first.

topPicks.name: WebXR DOM Overlay (dom-overlay feature for in-AR 2D HTML UI)
topPicks.why: The bridge that lets the vault's huge existing CSS/UI inventory (buttons, panels, command-palette, sheets) live inside a handheld AR session — request with requiredFeatures dom-overlay + domOverlay:{root}, with a beforexrselect handler so button taps don't leak as world hit-tests. Best as a sub-option of the webxr-scene helper (a session config flag, not a library), not standalone. Handheld-AR only (Android Chrome); headsets ignore it so VR UI must also exist as in-scene WebGL. Degrades cleanly to normal DOM outside a session.
topPicks.license: W3C spec / browser-native (pairs with MIT three.js)

topPicks.name: data-driven-3d-scene (compose-genre archetype)
topPicks.why: Closes the loop the question really aims at: once Scene3D exists, Design Book can COMPOSE a 3D scene from a data spec the same way it composes pages/decks/diagrams — a deterministic generator emitting the {geometry,material,position,children} tree from palette + taste-preset inputs, handed to Scene3D.build(), mounted via scene-runner, passing the draw-verify gate. Wires palette→material colors and taste-preset→lighting defaults so 3D inherits compose_page's seed semantics. Depends on Scene3D landing first; needs an imagery-floor exemption note since a live canvas isn't a static image.

topPicks.name: Eye-Dome Lighting (EDL) post-process pass (standalone)
topPicks.why: The EDL algorithm packaged as a reusable EffectComposer pass alongside the existing UnrealBloom usage — one extra fullscreen pass comparing log-depth to neighbors. THE standard for unlit point clouds (CloudCompare/Potree/ParaView/ArcGIS) precisely because clouds have no normals, and it generalizes to sharpen depth on ANY structureless geometry (wireframes, voxel fields, dense particle scenes, even instanced-grid). The canonical three PR (#14016, MIT) was never merged so there is no official three EDL — making a clean vanilla version genuinely net-new. Mostly subsumed by the point-cloud-viewer pick which already ships EDL, so this is the 'extract the pass' follow-on.

topPicks.name: three.js XR controller + XRHandModelFactory input layer
topPicks.why: The hand/controller half of the immersive lane the WHY explicitly calls for: renderer.xr.getController for ray/grip + select/squeeze events, XRControllerModelFactory meshes, XRHandModelFactory for the 25-joint hand skeleton (Quest/Galaxy XR/Vision Pro). Net-new vs the vault's only spatial inputs (device-tilt gyroscope, MediaPipe webcam hands) which are 2D-screen effects, not in-headset 6DoF. Makes the immersive-vr branch actually interactive (grab/point/place). Ships as the controller/hand option of webxr-scene, not standalone; not headless-verifiable, so progressive enhancement on the verified 2D fallback. Value 3 — narrowest audience, last in the XR sub-cluster.

topPicks.name: Spectral-flux onset detection -> --beat pulse (extend sound-react.js)
topPicks.why: Highest impact-per-line in the category. Verified: sound-react.js exposes only amplitude (--snd-level/bass/treble), which lags/smears and never HITS on transients; no beat/onset/BPM anywhere in the vault. A ~30-40 line vanilla addition to the EXISTING AnalyserNode loop fires on the downbeat. Reuses the family's exact 'sensor drives CSS vars' contract (--beat decays 1->0), freezes under reduced-motion like sound-react does. Reimplement-from-algorithm (MIT reference) keeps it zero-dep. Load-bearing for every audio-reactive composition.

topPicks.name: Stylus-reactive ink source (PointerEvent pressure + tilt + twist, vanilla)
topPicks.why: Opens an entirely new reactive axis the vault cannot express. Verified absent: cursor-paint.js / signature-pad.js / whiteboard-pack.js are all pointer-position quadratic with zero pressure/tilt/twist. altitudeAngle/azimuthAngle went Baseline Dec 2024 so tilt is finally shippable in 2026. Zero-dep, web-standard, writes --ink-pressure/--ink-tilt-x/--ink-azimuth so styling stays in CSS and the input layer stays swappable — textbook reactive-input family shape. Normalizes the two mutually-exclusive orientation sets and degrades to constant-width on mouse/touch.
topPicks.license: Web standard (W3C Pointer Events L3) — no dependency

topPicks.name: Global rAF scroll-velocity signal source (--scroll-vel / --scroll-dir on :root)
topPicks.why: A singleton signal source that unlocks a whole class of speed-reactive effects (skew/blur/RGB-split/marquee-speed) via plain CSS. Verified: the exact velocity math is currently DUPLICATED and trapped inside marquee.js (dy/dt*16, *0.95 decay) and webgl-drag-gallery.js; no reusable scroll-velocity util exists. One shared rAF + one passive listener, de-dupes two call sites, gates on reduced-motion (freeze at 0). Pure web-standard, in-house reimplementation (MIT reference). High leverage, low surface area.

topPicks.name: presence-source.js (vanilla attention/idle/presence source)
topPicks.why: The conspicuously-missing LIGHTEST rung of the reactive ladder (pointer->gyro->mic->webcam): needs NO permission, works everywhere, so it's the safest live source in the vault. Verified absent: all 'idle/presence' hits are static UI (presence dots, button idle-states), nothing reads Page Visibility or an inactivity timer. Also fixes a real correctness gap — nothing in the vault pauses RAF/audio loops when the tab is backgrounded (battery/CPU waste). Writes --presence/--idle/--away + data-presence, degrades, freezes under reduced-motion. Fold in the Page-Lifecycle freeze/resume/pageshow guidance (separate candidate) so resume is correct across bfcache.
topPicks.license: Web standard (Page Visibility L2 + setTimeout); no dependency

topPicks.name: face-react.js — MediaPipe FaceLandmarker expression/gaze/head-pose CSS vars
topPicks.why: Unlocks an entire expression/gaze/head-pose axis at ZERO new dependency cost — same @mediapipe/tasks-vision Apache-2.0 package, same lazy dynamic-import + .enableCamera() consent gate + pointer fallback already vetted in the skill. Verified net-new: vision-react.js instantiates HandLandmarker only and emits --vr-x/y; it never touches FaceLandmarker, blendshapes, or transform matrices. Writes a normalized family (--face-gaze-x/y, --face-yaw/pitch/roll, --face-smile/brow/blink). The permissive, already-blessed path to gaze (vs the LGPL WebGazer trap below).
topPicks.license: Apache-2.0 (MediaPipe Tasks Vision; dep already vetted in reactive-input.skill.md)

topPicks.name: voice-command.js — keyword-grammar voice source (transcript -> intent -> CustomEvent)
topPicks.why: The missing speech-to-INTENT counterpart. Verified: voice-input.js + voice-transcribe.js do speech-to-TEXT (dictation) only; sound-react.js is amplitude only — nothing maps keywords->intent/events. Zero-dep wrapper over SpeechRecognition with a tiny literal/wildcard/optional grammar + Levenshtein tolerance, fires namespaced CustomEvents and sets --voice-intent, mirroring the family's CSS-var contract. Mandatory feature-detect + visible text-command/hotkey fallback dispatching the SAME events, gesture-gated mic. Pairs with the existing ai/voice-input.css mic UI. Should layer on the on-device processLocally/phrases capability (separate candidate) for privacy+accuracy.

topPicks.name: ShakeDetect — DeviceMotion acceleration peak/jerk detector (vanilla snippet)
topPicks.why: Fills the one empty cell in the reactive-input source table: tilt (orientation) is covered by device-tilt.js, but acceleration/shake/jerk gestures (shake-to-shuffle/clear/re-roll) are not, and they're a staple mobile interaction. Verified absent (no accelerometer/devicemotion in the tree). Zero-dep, reuses device-tilt's exact iOS 13+ requestPermission gesture flow, writes a transient decaying --shake var. Critical platform caveat to encode: LinearAccelerationSensor (Generic Sensor) is NOT on iOS Safari, so accelerationIncludingGravity is the only cross-platform source. Degrades to a manual button on desktop.

topPicks.name: $1 Unistroke Recognizer (vanilla reimplementation)
topPicks.why: The missing shape/symbol gesture vocabulary — upgrades the existing 4-direction Swipe detector into a draw-to-command layer (draw a check to confirm, X to dismiss, circle to select). Verified absent: vault has Swipe/SwipeActions/SignaturePad/Whiteboard/long-press/pinch-zoom but no symbol classifier. ~100 core lines, trivially reimplementable as a zero-dep snippet (feed a pointer-path array, get {name,score}); ships 16 built-in symbols. New BSD is permissive/snippet-safe (keep copyright header). Pairs directly with the snippets that already capture pointer paths.
topPicks.license: New BSD (3-Clause) — permissive; keep copyright header

topPicks.name: Low-latency ink loop (getCoalescedEvents + getPredictedEvents + rAF)
topPicks.why: A drop-in upgrade that materially improves EVERY pen surface for free. Verified: signature-pad.js, cursor-paint.js, and whiteboard-pack.js all draw one quadratic segment inline in pointermove with no coalesced history, no prediction, no rAF decoupling. The technique (drain coalesced sub-frame history, draw committed path, extend with predicted points, erase/redraw predicted next frame) is the single thing separating 'toy paint' from 'real ink'. Zero-dep web standard, degrades to a length-1 event array. Should ship as the inking-loop backbone the stylus source + sketch genre sit on.
topPicks.license: Web standard (Pointer Events L3) — no dependency

topPicks.name: Two-finger affine transform gesture (rotate + scale + translate, DOMMatrix-based)
topPicks.why: The canonical mobile editor gesture and an exact verified gap: I read pinch-zoom.js — it computes distance + midpoint but DISCARDS the angle, so it pinches/pans but never rotates. Adding the atan2 angle term + DOMMatrix composition (around the initial midpoint) turns single-axis zoom into a true manipulation surface where an object stays stuck to both fingers. Zero-dep, reuses the existing pointers-Map + touch-action:none + setPointerCapture idioms. Emits onTransform({matrix,scale,rotation,tx,ty}); single-pointer falls back to drag.
topPicks.license: Educational technique — reimplement in-house (transform-gesture npm is MIT but stale since 2014); not a dependency

topPicks.name: On-object free-transform gizmo (8 resize handles + rotation handle, Figma/Photoshop-style)
topPicks.why: The desktop/mouse counterpart to the two-finger gesture and the core primitive for any spatial-editor compose genre. Verified net-new: image-crop.js has 8 AXIS-ALIGNED resize handles but no rotation and stays axis-locked; image-tools.css rotates only via slider/90deg buttons. A real gizmo unifies move+scale+rotate and resizes a rotated box along its LOCAL axes (the hard part naive croppers get wrong). Modernize senocular/TransformTool (MIT but 2013-era) with PointerEvents + DOMMatrix decompose; anti-slop: snap rotation to 15deg + live HUD readout (reuses cursor-hud.js).

topPicks.name: compass-heading.js (vanilla --heading sensor source)
topPicks.why: A genuinely absent reactive source: device-tilt.js gives relative tilt-x/y but NO absolute facing direction, and the skill's decision table has no 'which way am I facing' row. Zero-dep, mirrors device-tilt's API: iOS reads webkitCompassHeading after requestPermission on gesture; Android uses deviceorientationabsolute. Writes --heading (turns 0..1) + --heading-deg + --heading-accuracy. Surfaces accuracy and a figure-8 calibrate hint, degrades to a draggable manual dial. Base MUST be DeviceOrientation, not the Chromium-only Magnetometer/AbsoluteOrientationSensor.
topPicks.license: web standard (DeviceOrientation Event Spec) — no dependency

topPicks.name: reactive-input.skill.md decision-table row for acceleration/shake
topPicks.why: Low-effort, high-leverage skill update that makes the new shake source discoverable and codifies the source-selection decision (orientation gamma/beta for continuous tilt vs acceleration magnitude for discrete gestures) plus the load-bearing platform caveat (Generic Sensor LinearAccelerationSensor is NOT on iOS Safari -> steer composition to DeviceMotionEvent). The skill family IS the documented decision layer and has a gap exactly here. Pairs 1:1 with the ShakeDetect snippet.

topPicks.name: reactive-input.skill.md 'facing/heading' decision row + sundial preset
topPicks.why: Companion skill update that makes the --heading source a first-class sibling of tilt/sound/webcam and documents the cross-platform normalization + accuracy/calibration caveats so the engine wires it correctly. Includes a tasteful non-AR 'sundial' demo preset to exercise the var. Low effort, enforces the consent+degrade rules already governing the family. Pairs 1:1 with compass-heading.js.

topPicks.name: Sketch / signature / annotation compose genre
topPicks.why: The user-facing payoff that justifies the stylus source + ink loop: a stylus-aware drawing surface (e-sign / image-doc annotation / whiteboard notes) with obvious real product uses. Verified net-new: no signature/annotation/sketch genre exists, though it REUSES heavily-present pieces (cursor-tool.css pen/brush/eraser HUD, infinite-canvas.js + canvas-minimap.js for pan/zoom, watercolor for paper grain). Net-new is mostly genre recipe + the stylus glue. Degrades to finger/mouse drawing.
topPicks.license: n/a (composition of vault snippets + MIT perfect-freehand)

topPicks.name: getCoalescedEvents() high-fidelity stroke sampling
topPicks.why: The single technique that recovers the full sub-frame stylus sample history (120-240Hz) the browser merges away, turning faceted fast-stroke ink into smooth ink. Verified: no vault snippet uses getCoalescedEvents (all pointer effects use plain pointermove). Pure web API, trivially vanilla, degrades to the length-1 array. Slightly redundant with the low-latency ink loop (which subsumes it), so ship it AS PART OF that loop rather than standalone.
topPicks.license: Web standard (Pointer Events L3) — no dependency

topPicks.name: Vibration API named-pattern haptics helper (Haptics.fire('success'|'tap'|'warning'|'longpress'))
topPicks.why: Closes the input->feedback loop: the vault has rich gesture INPUTS (swipe/long-press/pull-to-refresh/sortable/pinch) but verified ZERO tactile OUTPUT (no navigator.vibrate usage; only an inspirational 'haptic' tag on long-press.js). It's the exact structural twin of the existing ui-sounds.js (fire-and-forget named effects, global mute, reduced-motion-aware). ~40 lines, feature-detects, pure no-op where unsupported. CAVEAT to bake in: iOS has NO Vibration API and the old <input switch>.click() hack was patched in iOS 26.5 — ship as Android-fires / iOS-noops, decorative never load-bearing.
topPicks.license: Web standard (W3C Vibration API) — helper is original MIT-style vanilla

topPicks.name: wayfinder / compass-rose directional UI archetype
topPicks.why: A spatial/wayfinding compose genre that's currently unserved, and mostly composition glue: the visual primitive (radial-nav.css with --r-count/--r-start trig positioning) ALREADY exists but is static/click-driven — verified. Net-new is binding ring rotation to live --heading (rotate(calc(var(--heading)*-1turn))) + computing relative bearing. Builds directly on the proposed compass-heading.js. Must honor reduced-motion (freeze ring, show numeric bearing) + desktop/manual fallback.
topPicks.license: vanilla-reimplementable (own CSS/JS; technique inspired by public CSS radial-menu galleries)

topPicks.name: On-device SpeechRecognition (Chrome 139+): processLocally + available()/install() + SpeechRecognitionPhrase biasing
topPicks.why: The 2026-current way to do voice commands well: processLocally=true (audio/transcripts never leave device), available()/install() language-pack gating, and crucially the modern replacement for the now-deprecated/no-op SpeechGrammarList — recognition.phrases contextual biasing to boost your keyword set. Verified net-new: existing voice snippets predate these. Best shipped as a capability the voice-command.js snippet feature-detects and layers on, always falling through to cloud/no-bias. Document in the skill's Privacy section.

topPicks.name: Elastic rubber-band overscroll container (iOS UIScrollView resistance curve + spring release)
topPicks.why: Verified gap: pull-to-refresh.js uses a flat resistance:0.5 + a hard CSS ease-out snap (top edge only) — that is NOT a rubber band (no nonlinear stiffness, no spring, no bottom edge). This ships the real iOS curve f=(x*d*c)/(d+c*x), c=0.55, both edges, spring release reusing animations/spring.js, with overscroll-behavior:contain to kill scroll-chaining. The 0.55 constant + bottom-edge + spring are the load-bearing additions. Distinct from bottom-sheet velocity-dismiss. Treat native Chrome 145+ elastic overscroll as baseline; only enable JS where absent or inside in-page containers.
topPicks.license: Public-domain math (UIScrollView curve); reimplement from scratch

topPicks.name: perfect-freehand (getStroke)
topPicks.why: The canonical pressure-sensitive variable-width stroke generator (tldraw's Steve Ruiz): pure data, MIT, ~5KB, zero-dep — feed [x,y,pressure], get a tapered outline polygon. Wraps cleanly behind a need()-guard and degrades to the constant-width quadratic-bezier path already in cursor-paint.js when pressure/lib is absent. Powers the variable-width half of the ink source so the vault doesn't hand-roll outline tessellation. MIT + pure-data = CDN-safe; could also be reimplemented to stay fully vanilla per the vault's zero-dep preference.

topPicks.name: $Q Super-Quick / $P Point-Cloud Recognizer (multistroke + scribble)
topPicks.why: The point-cloud cousin of $1 that unlocks the marquee scribble-to-delete UX plus multistroke symbols (X as two strokes, hash, plus) that $1's single-ordered-stroke model can't match. ~half the code is shared with $1, so it slots into the same vanilla snippet as an optional 'cloud' mode. New BSD permissive. Prefer $Q over $P for the mobile/pointer footprint. Ships alongside (not before) $1.
topPicks.license: New BSD (3-Clause) — permissive; keep copyright header

topPicks.name: Attention-aware presence behavior: look-away-to-dim / lean-in-to-focus
topPicks.why: Turns the raw face source into a finished, demoable attention-aware archetype: derive a single --attention scalar from yaw/pitch + face-detected + debounce, then CSS dims an unfocused page, sharpens on lean-in, and pauses autoplay on look-away. On-trend (mirrors shipping Windows 11 Adaptive Dimming / Dell presence detection, but that's NOT web-exposed, so a MediaPipe browser version is net-new). Distinct from vision-react's positional --vr-x/y (this is a stateful engagement signal). Depends on face-react.js; must be consent-gated, reduced-motion-off, with a pointer/visibility idle fallback.
topPicks.license: Pattern/technique (no dep beyond the Apache-2.0 MediaPipe source); OS implementations are precedent only

topPicks.name: prefers-color-scheme + light-dark() graceful-fallback auto-theme
topPicks.why: PARTIALLY DUPLICATIVE — flag and trim before shipping. Verified: the vault ALREADY ships theme-switch.js (prefers-color-scheme OS fallback + persistence), dark-mode.css (token system keyed off prefers-color-scheme), and 50 .pal-* palettes. So the system-color-scheme->dark/light contract exists. The genuinely net-new slice is narrow: the CSS light-dark() function + the continuous --bloom/--room-theme tokens that the AmbientLightSensor layer overrides. Ship ONLY that thin delta, layered onto the existing dark-mode.css tokens — not a new full auto-theme. Still the honest, no-permission ~99.6% degrade target that makes the lux sensor shippable.
topPicks.license: Web standard (CSS Color 5 / Media Queries) — no dependency

topPicks.name: desynchronized low-latency canvas hint
topPicks.why: One-line ink-latency win: getContext('2d',{desynchronized:true}) bypasses the compositor queue. Verified net-new: the three canvas pens all call getContext('2d') with no options. Feature-detect via getContextAttributes().desynchronized, fall back silently. Natural pairing with the coalesced/predicted loop. Encode caveats: possible tearing on front-buffer devices, attributes immutable after first getContext, translucent desync canvases can't have DOM stacked above. Best folded into the inking-loop snippet.

topPicks.name: Tiny 2D affine matrix utility (compose / invert / decompose / DOMMatrix bridge)
topPicks.why: The load-bearing plumbing both manipulation candidates silently need: decomposeTSR() to pull rotation/scale/translation back OUT of an accumulated matrix (so a gizmo resizes a rotated object along local axes) + screenToWorld/worldToScreen. Verified: no DOMMatrix/affine tooling exists in any .js — every spatial editor would otherwise reinvent ad-hoc trig (note infinite-canvas.js already has its own screenToWorld, so scope this to the gizmo/gesture, not canvas). DOMMatrix covers compose/invert natively; only decomposeTSR is custom (~40 lines) — prefer reimplementing vanilla over the MIT chrvadala dep to stay zero-dep. Value 3 alone (plumbing) but it makes the two #5 manipulation picks robust rather than trig-hacks.

topPicks.name: Page Lifecycle freeze/resume + bfcache pageshow (correct resume/restore)
topPicks.why: Robustness layer that makes presence/auto-pause actually correct: visibilitychange alone misses bfcache restores (pageshow + event.persisted) and OS-initiated freeze/resume, leaving paused animations stuck or double-firing loops — a subtle bug the vault's many RAF/scroll/audio effects would inherit. Verified: no freeze/resume/pageshow/bfcache handling anywhere. Best folded as guidance INTO presence-source.js + a skill rule ('resume on resume/pageshow, not just visibilitychange'), not a standalone file.
topPicks.license: Web standard (WICG Page Lifecycle); pageshow/persisted Baseline, freeze/resume Chromium

topPicks.name: Scroll-state container queries (@container scroll-state(scrollable/stuck/snapped))
topPicks.why: A cheap declarative complement to the velocity source: style descendants by their scroll container's STATE (can-still-scroll direction, sticky stuck, snap-child snapped) with zero JS/listeners. Verified net-new: vault does sticky/snap layout + JS headroom but nothing uses scroll-state(). Progressive-enhancement only (Chrome 133+, Safari/FF lagging) so MUST degrade to a static style. Distinct from --scroll-vel (state vs speed); lower value but reinforces the 'react to scroll' story.

topPicks.name: AmbientLightSensor lux auto-theme wrapper (illuminance -> --room-lux/--theme/--bloom)
topPicks.why: FLAG: NOT shippable as a primary signal in 2026 — ~0.39% global support, removed from ALL desktop engines (Chrome/Edge/Safari/Firefox) over privacy, 'disabled by default' on Chrome-for-Android, and the enable-generic-sensor-extra-classes flag was REMOVED so there's no dependable user flag. Add ONLY as a labelled progressive-enhancement layer whose headline behavior is the no-permission fallback (the prefers-color-scheme/light-dark() pick), writing the SAME --room-theme/--bloom tokens. Requires HTTPS + permission grant + permissions-policy; handle the sensor 'error' event by silently degrading. Genuinely orthogonal source (vault has zero ambient-light), but the value is the pairing, not the lux path.
topPicks.license: Web standard (W3C Ambient Light Sensor / Generic Sensor) — no dependency

categories.trends: Reactive input in 2026 is consolidating around a few real platform shifts the vault should ride. (1) Pointer Events L3 finally made high-fidelity inking shippable: stylus altitudeAngle/azimuthAngle went Baseline (Dec 2024), and the getCoalescedEvents + getPredictedEvents + rAF loop (optionally with desynchronized canvas, pointerrawupdate, and OS-delegated navigator.ink) is now the standard recipe separating 'toy paint' from real ink — the vault's three canvas pens all predate it. (2) Web Speech moved from speech-to-TEXT toward speech-to-INTENT: Chrome 139+ ships on-device recognition (processLocally) with SpeechRecognitionPhrase contextual biasing replacing the now-deprecated SpeechGrammarList, making private offline voice commands viable. (3) The $-family stroke recognizers ($1/$P/$Q, New-BSD) remain the canonical, tiny, reimplementable way to add a draw-to-command symbol vocabulary on top of captured pointer paths. (4) WebGL/canvas vision via MediaPipe Tasks (Apache-2.0) extends cleanly from hands to FaceLandmarker blendshapes/gaze/head-pose, giving a permissive route to attention-aware UX (paralleling shipping OS presence-dimming) and sidestepping the LGPL/GPL WebGazer license trap. (5) Scroll and overscroll are going declarative: scroll-driven animations, scroll-state() container queries, and native elastic overscroll (Chrome 145+, FF for years) mean JS should be the fallback, not the default — but a single shared-rAF scroll-velocity signal is still needed and is currently duplicated across snippets. Caveats to honor: the Generic Sensor API family is a minefield — AmbientLightSensor is effectively dead (~0.4%, removed from all desktop engines for privacy) and LinearAccelerationSensor/Magnetometer are Chromium-only, so DeviceMotionEvent/DeviceOrientationEvent remain the only cross-platform accelerometer/compass sources; Vibration API is Android-only (iOS has none, and the old switch-click haptic hack was patched in iOS 26.5). Across all of it the vault's reactive-input contract holds: pick the lightest source that carries the effect, write CSS custom properties so styling stays declarative and the input layer stays swappable, gesture-gate consent, always degrade, and freeze under prefers-reduced-motion.

The category's center of gravity in 2024-2026 is reproducible, seed-driven generative art (the fxhash/Art Blocks "same hash = same pixels" contract) plus a revival of stateful/iterative GPU work that the single-pass shader era skipped. Four currents stand out. (1) Determinism as a first-class concern: the simplex patent expired Jan 2022, jwagner's simplex-noise v4 deliberately externalized its PRNG, and bryc's public-domain sfc32/splitmix32/xmur3 became the de-facto seedable-RNG stack — so a real CPU noise()+seeded random() primitive is now table-stakes, and the vault has literally zero (only sin-wave fakes in vortex.js/wavy-background.js and GLSL-locked noise). (2) Stateful GPU is back: ping-pong FBO reaction-diffusion, SwissGL's <1000-LOC neural-CA family, and raw-WGSL compute particles are the fresh "alive texture" looks that stateless mesh-gradient/aurora/plasma cannot produce; the vault's fluid.glsl.js literally asks for the ping-pong runner it lacks. (3) Geometry-driven generative (Voronoi/Lloyd stippling, Delaunay, d3-contour marching-squares, aperiodic tiling) is having a moment off the back of the 2023 "hat" / 2024 "spectre" monotiles — and the vault has only a procedural GLSL voronoi pattern, no relaxable points/polygons, no contour, no tiling. (4) The genuine moat is verification: the research repeatedly lands on a render-twice "same seed = same pixels" self-consistency gate (no committed baselines, GPU-portable), a Chrome deterministic-flag profile, a viewport-invariance check, and a cheap aesthetic-fitness scorer that lets a reroll loop keep argmax instead of just proving "it drew." That verification layer — plus the headless-Chrome WebGPU-adapter-absent / WebGL-readback gotchas already flagged in MEMORY — is where this category compounds the vault's existing save-gate moat. Net: prioritize the missing vanilla primitives (seedable RNG + real noise), the determinism gate, and the highest-impact net-new families (reaction-diffusion ping-pong, Voronoi/Lloyd, aperiodic tiling, L-system/differential-growth), and keep heavy libs (gpu-io, SwissGL, @thi.ng/boids) as reimplement-from/inspiration rather than deps.

topPicks.name: Tiny seedable PRNG module (sfc32 + mulberry32 + xmur3) — vanilla prng.js
topPicks.why: THE missing determinism substrate, and everything else in the category sits on it. The generative-art skill explicitly says 'always seed the PRNG so a piece is reproducible from one number/hash' but ships NO primitive — verified: search returns only gradient-avatar.js's FNV hash, which derives one color set, not a reusable reproducible draw stream. Public-domain bryc functions (Math.imul/|0, byte-identical across V8/JSC/Node), ~30 LOC, zero-dep, directly vendorable with no license risk. Standardize on sfc32 (what fxhash uses for $fx.rand) with mulberry32 as the tiny single-int option; xmur3 turns a brand string/hex into the seed words. simplex-noise.js v4 REQUIRES exactly this as an external PRNG, so it is also a hard dependency of the noise pick. This collapses the five near-duplicate PRNG candidates into one canonical module.

topPicks.name: Reproducible-render verification gate — render-twice 'same seed = same pixels' self-consistency
topPicks.why: The category's real moat and a clean extension of the existing headless-Chrome save-gate (overflow -> coherence -> imagery-floor -> seed-determinism). Render a seeded composition twice in ONE harness run, hash the PNG/RGBA (node:crypto) or bound a pixelmatch diff, and FAIL if they differ — proving determinism with NO committed baselines and GPU/OS-portably (both passes share one machine), distinct from any baseline-regression diff. Also statically flags determinism-breakers (Math.random, Date.now/performance.now for generation, devicePixelRatio reads). Directly enforces that the prng.js snippet is actually used. Encodes the two gotchas that match MEMORY's WebGL draw-verify note: WebGL needs preserveDrawingBuffer/forced readback before hashing, and cross-machine identity is a CPU-2D-only guarantee. Own code over already-pinned pixelmatch — no new dep.
topPicks.license: MIT-compatible (own code; pixelmatch MIT + node:crypto). Highlight FAQ is unlicensed prior-art — pitfall checklist only, no copied code.

topPicks.name: simplex-noise.js (jwagner) as a vendored ~40-line vanilla noise core (createNoise2D/3D/4D)
topPicks.why: The vault has ZERO real CPU noise — confirmed only sin-wave 'simplex-ish' fakes (vortex.js, wavy-background.js) and curl noise locked inside GLSL. This is the canonical zero-dep fix: MIT, ~2KB, fastest pure-simplex, and as of v4 it takes an injected seeded PRNG, so it pairs exactly with the prng.js pick and becomes a noise.js the vault fully OWNS (no CDN, no need()-guard). 4D is a real edge for seamless looping animation via a time axis. Re-points every existing fake-noise effect at a real, seedable, reproducible source and underpins flow fields, drift, blob morphs, terrain, stippling. Prefer this owned vanilla core over a CDN dep; FastNoiseLite is the optional behind-a-guard breadth upgrade (Cellular/OpenSimplex2S), not the default.

topPicks.name: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion ping-pong shader + PingPong FBO helper (reaction-diffusion.glsl.js)
topPicks.why: Marquee net-new GPU family AND it backfills infrastructure the vault explicitly wants — verified all 18 shaders run the single-pass runner.js fullscreen quad, and fluid.glsl.js's own description ('single-pass approximation') is the note that a true ping-pong runner is missing. RD is the canonical stateful/iterative shader: organic coral/Turing-skin/labyrinth textures that mesh-gradient, aurora, plasma, voronoi and noise-flow structurally CANNOT make (those are stateless per-frame). Public-domain Gray-Scott math, clean-room reimplementation, ~200 LOC self-contained WebGL2. Guard behind WebGL2 + EXT_color_buffer_float / OES_texture_float with a static-image fallback so the headless save-gate still renders clean. Beats gpu-io (npm deps, staleness, bundle weight) for a single RD background.
topPicks.license: Reimplemented — Gray-Scott equation is public-domain math; no copied source (reference gists are no-license, not vendored)

topPicks.name: d3-delaunay + Lloyd-relaxed CVT stippling/mosaic/shatter technique (one snippet, mode flag)
topPicks.why: Highest-impact geometry pick: the vault has ZERO geometric Voronoi — confirmed only the procedural GLSL voronoi.glsl.js pattern, which produces no relaxable points or polygons. d3-delaunay (ISC, ~12KB, bundles Delaunator) emits SVG d-strings directly (no canvas needed for print) and supports in-place Lloyd relaxation, powering three in-scope layouts from one codebase: weighted CVT stippling (image->dots), Voronoi mosaic (avg-color cells), and cell-shatter. Seedable via the prng.js pick for determinism; the relaxation itself is a satisfying convergence animation. Ship the technique wrapper as the deliverable (turnkey effect, not raw lib) behind a need()-guard degrading to a grid/image. ISC is MIT-equivalent and CDN-safe. Folds in the redundant Delaunator and second d3-delaunay candidates (mention Delaunator only as the triangle-only lighter option in a comment).
topPicks.license: ISC (d3-delaunay + bundled Delaunator); technique wrapper is own MIT/ISC code

topPicks.name: Hat / Spectre aperiodic monotile generator (SMKGS substitution) — vanilla, seeded coloring
topPicks.why: The freshest decorative hook in the category (2023 'hat' / 2024 'spectre' Tile(1,1)) and a whole net-new family: the vault has ZERO tiling systems — confirmed only static CSS patterns.css/patterns-mega.css, nothing generative or provably non-repeating. A single shape that tiles the plane with no periodic repeat reads as 'we know our math.' Deterministic geometry (~250-LOC substitution over 4-5 generations) + a seeded PRNG driving per-tile fill from a vault palette, accent placement, hue rotation, stroke weight => one seed -> one reproducible non-tiling wallpaper. Permissive sources to port (hatviz BSD-3, christianp/aperiodic-monotile CC0). Verifies cleanly (static fill, no overflow). Subsumes the Truchet/Wang/Penrose candidates as the headline member of an 'aperiodic backgrounds' family.
topPicks.license: BSD-3-Clause (hatviz) / CC0 (christianp) — both permissive; reimplement from reference

topPicks.name: Headless aesthetic-fitness scorer (hue-entropy + sat/hue contrast + quadrant balance over canvas pixels)
topPicks.why: Closes the reroll loop and is the verification capability the angle most asks for: the existing render-smoke harness samples pixels only for variance + distinct-color COUNT (a binary did-it-draw), so it cannot say variant A beats variant B. A ~60-line scoreFrame(imageData)->{entropy,contrast,balance,score} from open aesthetics research (Redies et al.: Shannon entropy of HSV hue, color contrast = sqrt(2*sigma_hue*sigma_sat), luminance balance, quadrant variance) turns reroll-seed -> render -> score -> keep argmax into reality. Pairs with the seed-determinism gate and the param-manifest below to make the engine actually drive and rank generative knobs headlessly. Public-domain methods, vanilla, runs on the same getImageData readback the harness already uses. Net-new vs coherence_check (which scores HTML slop-tells, not generative aesthetics).
topPicks.license: Public-domain research methods (CC-BY paper); reimplement vanilla

topPicks.name: Differential growth (organic-line) — vanilla SVG snippet, seeded
topPicks.why: Marquee net-new generative family for editorial/poster compose genres: a node-and-edge growth loop that buckles a polyline into coral/brain-coral/meander/lichen forms (attract-to-neighbors, curvature-align, min-separation repel via spatial hash, edge-split node injection). Confirmed absent — the closest vault neighbors (lava-lamp metaballs, flow-field canvas, mesh gradients) are pixel/blur effects, not growing vector geometry. With the prng.js seed it is fully deterministic and exports <path>/<polyline> — exactly the print-quality stroke output the compose engine and svg.skill plotter path want; degrades to a static seed render under reduced-motion. ~150 LOC, trivially clean-room (the canonical Jason Webb repo is CC BY-NC-SA — inspiration ONLY, do NOT vendor; the algorithm itself is unencumbered).
topPicks.license: Algorithm public/unencumbered; reimplement clean-room. FLAG: Jason Webb reference repo is CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (non-commercial) — do not vendor.

topPicks.name: Canvas2D dithering pack — recursive Bayer + Floyd-Steinberg + Atkinson error-diffusion
topPicks.why: Net-new and genuinely distinct: confirmed the vault's only dithering is the SVG-feTurbulence fake in stepped-gradient.css (.dither-*) and the GLSL halftone.glsl.js — neither does real per-pixel error diffusion or palette quantization. This is true CPU error-diffusion + ordered Bayer on getImageData/putImageData, taking any source (image, gradient-to-canvas, procedural noise) down to a 1-4 bit palette for riso/lo-fi/e-ink looks. Public-domain algorithms (Bayer 1973, Floyd-Steinberg 1976, Atkinson), zero-dep, no WebGL, reduced-motion-safe and static-exportable. Pairs with the blue-noise void-and-cluster variant as the 'designerly grain' tier (ship that as a sibling, optionally as a precomputed CC0 texture).
topPicks.license: Public-domain algorithms; reference impls MIT — reimplement

topPicks.name: Deterministic L-system turtle (parametric, 2D + isometric, SVG-path output) — vanilla
topPicks.why: The vault has NO grammar/rewrite-system generative tool — confirmed its generative set is entirely noise/flow-field/particle/shader (continuous fields). An L-system produces crisp recursive botanical/branching VECTOR marks (ferns, vines, dragon curves, lightning, coral, dividers/corners) that look like nothing in the current library. ~60 LOC vanilla, seeded for reproducibility (uses the prng.js pick for optional stochastic rules). Two high-leverage upgrades over the MIT reference (rgmiranda/turtle, canvas-only deterministic): an isometric projection toggle (same axiom renders flat OR iso, pairing with effects/isometric-grid.css) and an SVG-path output mode feeding the svg.skill/AxiDraw plotter path crisp at any scale. Borrow lindenmayer's parametric+stochastic production model as inspiration without taking the dep.

topPicks.name: Named Gray-Scott parameter presets (f/k table -> designed-texture map + paired color ramps)
topPicks.why: The piece that converts the RD sim from a science demo into a usable design generator and directly serves the authenticity law (bespoke organic background, not a stock gradient). A data map of named (f,k,dA,dB) presets — coral, mitosis/self-replicating-spots, worms, fingerprints, labyrinth/maze, Turing-spots, marble-veins — each paired with a curated colorize ramp (bone/ivory marble, coral-pink, copper-patina, ink-on-paper). The f/k numbers are public mathematical constants; the color ramps are original. Ships as presets+palettes on the RD shader object, exactly mirroring how gradient-flow.glsl.js and mesh-gradient-wgl.glsl.js already expose named palettes. Value is fully contingent on the RD shader landing — it is the second half of that pick.
topPicks.license: f/k values are public-domain constants; color ramps original (source playground code is CC-BY-NC-SA — values only, not its code)

topPicks.name: Vanilla seedable boids field (spatial-hash O(n) neighbors + named behavior presets)
topPicks.why: Net-new emergent behavior: verified the vault has particles.js (O(n^2) connect-lines + mouse-repel, Math.random, no flocking/seed), vortex.js (curl-orbit, not flocking), and interactive-canvas field/network modes — but NO actual Reynolds flocking with tunable design presets, and none seed-deterministic. Classic separation/alignment/cohesion with a uniform spatial-hash grid scales to a few thousand 2D agents at 60fps in pure canvas, driven by the prng.js seed so one seed lays out the same flock. Named presets (murmuration/school/swarm/embers/calm-drift) are just weight bundles. Clean-room from the @thi.ng/boids behavior model (Apache-2.0, but ALPHA + headless + 7 internal deps -> reference, not dep); respect reduced-motion (cut count + freeze).
topPicks.license: Apache-2.0 reference model (@thi.ng/boids) — clean-room vanilla, no code copied

topPicks.name: Generative brand-identity compose-genre (seed in -> mark + pattern + palette out)
topPicks.why: The headline net-new compose-genre: one brand string/hex -> a coherent identity sheet (a parametric mark on an NxN grid under an aesthetic ruleset a la the MIT Media Lab 7x7 system, a matching seamless tiling pattern, and a palette), all reproducible from the seed. It composes the vault's existing determinism + palette infra (palette-generator/gradient-builder, gradient-avatar's hashing) into a real verifiable artifact the engine emits. Distinct from gradient-avatar (single identicon) and from static CSS pattern packs. The seed->params->render contract is the spec. Depends on the prng.js + verification picks to deliver its 'reproducible' promise; reference identity systems are case studies/inspiration, not deps.
topPicks.license: Concept/technique — reimplement, no dep (reference systems are case studies)

topPicks.name: d3-contour (marching-squares isolines/isobands -> SVG paths)
topPicks.why: Fills the contour/isoline leg the vault entirely lacks — confirmed data-viz is charts only, no marching-squares. Feed it a scalar field (the new CPU simplex/fBm noise, or an RD buffer) and it returns GeoJSON MultiPolygons mapping 1:1 to SVG <path> d-strings: instant topographic / reaction-front organic line art. Deterministic (no internal RNG; same field in -> same paths out), so it satisfies the reproducibility gate and renders clean to print SVG. ISC, pure path-string output (no DOM dep), behind a need()-guard. Strongly preferred over the AGPL MarchingSquares.js alternative (flag that as inspiration only). Pairs naturally with the noise and RD picks as its input field.

topPicks.name: SwissGL (google/swissgl) — Neural-CA / GPGPU 'alive texture' family
topPicks.why: Genuinely net-new self-organising/self-healing textures (grow-from-seed, damage-and-heal NeuralCA, ParticleLenia, Physarum) that nothing in the vault produces and that even the RD pick can't match (RD is PDE, this is emergent NCA). Minimalist <1000-LOC single-file WebGL2 wrapper by the NCA inventor, Apache-2.0 (license-clean), with 8-bit-quantized weights baked into GLSL — no ONNX/WASM, no model download. Ranked below the vanilla RD pick because the author calls it unstable: pin a version and VENDOR the single swissgl.min.js (the CDN mirror is @pluvial v0.0.0) behind a WebGL2 need()-guard, degrading to the existing gradient-mesh/aurora. RD is the always-available lightweight fallback tier under this.
topPicks.license: Apache-2.0 (lib + repo demos). Caveat: author-declared unstable — pin + vendor the single file. (Demo site assets are CC-BY-SA but not vendored.)

topPicks.name: Typed param-manifest convention ($fx.params-style schema) for generative snippets
topPicks.why: The structural half of 'seed reroll + score' that the vault is missing: utils/live-controls.js (Tweakpane) exposes knobs only to a HUMAN at runtime — there is no machine-readable manifest the compose engine can enumerate and set headlessly. A ~30-line vanilla utils/param-manifest.js (parse a <script type=application/json data-params> block of {id,name,type,default,options}, apply values, expose setParam/reroll(seed)) turns any generative snippet into something the headless harness can drive in a loop and the aesthetic scorer can rank. Reimplement the fxhash params shape vanilla — do NOT add the fxhash SDK (blockchain-coupled). Enables the scorer + ParamExplorer Gaussian-perturb-the-winner search strategy.

topPicks.name: Chrome deterministic-rendering flag profile (--deterministic-mode + --disable-skia-runtime-opts + software raster)
topPicks.why: Makes the seed-determinism gate trustworthy by neutralizing the known nondeterminism sources, and is partly net-new over the vault's existing srgb + SwANGLE software-WebGL setup. Net-new pieces: --deterministic-mode (sets compositor-stages-before-draw, disables PaintHolding, in-process-gpu), --disable-skia-runtime-opts (kills runtime CPU-SIMD Skia paths so raster matches across hardware), the CPU-vs-GPU pixel-perfection rationale (~99.7% CPU-raster identity vs 12-17px/10k GPU jitter), and the nondeterminism taxonomy (RNG/timers/AA/float/OffscreenCanvas) as gate-design knowledge. Critical 2026 caveat it carries: HeadlessExperimental.beginFrame was removed in Chromium 147+, so capture must use Page.captureScreenshot / page.screenshot with rAF timing (which the harness already does) — prevents shipping a broken capture path.

topPicks.name: Viewport-invariance / responsive-seed check (+ dHash/pHash perceptual tolerance tier)
topPicks.why: A second determinism axis unique to RESPONSIVE generative art and net-new: the vault verifies a canvas drew at ONE fixed viewport (1100x800 in render-smoke); nothing checks that a seed produces structurally equivalent output across sizes/DPR. Render the same seed at two viewports and assert equivalence after normalization (same dominant-color signature / perceptual hash / primitive count) — catching the common bug where layout/particle count is keyed off raw pixel width instead of seeded normalized space. Needs a perceptual hash, not byte hash, since dimensions differ: bundle a ~30-40 line vanilla dHash/pHash comparator (also the graceful tolerance tier between sha256-strict and 'looks similar' for benign AA jitter on hardware-GL runs). canvas-sketch's dimensions/PPI model is the size-independent-seeding reference. Complements the render-twice gate (temporal) with spatial/seed-normalization determinism.
topPicks.license: MIT (canvas-sketch reference); imagehash is BSD-2 reference only — reimplement dHash/pHash vanilla, no native/Python dep

topPicks.name: Domain-warped fBM value-noise field on canvas2D (Inigo Quilez recipe)
topPicks.why: A no-GPU companion to the WebGL noise the vault already has: noise-flow.glsl.js / gradient-flow.glsl.js are WebGL-bound, but there is no CPU/canvas2D domain-warp that works with no GPU, degrades on low-end devices, and is plottable/static-exportable. IQ's fbm(p+fbm(p+fbm(p))) recipe with the canonical offsets (5.2,1.3 / 1.7,9.2 / 8.3,2.8) through a palette LUT gives marble/cloud/topographic/ferrofluid backgrounds, reduced-motion-friendly (render low-res + CSS upscale). Distinct anti-dup value is the CPU/static path, not the look. Becomes trivial once the simplex-noise core lands (swap value-noise for it); ranks here as a derived deliverable rather than a foundational primitive.
topPicks.license: Technique freely usable (IQ article); value-noise reimplementation is original (MIT-able)

topPicks.name: Blue-noise dithering via void-and-cluster threshold texture (canvas2D)
topPicks.why: The 'designerly grain' tier the Bayer/error-diffusion pack can't reach: confirmed the vault's grain is SVG feTurbulence (white-noise-ish) and its dithering is ordered/SVG — nothing produces a true blue-noise spectrum (even point distribution, no repeating cross-hatch, no clumpy white-noise) for film-grain overlays, two-tone duotone posters and animation-stable dithered backgrounds. Public-domain Ulichney void-and-cluster; precompute a 64x64 tileable texture once (or ship a precomputed CC0 PNG from momentsingraphics.de to skip the generator entirely) so runtime is just a threshold compare — cheap and vanilla. Ship as a sibling of the dithering pack; ranked just under it as the optional grain upgrade.
topPicks.license: Public-domain algorithm (Ulichney); reference impls MIT/CC0 — reimplement, or vendor a CC0 texture

topPicks.name: Recursive circle packing (Kusama-style) — vanilla SVG snippet, seeded
topPicks.why: Rounds out the circle-packing leg with zero dep when a CDN isn't warranted: confirmed the vault's dot backgrounds are CSS uniform grids (dot-pattern.css), not size-varied collision packing. ~60-100 LOC emitting <circle> elements — place candidates, grow each until it touches a neighbor/bounds, reject overlaps, repeat with the prng.js seed — giving Kusama-dot posters and packed-disk fills. Lighter vanilla companion to the d3-delaunay cell approach; single static pattern, so lower impact than the differential-growth/d3 pair, but a clean reproducible poster fill with no dependency.
topPicks.license: Trivial public algorithm — reimplement clean-room (tutorial is MIT-spirit)

topPicks.name: seeded-generative / reproducible-art skill (.skill.md decision tree)
topPicks.why: The glue doc that operationalizes the determinism discipline the existing generative-art skill states ('always seed the PRNG') but never spells out — verified the skill only points to p5 randomSeed / $fx.rand with no PRNG choice guidance, no determinism checklist, no noise decision tree. Codifies: mulberry32 (one int) vs sfc32 (string/hash via xmur3, fxhash-grade); route ALL randomness through one seeded rand(); drive animation by frameCount not wall-clock; keep RNG call-order stable (the #1 determinism bug); seed GLSL noise via a uniform. Should fold in the OpenSimplex2/2S artifact-quality decision tree ('flow-field -> simplex 2D; visible terrain/displacement -> FastNoiseLite OpenSimplex2S; 4D loop -> simplex 4D; cellular -> FastNoiseLite Cellular') and the NCA-vs-RD-vs-Lenia 'generative-life' tree (record the ONNX-vs-GLSL verdict: in-browser NCA is GLSL with tiny baked weights, NOT onnxruntime-web). Keep tight, additive, cross-referencing not duplicating. Feeds the verification gate.
topPicks.license: Documentation/pattern (fxhash docs + hl-gen FAQ + Distill CC-BY as references)

categories.trends: The 2026 frontier in this category is "native-first, JS-deleting" platform primitives plus verification moat. Three platform features crossed Baseline and now obsolete whole classes of JS the vault ships: CSS Anchor Positioning (anchor-name/position-anchor/position-area/@position-try, ~83% reach) kills the getBoundingClientRect measure-loops behind every popover/tooltip/menu/segmented-indicator; the HTML Popover API + Invoker Commands (command/commandfor) give zero-JS open/close/top-layer/light-dismiss, fixing the z-index/stacking-context and clipped-dropdown bug class; and customizable native <select> (appearance:base-select + ::picker + <selectedcontent>) styles a real select with native a11y intact. Adjacent Baseline-ish wins: field-sizing:content (auto-grow inputs), interpolate-size/calc-size() (animate to height:auto), and scrollbar-gutter:stable. A second cluster is scroll-driven CSS — native carousels (::scroll-button/::scroll-marker, Chrome-only, must be @supports-gated) and scroll(self) overflow-fade — that replaces JS scroll listeners but is NOT yet cross-browser, so progressive-enhancement guards (@supports) are the load-bearing detail. The micro-interaction trend is "instant or it's broken": optimistic-UI lifecycle (apply→pending→commit/rollback→undo, from React useOptimistic/TanStack, still React-only in the wild so a vanilla version is genuinely net-new), per-digit NumberFlow rolling numbers, and spring-FLIP island morphs. Motion is shifting from JS-runtime springs to static CSS linear() spring/bounce tokens (Open Props, generated once at build time). Crucially, the highest-moat items are verification gates, not styles: CDP DOM.getTopLayerElements for open-state overlay checks, tabbable for focus-trap proof, clip-rect ancestor walks, WCAG 1.4.13 hover-popover conformance, and WCAG 2.2.1/4.1.3 toast aria-live/duration checks — diagnostics that catch bugs (including the vault's own toasts.js shipping duration:4000 with no aria-live) which currently pass silently. Strong anti-dup note: CSS Anchor Positioning shows up as four near-duplicate candidate entries (general primitive, select-picker positioner, caret-anchored, sliding-indicator) — they are ONE underlying primitive with applications, not four findings; likewise the native CSS carousel and its @supports guard are listed twice each.

topPicks.name: CDP DOM.getTopLayerElements — open-state overlay verification gate
topPicks.why: Highest moat-fit item in the category: a true diagnostic, not another style.
