The vault's render-smoke (tools/render-smoke.mjs, confirmed Playwright-driven) only asserts the default-CLOSED state and checkCoherence is pure static regex — nothing ever OPENS an overlay, so clipped/off-screen/not-top-layer dropdowns pass silently today. getTopLayerElements is the authoritative oracle for the single hardest open-state bug class. Reuses the existing playwright-core devDependency; zero shipped-snippet weight. Pair with a per-component open-recipe map. Net-new, permissive (CDP/BSD).
topPicks.license: N/A (Chromium/CDP, BSD-style; via existing playwright-core devDependency)

topPicks.name: HTML Popover API + Invoker Commands (popover attr, command/commandfor, popover=hint)
topPicks.why: Baseline (popover Apr 2025; invoker commands late-2025/2026) and deletes the most JS in the category: a trigger+panel needs ZERO script, and native top-layer kills the vault's z-index/stacking-context/clipped-dropdown bugs directly. Confirmed net-new: every floating panel in the vault (components/popover.js, blocks/tooltips.js, dropdown-menu.js, context-menu, command-palette) is JS-driven; grep-confirmed no popover=/commandfor/showPopover usage. Degrades cleanly (togglePopover shim, then JS Popover fallback). This is the behavior half of the one native floating primitive.

topPicks.name: CSS Anchor Positioning (anchor-name / position-anchor / position-area / @position-try) — single primitive
topPicks.why: The positioning half of the native floating primitive, and the highest-leverage JS-deletion in the vault: confirmed every floating panel positions via getBoundingClientRect, and the three sliding controls (animated-tabs.js, ios-segmented-control.js, nav-pack segctl) all JS+ResizeObserver-position their pill. Baseline 2026 (~83%) with an exact @supports(anchor-name:--x) guard falling back to the existing JS positioners. IMPORTANT DEDUP: this appears as 4 separate candidate entries (general, select-picker positioner, caret-anchored bar, sliding-indicator) — ship ONE primitive snippet plus thin application recipes (popover, select picker, sliding indicator, caret-anchored bar), not four findings. Encode the caveat: ship only the renamed properties (position-area/position-try-fallbacks), never Chrome-125 inset-area/position-try-options.

topPicks.name: tabbable (focus-trap/tabbable) — focus-trap verification oracle
topPicks.why: The verification partner to the top-layer gate. The vault SHIPS focus traps (drawer.js, dialog.js, celebration-screen.js) but has NO capability that proves a trap actually holds or that the background is inert. Injected harness-side via page.addScriptTag (NOT shipped into snippets, so components stay zero-dep), it asserts first-focus-on-open, Tab/Shift+Tab wrap, and background-not-reachable — the exact broken-trap failure the brief targets. MIT, tiny, battle-tested (v6.5.0). Moat-grade and non-duplicative (vault uses 'focus-trap' only as a component tag, never as a verifier).

topPicks.name: optimistic-action.js (vanilla optimistic-action lifecycle: apply→pending→commit/rollback→undo)
topPicks.why: The dominant 2025-26 interaction philosophy ('delayed feedback reads as broken') and genuinely net-new as vanilla: confirmed the vault has every ingredient SEPARATELY (toasts.js, inline-toast, success-checkmark, error-shake, feedback-pack timed-undo, buttons-states.js) but NO orchestrator that sequences capture→apply→pending→commit/rollback→undo on an ARBITRARY element. buttons-states.js is button-scoped with no DOM rollback; FeedbackPack.undo() is fire-and-forget. Codifies React useOptimistic/TanStack semantics framework-free, reusing existing visuals. High design-impact (likes/follows/toggles/reorders feel instant), zero-dep, MIT pattern.

topPicks.name: Toast/live-region a11y verification gate (toast-a11y rules in checkCoherence)
topPicks.why: Moat-grade and immediately self-justifying: the vault has ~8 toast styles and ZERO a11y verification, and its own blocks/toasts.js ships duration:4000 with no aria-live/role — so the gate catches the vault's own flagship snippet on day one. Pure-regex rules on composed HTML (same technique checkCoherence already uses): missing role=status/aria-live, interactive content in a live region, sub-5s auto-dismiss (WCAG 2.2.1), no keyboard dismiss. A diagnostic, not another style. Pairs with the WCAG 2.2.1/4.1.3 duration formula and the accessible-toast skill below.
topPicks.license: N/A (original rule logic; grounded in WCAG 2.2.1 / 4.1.3)

topPicks.name: Customizable native <select> (appearance:base-select + ::picker(select) + <selectedcontent>)
topPicks.why: The 2026 platform primitive that fully CSS-styles a real <select> while keeping native a11y, keyboard nav, and form semantics — supports rich option markup and top-layer picker animation. Confirmed net-new (zero appearance:base-select/::picker/selectedcontent in the vault; blocks/select.js + combobox are the JS fallbacks). Ships behind @supports(appearance:base-select) and degrades to a working native select. Encode caveats: inner <button> inert, decorative ::checkmark/::picker-icon aria-hidden, author as vanilla HTML (not JSX). Pairs with the anchor-positioning primitive.

topPicks.name: NumberFlow-style per-digit rolling-number (vanilla reimplementation)
topPicks.why: The canonical, trendy 2024-26 primitive for prices/stats/timers and confirmed distinct from everything in the vault: count-up.js / gsap counter.js interpolate a single VALUE and rewrite textContent; gradient-numbers.css is static; flip-clock is a mechanical half-fold. None do the per-digit overflow-masked translateY roll with Intl.NumberFormat place reconciliation. barvian/number-flow is MIT and actively maintained (v0.6.x, Feb 2026) but better reimplemented zero-dep. Gate behind reduced-motion (animated=false) to pass the verify gate. High design-impact, clean anti-dup.

topPicks.name: Spring-FLIP island morph engine (measure → spring-tween width/height/radius)
topPicks.why: Turns the vault's two existing island shells from fixed 5-state demos into a general status/now-playing/live-activity widget that springs to the MEASURED size of arbitrary content — the signature 'liquid' micro-interaction. Strong, verified anti-dup: the shells exist (mobile/ios-dynamic-island, components/dynamic-island) AND spring.js exists, but they're NOT wired together — both islands snap to hardcoded per-state px with a fixed bezier and never call spring.js or measure content. Ship as an UPGRADE to ios-dynamic-island.js, not a new shell. Vanilla, MIT pattern (cult-ui), high impact.
topPicks.license: MIT (cult-ui pattern; reimplemented vanilla, no Framer-Motion)

topPicks.name: Safe-triangle (hover-intent) vanilla primitive
topPicks.why: The actual hover-INTENT engine the vault is missing: confirmed tooltips.js has only a show-delay (no close-delay, no triangle, no touch suppression). Reimplements Floating UI safePolygon / Radix HoverCard semantics vanilla (open-on-dwell, close-on-leave-with-delay, point-in-polygon safe-triangle, touch suppression, focus parity) so it can wrap ANY trigger+panel — hover-card, citation popovers, megamenus. Zero-dep, reusable, and the natural JS partner for the hover-card path that native interestfor can't yet cover cross-browser.
topPicks.license: MIT-style technique (reimplement; Floating UI safePolygon MIT for reference)

topPicks.name: ViewTransitions.list() — per-item VT list reorder/filter/sort with WAAPI-FLIP fallback
topPicks.why: The missing ZERO-DEP path for multi-item layout animation. Confirmed: ViewTransitions.run() only cross-fades the whole list (no per-item names) and the only multi-item FLIP reorder in the vault (gsap/flip-layout.js) NEEDS the GSAP Flip plugin (CDN dep). Adds per-item view-transition-name via 2025 match-element inside startViewTransition, with a batched WAAPI-FLIP fallback. High-impact for tables/kanban/galleries; additive to existing transitions/view-transitions.js. Bake in the pitfalls (clear names after finished, unique names only during swap, reduced-motion skip).

topPicks.name: Open Props easing tokens (--ease-spring-1..5 / --ease-bounce as CSS linear())
topPicks.why: The 'blessed, drop-in, zero-JS-runtime spring tokens' the angle asks for, and confirmed net-new on the right axis: the vault has JS-runtime springs (spring.js, physics.js), JS f(t) easing (utils/easing.js), and blessed DURATION tokens (taste/motion.css 90-480ms) — but ZERO CSS linear() spring/bounce custom-property file. MIT lets you copy/regenerate strings verbatim. Critical recipe detail to encode: a spring linear() is incomplete without its paired duration; gate behind @supports(animation-timing-function:linear(0,1)) with a cubic-bezier fallback + reduced-motion swap. Propose ONLY the linear() spring+bounce subset (skip elastic, already covered by easing.js).

topPicks.name: Inline AI action bar (caret/selection-anchored 'Ask AI' command bar) — compose-genre
topPicks.why: The actual 2026 trend the question targets and a genuinely new ARCHETYPE: a caret/selection-anchored bar fusing cmd-K + slash + selection-toolbar with an AI lifecycle (idle verb list → free-text prompt → streaming → result-preview with Accept/Discard/Retry). Confirmed distinct from what exists: command-palette is global+static+synchronous, slash-menu inserts static blocks, ai-watermelon ctxbar is just a styled input+hotkey+onSubmit (no suggestions/ranking/async result/anchoring). Reimplement vanilla, reusing streaming-text + slash-menu trigger logic + the anchor-positioning primitive. Tiptap AI Menu is reference only (React + commercial Content-AI), not a dep.
topPicks.license: MIT (Tiptap core inspiration; AI Menu UI commercial — reference only)

topPicks.name: Native CSS carousel (::scroll-button + ::scroll-marker + scroll-marker-group) with @supports guard
topPicks.why: Replaces the entire JS layer of a dots+arrows carousel with declarative CSS, with browser-supplied ARIA/keyboard nav — directly shrinking the JS surface the verifier audits. Confirmed net-new (vault has layout/scroll-snap.css + carousel-pack 'minimal' but no scroll-button/scroll-marker primitive). DEDUP: this is listed twice (and its @supports(scroll-marker-group:after) guard a third time) — ship as ONE .carousel-css snippet that bundles the guard as its fallback contract, with the base layer being a plain scroll-snap strip reusing carousel-pack. Chrome-only mid-2026, so the @supports gate is load-bearing, not optional.
topPicks.license: Web platform (CSS Overflow L5, no restriction)

topPicks.name: Clip-rect / overflow-clipping detector for expanded overlays
topPicks.why: Quantifies the 'by how much is it clipped' that the top-layer gate only booleans, and catches the layout-overflow-when-expanded case (a populated combobox/palette overflowing its container or viewport). getBoundingClientRect reports the full unclipped box, so a naive in-viewport check misses overflow-hidden clipping — this ancestor-clip walk is the correct detector and the vault's render-smoke (closed preview only) lacks it. Vanilla, zero-dep, slots into the open-state verification loop alongside the top-layer + tabbable trio.

topPicks.name: WCAG 1.4.13 hover-popover conformance gate
topPicks.why: The natural verification partner to the safe-triangle and interestfor work — makes those snippets provably correct rather than merely present. Drives CDP to assert the three testable 1.4.13 rules (Dismissible via Escape, Hoverable via cursor-onto-panel, Persistent/no auto-hide) plus focus parity and touch sanity. Catches the single most common hover-popover slop failure (flicker/trap/timeout) automatically. Net-new diagnostic feeding the coherence score; no license concerns.

topPicks.name: data-state pending/confirmed/failed CSS layer (.opt-* attribute-driven states)
topPicks.why: Keeps the optimistic-action JS logic-only and themeable; independently useful for hand-rolled optimistic flows. Confirmed anti-dup: shimmer in the vault is component-local (shimmer-button, product-card skeleton, sources-panel loading) — there is NO generic 'this row/card/toggle is mid-flight' state styling droppable on any element. Reuses feedback/error-shake.css + success-checkmark.css for the confirmed/failed ends; honors prefers-reduced-motion. Ship as the CSS companion to optimistic-action.js.

topPicks.name: Pure-CSS scroll shadows / overflow fade via animation-timeline: scroll(self)
topPicks.why: Removes the JS dependency from the vault's exact existing effect: scroll-area.js reproduces edge-fades with a scroll listener + ResizeObserver + MutationObserver toggling .is-top/.is-bottom, and scroll-area.css ALREADY ships the mask gradients — only the class-toggling JS is being replaced. A scroll-timeline self-detects overflow, so it's also more correct. Confirmed the vault uses scroll(root)/view() but never scroll(self) for container affordances. Add as a .sa-fade-css variant inside scroll-area.css behind @supports(animation-timeline:scroll()) + @property; keep scroll-area.js as the fallback. Reduced-motion safe.

topPicks.name: Undo-commit window (deferred-commit reversible undo bound to the optimistic inverse)
topPicks.why: The Gmail/Linear soft-commit pattern: show result instantly, hold a 5s undo that actually DEFERS the irreversible side-effect and reverses via the inverse the optimistic primitive captured. Confirmed half-covered: feedback-pack.js FeedbackPack.undo() + .fbk-undo give the shrinking-bar toast VISUAL but it's fire-and-forget with no commit-deferral. Net-new is the deferral + inverse-reversal logic. Ship as Optimistic.commit(el,{undoMs,doCommit,undo}) reusing .fbk-undo — a logic addition, not a duplicate component.

topPicks.name: interestfor / Interest Invokers polyfill (mfreed7/interestfor)
topPicks.why: Solves tooltips.js's exact hover failure mode (raw mouseenter/leave, flicker, no touch, no keyboard parity) DECLARATIVELY, with CSS-controlled show/hide delays and :has-interest styling. Native in Chromium 142; the BSD-3 single-file polyfill makes it safe in Safari/Firefox today behind a feature guard so it no-ops on native. Caveat to encode: NOT Baseline (WebKit opposes the hover-tooltip use case on touch grounds) — gate behind its own narrow check and keep the JS safe-triangle for the cross-browser hover-card path. Permissive, vendor the min.js.

topPicks.name: CSS-only native carousel via input:checked / generic scroll-snap fallback tier
topPicks.why: The progressive-enhancement BASE layer beneath the native carousel: a usable swipeable scroll-snap strip (reusing carousel-pack 'minimal' + layout/scroll-snap.css) so non-Chromium browsers degrade gracefully when ::scroll-button/::scroll-marker are absent. Confirmed: this is the fallback contract for the native carousel, not a standalone component — merge it into that snippet rather than ship separately. Captured here so the dedup is explicit. Zero-JS, zero-dep.

topPicks.name: field-sizing: content (CSS auto-grow inputs & textareas)
topPicks.why: One declaration that removes JS keystroke auto-grow. Confirmed net-new as a CSS primitive: the only auto-grow in the vault is JS — chat-input.js recomputes height=min(scrollHeight,160) on every keystroke; no field-sizing anywhere. Ship guarded by @supports(field-sizing:content) with chat-input.js as fallback, always paired with max-height so it caps to a scrollbar. Small scope but a clean JS-removal that fits the composer/ai-input blocks.

topPicks.name: Generic accessible Toggle-Group (role=radiogroup + roving tabindex, W3C APG)
topPicks.why: The correct a11y contract for 'choose one of N' and the keyboard partner to the anchor-positioned sliding indicator. Confirmed net-new: roving tabindex exists ONLY for role=tablist tabs (components/tabs.js); the segmented/toggle family (bgrp-segmented, ios-seg, animated-tabs, nav-pack segctl) has no arrow-key model, no radiogroup role, no roving tabindex (ios-segmented-control.js/nav-pack have zero keydown/aria). ~40-line vanilla upgrade for any .tgroup>[role=radio]. W3C APG permissive license. Reusable across web/deck/app.

topPicks.name: Accessible-toast skill (aria-live politeness + focus + keyboard-parity decision tree)
topPicks.why: The build-it-right partner to the toast verification gate (skill = how; gate = catch when wrong). Confirmed net-new territory: there is a reactive-input skill and a view-transitions skill but NO skill covering notifications/toasts, and the vault's toast snippets (toast-stack-2 advertises swipe-dismiss, toasts.js pauses on hover only) have no keyboard parity or aria-live. Encodes polite vs assertive, single persistent live region, no-interactive-in-live-region (escalate to alertdialog reusing dialog.js focus-trap), swipe needs X+Esc, queue/cap, pause on hover AND focus.

topPicks.name: need()-guarded native-first floating primitive + skill
topPicks.why: Wraps the popover + anchor-positioning + invoker-commands trio into the vault's need()/guard idiom and a decision-tree skill telling the engine WHEN to emit native vs JS (tooltip/popover/menu/hover-card). Confirmed the vault has NO capability-guard pattern in any component JS, so this also introduces a reusable guard convention. Slightly lower than its constituents because its value is realized only once candidates 2-3 ship; it's the connective tissue + authoring guidance, not a standalone artifact. Encode the interestfor-not-Baseline caveat.

topPicks.name: WCAG 2.2.1 / 4.1.3 readable-duration formula for auto-dismiss timing
topPicks.why: The numeric backbone of both the toast gate and the accessible-toast skill: converts 'don't dismiss too fast' into a testable number (1s/3 words + 3s base; >=6s typical; >=8s for action toasts; sub-5s=fail). Confirmed net-new: nothing in the vault encodes accessible DWELL timing — the blessed-durations token set (90-480ms in motion.css) is animation easing, a different axis. Fixes the vault's 4000ms default. Bundle into the gate + skill rather than ship standalone.

topPicks.name: Batched WAAPI-FLIP fallback for multi-item layout animation
topPicks.why: The zero-dep fallback half of ViewTransitions.list(): First-Last-Invert-Play across a whole list/grid with all getBoundingClientRect reads batched before the mutation to avoid N forced reflows. Confirmed the vault's existing ViewTransitions.flip() does this for ONE element only. Makes the VT enhancement degrade gracefully instead of snapping where startViewTransition is absent or under reduced-motion. Ship as the internal fallback path (and standalone ViewTransitions.flipAll). Bundle with the VT-list finding.

topPicks.name: view-transition-class + ::view-transition-group(*) wildcard for shared per-item motion
topPicks.why: The taste/ergonomics CSS layer that makes the VT-list pattern practical: tag items with view-transition-class and style duration/easing/stagger once via the wildcard, plus element-scoped el.startViewTransition() (feature-detected) so a kanban/table transitions without freezing whole-page input. Best delivered as a small transitions/vt-list.css companion + a row in view-transitions.skill.md. Additive to existing named-transitions.css (which only times pseudo-elements for shared-element morphs).

topPicks.name: Frecency ranking (frequency + recency usage scoring)
topPicks.why: The result-ranking axis that should differentiate an inline AI bar from a global palette. Confirmed net-new: command-palette.js uses naive static fuzzyScore (score/haystack.length) with no recency/frequency, slash-menu does substring filter; nothing decays or weights by usage. ~30-line localStorage frecency util (double-exponential decay, blended with fuzzy) that both the new inline bar and the existing palette can opt into. MIT, zero-dep, verifiable.

topPicks.name: interpolate-size / calc-size() auto-size morph (animate pill to content-natural size, no JS)
topPicks.why: The no-JS progressive-enhancement layer of the island morph: interpolate-size:allow-keywords lets width/height transition to auto/max-content. Confirmed not in the vault (grep for calc-size/interpolate-size empty; ios-dynamic-island.css uses fixed-px states). Chromium-only today, so it layers UNDER the spring-FLIP engine (which provides cross-browser + bounce) rather than replacing it. Small CSS recipe + a :root opt-in note in view-transitions.skill.md.

topPicks.name: Content cross-fade + view-transition-name swap on island state change
topPicks.why: Fixes a visible flaw: when island data-state changes the SHELL tweens but CONTENT hard-swaps. Wrap the per-state content swap in ViewTransitions.run (already exists) with per-state view-transition-name so the browser cross-fades content synced to the shell morph. Confirmed net-new: no island in the vault cross-fades content on state change. Additive recipe documented in view-transitions.skill.md + ios-dynamic-island.js; ships as part of the island-morph upgrade.

topPicks.name: Pure-CSS odometer / rolling-digit (@property + mod()/round() + translateY)
topPicks.why: The zero-JS companion tier of NumberFlow for static pages/decks/scroll-driven contexts (pair with animation-timeline:scroll()). Confirmed net-new: the vault has no pure-CSS rolling-digit (count-up is JS+value-based, gradient-numbers static). Caveat: newer CSS math (mod/round/sibling-index) is bleeding-edge — ship with a static-numeral fallback so the verify gate passes. Complements, not duplicates, the JS NumberFlow snippet. MIT technique, reimplement.

topPicks.name: Inline-bar vs command-palette keyboard-model & ranking skill
topPicks.why: Captures the 'how does the inline bar differ from a global palette' answer as reusable rules: global palette = modal launcher (mod+K, centered, focus-trapped, fuzzy-only stable order); inline bar = caret-local, dual-triggered, Enter submits a prompt, reorder by context + frecency, degrade to free-text Ask-AI. Confirmed no skill covers command surfaces/keyboard models today. Small .skill.md tying together the inline-bar genre + frecency + anchoring; lower value as guidance vs an addable artifact.

topPicks.name: CSS Anchor-Positioned Sliding Indicator (anchor-name + :has fallback) — application of the anchor primitive
topPicks.why: A high-value APPLICATION of the anchor-positioning primitive: the active segment gets anchor-name and a single ::after sets left/right via anchor() so the browser interpolates position AND width natively — tracking content-width segments and surviving resize/wrap with ZERO resize-JS. Confirmed all three vault sliding controls (animated-tabs.js, ios-segmented-control.js, nav-pack segctl) are JS-rect/ResizeObserver-positioned. DEDUP NOTE: do not ship as a separate primitive — it's a recipe on top of the anchor-positioning finding, paired with the APG toggle-group for keyboard. Ranked here to flag it as a concrete must-build recipe.
topPicks.license: Public technique (W3C CSS Anchor Positioning spec)

topPicks.name: scrollbar-gutter: stable (+ both-edges)
topPicks.why: Smallest-effort, highest-certainty item: reserves scrollbar space to kill the content-shift jump when a region toggles overflow (dynamic lists, modals, tab panels). Confirmed net-new (scrollbar-gutter appears nowhere; scroll-area.css custom scrollbar doesn't address gutter stability). Baseline since 2024, no fallback needed. Trivial addition to components/scroll-area.css (.sa{scrollbar-gutter:stable} + .sa-gutter-both). Raises the no-reflow coherence signal.

topPicks.name: Sonner-style accessible-by-default upgrade for blocks/toasts.js
topPicks.why: The remediation that closes the loop so the toast gate doesn't flag the vault's own flagship toast forever: mount a single persistent aria-live region, bump default 4000→>=6000 (>=8000 with action), pause on hover AND focus, add Esc + focusable X with focus restoration, queue/cap. Confirmed targets real gaps (toasts.js is hover-only, no aria-live). Keep vanilla — Sonner is React (inspiration for API shape, not a dep). Lower value as a fix to an existing snippet rather than a new capability.

topPicks.name: CSS overflow detection as a verification/authoring signal (scroll() boolean + style query)
topPicks.why: Uses a scroll-timeline purely as a DETECTOR (@property --scroll toggle read via @container style query) so the anti-slop gate can assert 'scroll affordance present ⇒ element genuinely overflows', catching fake/always-on scroll cues. Confirmed nothing in the vault detects scrollability in CSS or gates affordance-honesty. Style-query branch Chromium-only, but the verifier runs headless Chrome so that's fine. Lower value — niche gate, best folded into the existing overflow checks.

topPicks.name: cult-ui Dynamic Island state-machine vocabulary (named presets + queued live-activity sequences)
topPicks.why: Ports the named size-preset map (compact/large/long/tall/ultra/minimalLeading…) + a scheduleAnimation([{state,delay}]) queue onto the vanilla island so it can script live-activity sequences — the actual iOS use case. Confirmed gap: ios-dynamic-island has 5 flat states and no sequencing API. Inspiration-grade (React/Framer-Motion MIT, not a drop-in) — the taxonomy + queue port cleanly. Ships as part of the island-morph upgrade, not standalone.

topPicks.name: Linear() Easing Generator (Jake Archibald & Adam Argyle) — build-time tool
topPicks.why: The AUTHORING mechanism behind the blessed spring tokens, NOT a runtime dep: run once at design time to mint vault-specific linear() strings (material-emphasized, overshoot-subtle) beyond the Open Props set, with a stop-precision slider for perf. Apache-2.0 source-available web app — cite in the easing snippet header + a motion skill so future tokens are generated correctly. Inspiration/tooling only.

topPicks.name: spring-easing — CSSSpringEasing() build-time generator
topPicks.why: Tiny lib whose CSSSpringEasing() returns a [linear()-string, duration] tuple computed from physics params, so each curve is authored WITH its correct paired duration — solving the 'springs need a paired duration' problem for the blessed-token file. Use as a BUILD-TIME Node generator (paste static output into the easing snippet), NOT a CDN runtime dep — the whole point of the angle is no physics-runtime JS. Overlaps conceptually with spring.js but produces static CSS. Inspiration/tooling.

topPicks.name: @axe-core/playwright — open-state a11y sweep (DROP / inspiration only)
topPicks.why: DROP as a core dep due to MPL-2.0 weak-copyleft (flagged) and overlap with the vault's existing static a11y checks in tools/audit.js. The only reusable insight is 'reopen the a11y sweep per-overlay-open-state' — keep that as guidance, and let the permissive top-layer + tabbable + clip-rect trio be the actual moat. Listed last so the license flag and dedup are explicit.
topPicks.license: MPL-2.0 (weak copyleft — FLAGGED; do not bundle into shipped snippets)

categories.trends: 2026 color tooling has decisively shifted from "pick nice hex" to "compose color by constraint, then prove it." Three currents dominate. (1) Perceptual contrast by construction: APCA (WCAG 3 draft, polarity-aware) and reverse-contrast solvers (apcach, Evil Martians' Harmonizer) compose a foreground/ramp to HIT a target Lc instead of checking after the fact — the headline stat being that ~12% of hex pairs pass WCAG AA but only ~0.08% pass APCA 90. The catch is licensing: the apca-w3 core math is a restricted "Limited W3 License" (non-commercial), so the published Lc formula must be CLEAN-ROOM reimplemented (~40 LOC) — never vendored. The clean, shippable win is the WCAG-model bisection, with APCA as documented aspiration. (2) Modern CSS color is now Baseline: oklch(from …) relative-color syntax derives whole token systems from one --accent at runtime, contrast-color() (Baseline Apr 2026) auto-picks accessible b/w text, and color(display-p3) + @media (color-gamut/dynamic-range) unlock ~30% more chroma on modern panels as pure progressive enhancement — all zero-JS, all net-new to sRGB-hex vaults. (3) Color science as a verification moat: CVD simulation (Viénot-1999 red-green matrix + Brettel-1997 for tritanopia, with mandatory sRGB linearization) + CIEDE2000 turns "never encode meaning by hue alone" from prose into an enforceable save-gate; the same gate pattern covers N×N role-aware WCAG pairing audits (EightShapes-style, but run per light/dark variant). Perceptually-uniform colormaps (viridis/cividis CC0; Crameri diverging MIT) bring magnitude-encoding to dataviz, and Kubelka-Munk pigment mixing (spectral.js, MIT) makes blue+yellow=green instead of additive mud. Cultural/historical palette corpora (Nippon, Werner 1814, Sanzo Wada, Dutch Masters) are public-domain or MIT and give editorial distinctiveness. The recurring license traps to avoid: apca-w3 (restricted), Mixbox (CC BY-NC), Harmonizer repo (unlicensed/all-rights-reserved — implement via MIT apcach instead), Color Hunt (ToS/scraper-only), Colorcet (CC-BY attribution vs CC0 alternatives), and the GPL-3 'sanzo' R package (use mattdesl's MIT port).

topPicks.name: CVD hue-collapse save-gate (Viénot-1999 + Brettel-1997 simulate → ΔE2000 collapse score)
topPicks.why: Highest moat-fit item. The vault's verify gate (render/overflow/a11y-focus/coherence/imagery) has ZERO color-accessibility checks (confirmed: no colorblind/APCA/CVD anywhere; only a runtime WCAG contrast-checker UI in color-tools-pack.css). color.skill.md warns 'never encode meaning by hue alone' in prose with no tooling behind it. This simulates each distinct semantic color under protan/deutan/tritan and flags pairs that collapse below a JND under ΔE2000 — turning a prose warning into an enforceable diagnostic. Public-domain science (Viénot is one 3×3 linear-RGB matrix; Brettel ~50-75 LOC for tritanopia), MIT reference impls. Vanilla-reimplementable, zero runtime weight. CRITICAL: linearize sRGB (gamma 2.4) before the matrix — skipping it is the classic Coblis/ColorJack bug; reimplement from DaltonLens/papers, not the inaccurate Lokno/ColorJack gist. Absorbs the duplicate 'CVD decision branch + verify gate' candidate (same capability, meodai-sourced).
topPicks.license: Public-domain algorithms (Brettel 1997 / Viénot 1999); reference impls MIT (DaltonLens, Colour)

topPicks.name: Palette pairing-matrix verification gate (role-aware N×N WCAG audit, per light/dark)
topPicks.why: The other top moat play, and the cleanest to ship. Enumerates the LEGITIMATE fg×bg role pairings the composer emits (--fg/--muted/--faint on --bg/--bg-alt/--surface; --on-accent on --accent/--accent-2; semantic ok/warn/danger/info as text; --border delta) and computes WCAG per pairing against role-specific thresholds, run TWICE (light + dark variant) so dark-mode polarity flips are caught. Net-new: confirmed the vault has only a runtime contrast-checker UI + opaque-hex PaletteGenerator.contrast()/bestText() (verified in palette-generator.js relLum/contrast lines), and the save-gate never audits the token matrix. Reuses the existing WCAG luminance formula already in palette-generator.js, mirrors MIT EightShapes Contrast Grid but role-aware, and slots next to the audit script with an identical exit-code contract. Turns every one of the ~50 palettes into a provably-safe role system.
topPicks.license: MIT (EightShapes Contrast Grid as reference pattern; gate itself vault-original)

topPicks.name: Runtime one-hue → token theme via relative-color syntax (oklch(from var(--accent) …))
topPicks.why: Massive design leverage, zero dependency, Baseline CSS. Derives an ENTIRE token system from a single --accent at runtime: hover/active by stepping L, surface/border by color-mix into the neutral, accent-2 by hue rotation, on-accent via contrast-color(). Confirmed net-new: ALL ~50 palettes in palettes.css are hand-authored token blocks and color.skill.md's 'build a palette from one brand hue' prescribes a MANUAL OKLCH ramp; there is ZERO oklch(from …) in the vault. Drives runtime theme pickers, white-label/multi-tenant theming, and 'theme from logo color' without authoring a palette block. Pair with @supports guard falling back to static .pal-* tokens.

topPicks.name: Perceptually-uniform sequential colormaps (viridis / magma / inferno / plasma / cividis) — vanilla t→color sampler
topPicks.why: Fills a genuine dataviz hole with the cleanest possible license. Confirmed net-new: scales.css is CATEGORICAL hue ramps (encode identity), heatmap-calendar tints ONE hue by level, charts-pro heatmap uses conic-gradient — there is NO continuous value→color mapping anywhere, and the dataviz skill is a library-selection tree with no colormap concept. This is the missing primitive for real heatmaps, choropleths, density plots, contour fills. Ship 5 ramps as small anchor tables + a matching CSS linear-gradient legend string. cividis is CVD-optimal and grayscale-safe so it doubles as the print/accessibility default and survives B&W printing (fits the vault's print-safe ethos). CC0/public-domain — copy verbatim, no attribution.
topPicks.license: CC0 / public-domain (viridis/magma/inferno/plasma CC0; cividis PNNL/DOE, effectively public domain)

topPicks.name: apcach reverse-contrast solver (WCAG-model path, vanilla reimplementation)
topPicks.why: The missing INVERSE of the vault's forward WCAG checker: composes a color to HIT a target ratio instead of checking after the fact. Fix OKLCH chroma+hue, bounded binary search over lightness (~20 iters) until measured contrast converges. Makes --fg/--muted/--accent provably accessible BY CONSTRUCTION. Confirmed net-new: PaletteGenerator only does forward contrast() on opaque hex; nothing targets a contrast. WCAG math is plain relative-luminance (unencumbered), the OKLCH bisection is the whole trick, polarity-aware so dark mode works. CRITICAL license boundary: ship ONLY the WCAG-model path; do NOT vendor apca-w3 (Limited W3 License). ~40-line vanilla helper the palette tokens consume at compose time.
topPicks.license: MIT (apcach + WCAG deps); reimplement clean-room. APCA-model path is restricted — excluded

topPicks.name: spectral.js (Kubelka-Munk paint-like mix) — vanilla pigment-mix util
topPicks.why: Genuinely novel visual primitive with a clean license. Confirmed net-new: the vault has ZERO pigment/Kubelka-Munk path (color-mix, k-means extract, additive/perceptual GradientBuilder only). spectral.mix(blue, yellow) = green where every existing path gives additive mud/gray. MIT, zero-dep, actively maintained (v3.0.0, ~1.2k stars). Exposes .OKLCh directly so it drops into the vault's OKLCH color/gradient skills. ~38 spectral samples + 7 primaries are static tables, fully vanilla-reimplementable; no official CDN so vendor the core as utils/pigment-mix.js exposing mix()/palette()/gradient(). Lets gradient stops route AROUND the muddy mid-tone. Absorbs the duplicate 'spectral.js cdn-snippet' candidate.

topPicks.name: Radix Colors 12-step semantic scale (role model + scale corpus)
topPicks.why: Highest-value GRANULARITY add, not just more palettes. Radix maps 12 perceptually-tuned steps to EXPLICIT UI roles (app/subtle bg, component bg/hover/active, subtle/component/focus-ring borders, solid bg, APCA-guaranteed text). Confirmed gap: every .pal-* ships ONE --border and ONE --accent with no encoded hover/active/pressed/focus-ring tiers; color.skill.md name-drops Radix only as a reason to move to OKLCH. Upgrading the ~10-token role model to a ~12-role model (add --accent-hover/-active, --border-subtle/-strong/--focus-ring) gives composed components correct interactive states for free. MIT, verbatim hex use.

topPicks.name: @material/material-color-utilities (quantize → Score → themeFromSourceColor)
topPicks.why: The genuine end-to-end bridge from gradient-extract's raw {dominant,accent,…} to a contrast-checked, role-assigned theme — nothing in the vault does this. Confirmed gradient-extract.js stops at raw RGB k-means with NO scoring/tonal/role/contrast. MCU's Score.score RANKS and FILTERS extracted colors (the missing curation step), then themeFromSourceColor/CorePalette expands ONE seed into tonal palettes that map 1:1 onto the vault role model. Apache-2.0, ZERO deps, ESM — loads via esm.sh behind a need()-guard, degrades to existing gradient-extract. Partial overlap ONLY on quantization (first step); Score-curation + tonal + harmonize are all net-new.

topPicks.name: Gradient Hygiene linter/auto-fixer (chroma-dip + sRGB-banding detector)
topPicks.why: A net-new gradient-quality diagnostic that fits the headless-Chrome save-gate cleanly, building directly on the gradients skill's OWN stated gap ('no systematic guard against the muddy-midtone chroma dip or sRGB banding'). Scans composed CSS for gradients lacking in oklch/oklab, samples the midpoint in OKLCH, flags (1) chroma dip below both endpoints (the orange→blue-through-gray artifact) and (2) banding risk on long low-contrast ramps; the fixer rewrites with a hue-arc hint and injects dither. Confirmed gradient-builder.js/gradient-extract.js only BUILD/EXTRACT, never diagnose. Detection is cheap and deterministic; color math reimplementable in ~40 lines, no dep.
topPicks.license: N/A (own implementation; public-domain CSS Color 4 + color math)

topPicks.name: apcach constant-APCA / consistent-chroma ramp engine (vanilla over culori)
topPicks.why: The missing 'constant-contrast ramp' primitive: generate step-600 at a fixed Lc so red, blue, and yellow read at the SAME contrast and chroma stays even across hues — fixing the 'L step looks even but contrast drifts across hues' problem that scales.css (fixed OKLCH-lightness grid) and palette-generator.js (HSL harmony, WCAG-only, verified) both have. Production engine behind Harmonizer. MIT; pair with culori (MIT) for OKLCH↔gamut. Reimplement the published APCA Lc formula (NOT vendor apca-w3) to sidestep the restricted license. Slightly below the WCAG reverse solver only because the perceptual half leans on reimplementing APCA math.
topPicks.license: MIT (apcach + culori); reimplement APCA Lc formula clean-room, do not vendor apca-w3

topPicks.name: P3-with-sRGB-fallback token layer (@media color-gamut/dynamic-range + @supports override)
topPicks.why: Pure progressive enhancement, zero JS, net-new. Ship existing sRGB hex as base, conditionally override the SAME var inside @media (dynamic-range:high){@supports(color:color(display-p3 …)){…}} — P3 panels gain ~30% chroma on vivid roles, everyone else untouched. Confirmed scales.css + all palettes are 100% sRGB hex with zero color(display-p3) and zero color-gamut queries (search returned none). Best as a sibling colors/scales-p3.css overriding only --accent/--accent-2/neon roles where the gamut shows. The dynamic-range guard prevents SDR-but-P3 panels over-saturating.

topPicks.why: The most safely-referenceable current trend set — Pinterest PUBLISHES the exact hexes (Cool Blue #D7EFFF, Jade #AEB8A0, Plum Noir #351E28, Wasabi #E9F056, Persimmon #FF5C34) and the descriptive color words are generic/non-trademarkable. Encode as 2-3 cohesive .pal-* themes (Plum-Noir dark-luxe, Cool-Blue light). Only the combined mark 'Pinterest Palette' is a TM — avoid it in class names.

Rougeux's modern reproduction publishes hex for all 110 openly (Skimmed Milk, Gallstone Yellow, Verdigris Green, Arterial Blood Red). Gives a uniquely literary/scientific-romantic, muted-historical palette family with NO vault equivalent — ideal for museum, editorial, naturalist, heritage-luxury genres. topPicks.name: Diverging colormaps for zero-centered data — Crameri vik/broc + ColorBrewer RdBu/BrBG
topPicks.why: The diverging companion to the sequential sampler (same lerp engine, fixed neutral midpoint, symmetric domain) for data centered on a meaningful zero: correlation matrices, anomaly/delta maps, profit-vs-loss, sentiment. Confirmed net-new: no diverging scale exists; scales.css has no diverging/sequential concept and charts-pro has no two-sided ramp. Ship Crameri vik/broc (perceptually-uniform, CVD-safe, MIT) + ColorBrewer RdBu/BrBG (Apache-2.0) as familiar fallbacks. Crucially AVOIDS the green-red trap that fails red-green CVD. Note in skill: diverging REQUIRES a known zero, else use sequential.
topPicks.license: MIT (Crameri Scientific Colour Maps v8); ColorBrewer Apache-2.0

topPicks.name: Centered ±0.5/255 ordered-noise anti-banding dither (interleaved gradient noise)
topPicks.why: The QUANTIFIED dither half of the hygiene fixer's output, explicitly distinct from the vault's decorative grain. Add per-pixel noise of amplitude exactly one quantization step centered on zero (+= (1/255)*noise - 0.5/255) so bands dissolve to stipple WITHOUT shifting average brightness or reading as texture. Confirmed the vault's .dither-* (stepped-gradient.css) is the OPPOSITE intent — it POSTERIZES into hard bands for a riso look — and gnoise is a visible-texture overlay; there is no invisible band-killer for smooth premium gradients. Vanilla via tuned feTurbulence+feComponentTransfer at ~1/255, or a 1px IGN-noise tile at ~0.4% opacity. Auto-injected only on long low-contrast ramps.

topPicks.name: OKLCH hue-arc selection rule (shorter / longer / increasing / decreasing)
topPicks.why: Small decision-tree skill that powers the hygiene fixer's arc choice and de-risks the vault's existing animated/conic gradients (.ganm-rainbow/.ganm-hue/.ganm-conic-spin, which currently rely on implicit shorter-arc behavior). Encodes WHEN to use each hue-interpolation keyword: default shorter for gaps <180° with no dead-zone; longer when the short arc passes through muddy hues; increasing/decreasing to FORCE a traversal that won't flip when the hue delta crosses 180° during animation. The vault gives ZERO guidance here (gradients skill mentions in oklch once, never the hue path). Net-new decision logic, public CSS Color 4.

topPicks.name: CSS contrast-color() (Baseline 2026) as the graceful-degradation floor
topPicks.why: Net-new native CSS (Chrome 147 / FF 146 / Safari 26, Baseline Apr 2026) that auto-picks accessible b/w text. It does NOT replace the reverse solver (UA-defined, WCAG2-only, b/w-only) — position it as the zero-JS FALLBACK floor: emit contrast-color(var(--bg)) for the simple case, reserve the JS bisection for tinted --muted/--accent that need a specific Lc/ratio. Pairs with the solver and the runtime-derivation snippet (--on-accent) as a progressive-enhancement story. Behind @supports(color:contrast-color(white)). Zero matches in vault.

topPicks.name: A Dictionary of Colour Combinations (Sanzo Wada) — ~6 pal-* from harmony pairs
topPicks.why: matt deslauriers's MIT-cleaned port of Wada's 1930s color theory: 159 named colors organized into 348 designer-vetted 2-4 color COMBINATIONS. Unlike a flat swatch list, the combinations are ready-made harmony pairs that map cleanly onto the vault's token model where each pal-* needs --accent + --accent-2 harmony. Adds a vintage-modernist, sophisticated-muted family absent from the vault. Curate ~6 pal-* themes seeded from Wada combinations. USE the mattdesl/MIT JSON — AVOID the GPL-3 CRAN 'sanzo' R package.
topPicks.license: MIT (mattdesl data port). Avoid GPL-3 CRAN 'sanzo' R package

topPicks.name: Dutch Masters painting palettes (Vermeer / Rembrandt) — ~3-4 dark pal-* themes
topPicks.why: MIT-sourced palettes sampled from 17th-c. Dutch master paintings (anatomy red-muscle, brown-cloak, ultramarine, ochre; milkmaid/pearl-earring/view-of-Delft). Deep-chiaroscuro painterly grounds that map beautifully onto the vault's dark-surface token sets and complement (don't overlap) pal-luxe-black-gold/midnight. Strong for gallery/fine-art/heritage-luxury composes. Only the hex values are needed; the R packaging is incidental. Express as ~3-4 dark pal-* themes.

topPicks.name: OKLCH runtime-derivation gotchas (clamp guard + l-is-unitless + contrast-color fallback)
topPicks.why: The skill rules that make the relative-color theme snippet ROBUST — the moat-y, non-duplicative complement to it. Encodes: (1) inside oklch(from …) channels are UNITLESS (l is 0-1, calc(l+20%) is INVALID, must be calc(l+.08)); (2) calc() is NOT auto-guarded so wrap every derived channel in clamp(); (3) pushing chroma on saturated hues silently clips at the gamut boundary, so prefer color-mix(in oklab) for surfaces and reserve chroma bumps for accents; (4) gate contrast-color() behind @supports with a luminance fallback. color.skill.md teaches OKLCH ramp theory but says nothing about these runtime traps. Could ship as a coherence check flagging un-clamped calc() in derived color tokens.

topPicks.name: Material Blend.harmonize() — shift brand/UI colors toward an extracted seed
topPicks.why: The 'make these N swatches feel like ONE palette' operation: rotates a color's HUE a bounded amount toward a source seed in HCT, preserving tone+chroma. Turns extracted-but-clashing image colors (or a fixed ok/warn/danger/info set) into a harmonious on-brand set. Confirmed net-new: color.skill.md describes harmony SCHEMES as prose tables but ships no function that mechanically harmonizes arbitrary colors toward a hue (palette-generator.js only does scheme generation from one seed). Algorithm is ~40 lines (HCT hue-rotation with a degree cap) — reimplement as a zero-dep vanilla snippet citing the Apache-2.0 source, avoiding bundle/NOTICE burden. Companion to the MCU seed-extraction snippet.

topPicks.name: color.skill.md APCA-by-construction branch (design to an Lc target, not a WCAG footnote)
topPicks.why: color.skill.md mentions APCA only as a rough WCAG map ('Lc 60 ≈ 4.5:1'). Add a real branch: pick text shade by target Lc (60 fluent body / 75 reading / 90 preferred), account for weight+size, and prefer APCA over WCAG-2 specifically for dark mode where WCAG-2 is over/under-strict. Pairs naturally with the existing OKLCH ramp section (fix hue/chroma, step L until Lc target hit) and grounds the reverse-contrast solver in the skill. Cheap, additive, net-new as a decision branch. APCA itself is open (formula reimplementable); just avoid vendoring apca-w3 and the trademarked name in output.
topPicks.license: CC BY 4.0 (skill text); APCA formula open/reimplementable

topPicks.why: The SHOW companion to the CVD gate: applies the same CVD matrices via SVG feColorMatrix (color-interpolation-filters='linearRGB') or canvas to render the composed page as protanopes/deuteranopes/tritanopes see it. DaltonLens publishes drop-in feColorMatrix coefficients. Net-new (vault has color-grade and shader effects but nothing simulating CVD). Ranked just below the gate because it's a workbench-preview convenience, not an enforced diagnostic. MUST set linearRGB interpolation or the simulation is invalid; avoid the disavowed ColorJack/Lokno matrices.

topPicks.why: The cleanest license of all sources (CC0 / US-government public domain — copy verbatim, zero attribution). 24 color families on 5-90 grade ramps engineered around a contrast model. Two net-new wins: (a) government/civic/healthcare/accessibility-first palette seeds the vault lacks (pal-civic-*), and (b) cool/warm hue TRIPLES (red-cool vs red-warm, gray-cool vs gray-warm) as vetted public-domain source for the 'tint neutrals toward the brand hue' rule color.skill.md teaches but ships no vetted values for. Add as a handful of vetted .pal-* sets, not the full ~240-token dump.

categories.trends: Verified against the live vault: every chart in data-viz/ is a one-shot SVG render built on hand-rolled `v/maxVal*plotH` math with a `niceMax()` that ceilings to the next power of 10 and a fixed `gridSteps=4`; there is zero hover/crosshair interaction (chart-network's pointer code is force-sim only), zero a11y (no role/aria/table/tabindex/pattern in any generator), zero perceptual colormaps, zero canvas data path, zero streaming, and the only map is a hardcoded equirectangular Greece bake. The 2024-2026 dataviz mainstream has converged on exactly the gaps this set targets: (1) accessibility as a first-class, machine-checkable invariant (WCAG data-viz criteria — role=img + name, table fallback, non-color encoding, focusable points; sonification is the emerging 2025 channel) — and the Design Book coherence gate currently scores only score/counts/authenticity, so an a11y subscore is pure net-new moat; (2) perceptually-uniform + colorblind-safe palettes as the default encoding layer (viridis CC0, ColorBrewer diverging, Okabe-Ito categorical) replacing decorative hex arrays; (3) a real scale/ticks/axis foundation (the d3-scale/d3-array `tickStep`→{1,2,5}×10ⁿ + `nice()` algorithm) that visibly upgrades every existing axis and is the literal prerequisite for crosshair/brush/annotation (all need pixel→domain `.invert()`); (4) decorate-don't-replace interaction overlays (bisector nearest-point, Voronoi hitboxes, brush/focus+context) attached to existing SVG rather than swapping in a chart engine; (5) big-data downsampling (LTTB + min-max, the Chart.js/ECharts default) as a pre-pass feeding the same path builder. License terrain is clean — the high-value picks are vanilla-reimplementable from ISC/MIT/CC0/Apache references; the only flags are Highcharts/Circos/Grafana (proprietary/AGPL/GPL — inspiration only, vendor nothing) and CDN map tiles (ODbL attribution for MapLibre/OpenFreeMap). The dominant strategic pattern is the verification-gate-plus-helper pair (a11y, big-series perf) since diagnostics are the vault's actual differentiator, not more chart types.

This adds a brand-new save-gate subscore on the vault's actual differentiator (diagnostics), reuses the existing headless-Chrome harness, and applies to every composed page with a chart. Pairs 1:1 with the helper below (gate flags, helper fixes).

Every vault chart is fed a {label,value} array so the helper can derive the <table> fallback for free with no new authoring burden. charts-pro.css already has ONE reduced-motion block, so the helper correctly scopes to name+table+focus+pattern and leaves motion to CSS. One ChartA11y.enhance() call retrofits 15 generators into WCAG compliance and makes composed pages pass the gate above.

The ~30-line {1,2,5}x10^n step + nice() rounding visibly upgrades EVERY existing chart's y-axis with a tiny, public, stable algorithm. Lands cleanly with the scale primitive below.

This single util upgrades line/bar/area/scatter at once AND is the literal prerequisite for crosshair, brush, and annotation (all need pixel->domain inversion). Reimplement to stay zero-dep; pairs with nice-ticks as one 'chart foundation' bundle.

Verified all three cartesian charts share one coordinate convention (pad/plotW/plotH, i/(len-1)*plotW+pad.left, v/maxVal) so a ~120-line overlay composes across bar/line/area without rewriting them. Every real report annotates charts (SLA line, goal band, 'launch' marker) and the vault literally cannot today. Convergent model across Recharts/ECharts/Chart.js, so it teaches one mental model.

Confirmed no color-scale encoder exists (charts use decorative #818cf8-style arrays + Math.random() fills in treemap). The optional CVD-matrix simulator doubles as a verification capability (the vault's moat) to confirm a chosen ramp stays colorblind-distinguishable. Keystone dependency for heatmap/treemap/future choropleth magnitude encoding.

topPicks.name: viridis / magma / inferno / plasma / cividis (perceptually-uniform sequential colormaps)
topPicks.license: CC0 public domain — maximally permissive, no attribution
topPicks.why: Biggest missing encoding primitive, gap confirmed (no viridis/magma anywhere except unrelated GLSL shaders). Constant-lightness, colorblind-safe sequential ramps stored as static hex anchor arrays (zero dep). Directly upgrades heatmap-calendar/treemap/charts-pro magnitude encoding which currently use single-hue or random fills. CC0 is the safest license in the whole set.

topPicks.name: ColorBrewer diverging + sequential schemes (CB-safe subset: RdBu/BrBG/PiYG + Blues/YlOrRd)
topPicks.why: The unique value is DIVERGING ramps (two hues meeting at a neutral midpoint) which viridis does not cover and the vault has zero of — the correct encoding for signed/centered data (+/- change, above/below average, sentiment). Ship only the colorblind-safe schemes. Completes the encoding pair: viridis = sequential default, ColorBrewer diverging = signed-data default; both feed the interpolator above.

topPicks.name: GeoChart — vanilla GeoJSON choropleth + pluggable projection engine (Mercator/EqualEarth/AlbersUsa, auto-fit)
topPicks.license: original code; projection math public domain (Snyder/Savric) — safe
topPicks.why: Net-new geographic capability, gap confirmed: data-viz/ has bar/line/area/pie/radar/sankey/treemap/funnel/gauge/network but NO map, and the only map anywhere is a hardcoded equirectangular GREECE_MAP_PROJECTION bake (verified in greece-map-data.js) with no pluggable projection. The projection math is tiny vanilla and the renderer reuses GreeceMap's existing color-scale/legend/tooltip/hover/pin code wholesale, turning a one-country artifact into a world/US/any-region engine with the same verification-friendly pure-SVG output.

topPicks.name: chart-interaction.js — vanilla crosshair + shared multi-series tooltip + focus-dot decorator (bisector/pointer-inversion)
topPicks.license: MIT (reimplemented from public d3-graph-gallery/Hazledine patterns) — safe
topPicks.why: Net-new interaction, gap confirmed: no pointermove/crosshair/bisect/tooltip in any chart (chart-network's pointer code is force-sim only). The charts' deterministic linear x-scale (i=round((px-padLeft)/plotW*(n-1))) makes pointer->data inversion trivial, so ONE ~120-line ChartInteraction.attach() decorator upgrades the whole folder at once. Non-duplicative vs cursor-hud.js (pixel-grid snap, data-blind) and tooltips.js (static element tooltips, no nearest-point). Synced-crosshair mode serves dashboard composes. Slightly behind the foundation picks because it benefits from the scale primitive's .invert().

topPicks.name: Box-and-whisker chart (chart-box.js)
topPicks.why: Most-requested distribution chart, gap confirmed by word-boundary grep (zero hits for boxplot/whisker/quantile). The single biggest distribution hole; ~60 lines of pure vanilla dropping straight into the chart-bar.js SVGNS + namespaced-global house style (sort -> quantile -> draw box+whiskers+outliers), supports grouped/categorical x. Keystone of a 'distribution charts' bundle (shares quantile() with violin/ridgeline).

topPicks.name: Bullet graph (Stephen Few spec)
topPicks.license: vendor-neutral public spec (D3 ref gist BSD-3) — safe
topPicks.why: THE canonical exec-dashboard micro-chart and it's missing (word-boundary grep: zero hits). Verified the vault has goal-bar/usage-meter/gauge single-value progress but nothing overlaying actual-vs-target-vs-qualitative-bands on a shared scale. ~30-40px tall, stacks into report tables, trivial vanilla SVG (bands as rects, measure bar, 2px target rule). High density payoff for report/deck genres at near-zero cost.

topPicks.name: Chord diagram (chart-chord.js, vanilla SVG center-pull bezier)
topPicks.license: ISC (d3-chord reference only; reimplementation original) — safe
topPicks.why: Fills a distinct relationship slot, gap confirmed (no chord in INDEX/dataviz/diagrams/charts-pro). Distinct from chart-sankey.js (directed stage flow) and chart-network.js (force-directed organic): chord = symmetric n*n matrix where every node relates to every node (migration/trade/co-occurrence). Fully vanilla arc+bezier math (~120-160 lines) with the signature center-control-point ribbon, same Chord.create('#el',{matrix,labels}) shape as sankey. Shares a center-pull bezier helper with the arc-diagram candidate.

topPicks.name: lttb.js — Largest-Triangle-Three-Buckets downsampler (+ min-max decimation sibling)
topPicks.license: MIT-compatible (Steinarsson thesis; permissive ports) — reimplement, safe
topPicks.why: The missing big-data perf primitive the skill's own '<1k SVG' rule prescribes but never shipped. ~40-60 line zero-dep O(n) pre-pass that feeds the SAME existing SVG path builder (non-invasive), reducing 100k+ points to ~1/pixel while preserving contour (100k->1k in ~15ms). Bundle the ~15-line min-max sibling (Chart.js's DEFAULT, ECharts 'minmax' in 5.5) for noisy signals where a lone spike must survive. Tie sample count to chart pixel width like Chart.js/ECharts. Strong but slightly behind the foundation/a11y picks because impact is gated on large-series composes.

topPicks.name: StreamChart — vanilla rolling-window streaming line/area (ring buffer + RAF, sub-pixel time scroll)
topPicks.license: MIT (Smoothie Charts reference) — reimplement vanilla, safe

Typed-array ring buffer (no array.shift() GC churn) + decoupled ingest/RAF + Smoothie strip-chart scroll delivers the monitoring-dashboard look the vault completely lacks. Foundation for the ops/observability compose genre. Reimplement vanilla — the technique is small and Smoothie is dormant.

topPicks.name: brush-zoom-overview (focus+context range selector decorator)

Zero-dep decorator (ChartBrush.attach(chartEl,{data,onRange})) wraps existing chart-line/area: context strip + draggable band + edge handles + double-click reset. Composes with the LTTB reslice-on-zoom angle and the crosshair decorator. Needs the scale primitive's .invert() to be cleanest, hence just behind the foundation.

topPicks.name: Violin plot + small Gaussian-KDE helper (chart-violin.js + kde.js)

The reusable ~30-line vanilla Gaussian KDE (Silverman bandwidth) is the keystone util that ALSO powers ridgeline, so it earns its place beyond the chart itself. Expose only the bandwidth parameter and document it. Ships in the distribution bundle after box-plot (reuses quantile() for the overlaid quartile lines).

topPicks.name: Horizon chart (banded/layered area)

Vanilla SVG (one path per band with clip/translate). Pairs with the small-multiples helper for dense executive dashboards.

topPicks.name: Deviation/ranking/distribution gap-fill snippets (diverging bar, slope, lollipop/dumbbell, waffle, histogram)

Each is a <120-line builder in the chart-bar.js house style. Prioritize diverging-bar + slope + dumbbell as the highest-frequency missing FT charts.

topPicks.name: verification gate: 'large series rendered to SVG' perf/slop diagnostic
topPicks.why: Moat play, gap confirmed: the headless-Chrome/coherence gate checks overflow/imagery/authenticity but has zero concept of a data-scale perf cliff (verified — confirmed no canvas data path and no downsample). Detect via DOM (count path/circle/polyline children or inspect series length) and flag >~2000 points rendered to SVG with no pre-pass, with a principled cutoff matching Chart.js (4x canvas width) / ECharts ('data far greater than pixels'). Suggested fix routes through lttb.js + canvas renderer; ships as one bundle with those two.

topPicks.name: Okabe-Ito colorblind-safe categorical palette (+ Tableau-10 second qualitative set)

The R 4.0+ default 8-color qualitative set makes multi-series charts default to a colorblind-safe rotation. Completes the encoding triad (categorical/sequential/diverging) with viridis + ColorBrewer.

topPicks.name: Trend/forecast confidence band (high-low fill series)
topPicks.license: MIT (uPlot reference, technique only) — reimplemented, safe

Small extension to chart-line/area: reuse the existing area-path builder (verified it already closes a fill region at L...plotH...Z) but compute the lower boundary from lo[] instead of the baseline, plus a forecast-cutoff x and a dashed continuation. Colors derived from series color at low opacity to match the existing rgba(...,0.18) fill convention.

Mirrors the ChartLine/ChartArea API into one Path2D, auto-engaging LTTB above ~2k points for millisecond renders. topPicks.name: chart-chooser.skill.md (FT Visual Vocabulary intent->snippet router)

topPicks.name: Beeswarm chart via one-pass dodge (chart-beeswarm.js)

Greedy one-pass dodge is explicitly preferred over d3-force collision because it's deterministic (no rAF/sim settling), which matters for the headless-Chrome verify save-gate — no preserveDrawingBuffer/animation-settle flakiness. ~40 lines vanilla; reimplement the algorithm, don't ship the stale dep.

Dead-simple vanilla (equal-width rects above/below a baseline) pairing with the existing Sparkline global. High value-per-line for status tables.

topPicks.why: The render half of the scale+ticks foundation, and it ships the two things d3-axis itself LACKS (auto rotation + collision-aware thinning that d3fc had to add). Confirmed the vault drops raw text labels at fixed positions with no rotation/overflow handling, so long category/date labels collide — which would also trip the verifier's overflow/coherence gate. Ties directly into the save-gate: rotated/thinned labels prevent flagged overflow.

High visual payoff for time-series-by-category / model-comparison report sections. Bundles with box+violin+beeswarm under one 'distribution charts' addition sharing quantile()+kde()+dodge().

topPicks.name: Small-multiples / trellis grid helper (shared-scale faceting)

Distinct from charts-pro sparkGrid (per-card mini bars with NO shared scale, confirmed). Highest leverage for report/deck genres because it amplifies the value of every other micro-chart in this set.

topPicks.name: chart-network/scatter Voronoi hitbox via d3-delaunay (nearest-point hover for scatter/dense)

Net-new (chart-network is force physics, no Voronoi/nearest-point). Ship behind a need()-guard (the rive-player.js lazy-inject pattern is confirmed present) that degrades to the linear-bisector decorator so it never hard-fails verification.

The CAVEAT is the tiles (ODbL attribution control), not the BSD-3 engine.

Thin taste recipe, not new rendering code; depends on ChartAnnotate + the band landing first. Use Grafana purely as the legend/annotation UX convention — it's AGPL, vendor nothing.

topPicks.name: Monitoring/observability dashboard — live-data compose genre

Confirmed structure/dashboard.html is fully static today. Distinct because it owns the time axis + ingest loop; depends on the StreamChart snippet shipping first.

topPicks.name: Arc diagram + hierarchical edge bundling (vanilla SVG, 1D node axis)

The linear-axis cousin of the chord diagram for ORDERED-axis relationships (genome positions, timeline co-mentions, dependency graphs) without Sankey's stage constraint. Shares the center-pull bezier math core with the chord snippet (ship them together). Deterministic layout passes headless verify without sim-settling, unlike chart-network.js.

A US-states choropleth ships as pure path strings, identical in shape to greece-map-data.js. The cleanest data half for the GeoChart engine.

topPicks.name: globe.gl — 3D point-density globe with KDE heatmap

CAVEAT for the guard (confirmed three.js already loads via 3d-web.skill.md): the globe.gl UMD bundle SHIPS its own three.js — the guard must avoid a double-three.js load to prevent two THREE instances.

Ships as TopoJSON (delta-encoded arcs) needing a ~40-line topojson.feature() decoder, small enough to inline vanilla or guard behind a CDN need(). Degrades gracefully to a stat table if offline. Pairs 1:1 with the GeoChart projection engine.

topPicks.name: bake-a-map.skill.md — decision tree mirroring the Greece pipeline for any region
topPicks.why: Codifies the currently-undocumented Greece bake (confirmed: GREECE_MAP_PROJECTION + frozen paths + viewBox + id->name table) into a repeatable recipe: get GeoJSON -> pick projection by region -> auto-fit bbox -> freeze to path strings -> feed the GeoChart renderer. Encodes the project-at-runtime vs bake tradeoff. Turns a one-off artifact into a capability; depends on the GeoChart engine for the runtime branch.

topPicks.name: webgl-plot (danchitnis) — realtime waveform/scope escape hatch
topPicks.why: The need()-guarded GPU escape hatch BEHIND the vanilla StreamChart: tens of thousands of points at 60fps for many-channel/kHz waveforms (ECG/telemetry/oscilloscope) where Canvas2D chokes. Not duplicative — dataviz.skill.md lists chart libs but none do realtime scope streaming, and shaders.skill.md is generic WebGL not line-plotting. Degrades to the vanilla StreamChart when WebGL is unavailable.

topPicks.name: dataviz.skill.md addendum — 'Make a static chart interactive' decision branch
topPicks.why: Cheap and it's what makes the crosshair/Voronoi/brush picks discoverable by the compose engine. Routes 'I want hover' -> linear x: bisector; scatter/dense: Voronoi; stacked dashboard: synced crosshair, and codifies the 'decorate, don't rewrite' rule the existing skill (which only routes library SELECTION) lacks. Lower standalone value, high glue value; cross-links the new snippets.
