Pure styling/decision reference — Circos is GPL/Perl, vendor nothing. Also seeds a 'which relationship viz' row (chord/arc/sankey/network) in the skill.

topPicks.name: Smoothie Charts strip-chart scroll technique (sub-pixel time scroll / blit-shift)
topPicks.license: MIT (Smoothie) — technique, not a shipped dep
topPicks.why: The 'why it doesn't jank' explanation that makes the StreamChart snippet correct: treat now as a moving origin and translate the series left by (now-last)*pxPerMs each frame (sub-pixel interpolation) instead of a naive shift()+full-clear, plus the optional drawImage(canvas,-dx,0) blit-shift for long windows. Folds into the skill's Streaming section as the decision note backing the StreamChart candidate — not a separate file.

topPicks.name: Highcharts Sonification / Sonification Studio (data-to-audio audio charts)
topPicks.license: Highcharts proprietary/non-commercial-free (FLAG) — reimplement in vanilla Web Audio
topPicks.why: The one a11y channel the vault completely lacks (confirmed audio-ui/local-audio skills cover audio-REACTIVE visuals, not data-as-audio output) and the WCAG search flagged it as the emerging 2025 differentiator. Capture as inspiration only — Highcharts is commercial, NOT MIT. A fresh ~40-line vanilla sonify(values) (x->time, y->OscillatorNode pitch) folds into chart-a11y.js as an optional play button; prefer it over the stale BSD-3 'sonifier' dep.

topPicks.name: Highcharts Stock Navigator (range-selector strip visual spec) + uPlot (CDN fallback)
topPicks.license: Highcharts Stock COMMERCIAL (FLAG, never vendor); uPlot MIT
topPicks.why: Inspiration only for the brush-zoom decorator's appearance: the navigator mask + grab handles + draggable window + preset-range pill row (1D/1W/1M/YTD/ALL) is the Stripe/Datadog/financial look to copy in vanilla. Highcharts Stock is proprietary — never CDN-load or vendor. uPlot (MIT) is the optional CDN escape hatch for huge series only and a source for the brush+wheel-zoom hook model, but NOT a default since it's a full canvas engine that overlaps the vault's SVG chart suite. The preset-range pill row is the cheap high-value bit to bundle with the vanilla brush.

topPicks.name: Canvas/screenshot-sample-behind-glyph worst-case contrast gate (pixel-sampled fg/effective-bg)
topPicks.why: THE flagship gap, confirmed in code. inspect.js bgOf() (line 308) literally returns null and SKIPS the contrast check the moment a text node sits on a gradient/image/backdrop-filter background — i.e. it is blind on exactly the mesh/aurora/holographic surfaces this vault composes by default. The probe already computes WCAG luminance (lum/parseRgb, lines 300-307) and already runs in headless Chrome; this swaps the give-up bgOf() for sampling the rendered pixels under each glyph rect (worst-case min over the rect) so the existing 'contrast 4.5/3:1' penalty (5/fail, line 494) finally fires on the surfaces it currently ignores. Highest design-impact x moat-fit: it makes an already-scored check TRUE instead of silently passing. NOTE the real architecture: the gate is Chrome-CLI via execFile + an in-page <script> probe and --screenshot, NOT Puppeteer/Playwright — sample via the existing --screenshot PNG path, not page.screenshot().
topPicks.license: N/A (own vanilla in-page technique; reuses existing lum()/WCAG math)

topPicks.name: WCAG21 contrast scoring as a vanilla luminance-ratio snippet (the scorer for the glyph-sampling gate)
topPicks.why: The scoring half, and it is ALREADY HALF-BUILT: inspect.js lines 300-307 (lum/relative-luminance) and 425-427 (ratio + the 24px / 18.66px-bold large-text rule) implement exactly this, but only against bgOf()'s resolved-hex path. Reuse that math verbatim, fed by the sampled fg/effective-bg pairs from the pick above. Fully unencumbered W3C formula, zero new dep, and the per-size thresholds map 1:1 onto the rule the probe already encodes. Pair with the sampler — together they are one gate dimension. Color.js itself is unnecessary (the ~10 lines already exist in-repo); cite Color.js only as the algorithm reference.
topPicks.license: MIT (Color.js, reference only); WCAG2 luminance-ratio math is a public W3C formula, already in-repo

topPicks.name: Chrome-CLI media-feature force-flags re-verify pass (--force-prefers-color-scheme=dark / --force-prefers-reduced-motion)
topPicks.why: Best architecture-fit on the whole list. inspect.js ALREADY shells the Chrome binary via execFile with a flags array (runChrome, line 953/1004/1038) — adding two force-flags and re-running the SAME diagnose probe is a near-literal, zero-new-dependency, zero-new-infra change. It re-runs the existing WCAG contrast logic (lum/parseRgb/bgOf) in dark state and adds a reduced-motion compliance read, closing the confirmed gap that the gate proves contrast in ONE media state only and never tests reduced-motion. Load-bearing detail the candidate gets right: use --force-prefers-color-scheme=dark (sets the media feature the page's @media answers), NOT --force-dark-mode (renderer luminance inversion).

topPicks.name: Asset-liveness + image-decode check (in-page naturalWidth===0 + url()-resource scan)
topPicks.why: Real, code-verified gap. The ERR_SHIM (inspect.js:254-275) only catches resource 'error' EVENTS on img/script/link, and CSS url() background/@font-face/mask loads never fire those. naturalWidth is used ONLY for an OVERSIZED check (line 397), never a decode-failed naturalWidth===0 check — so a 200-but-corrupt/truncated image renders 'clean' and passes the gate. Caveat that adjusts the candidate: the gate is execFile+probe, not Playwright, so the page.on('response') 4xx half is OFF-ARCHITECTURE; implement the achievable half in-page (walk document.images for complete && naturalWidth===0, and probe getComputedStyle backgroundImage url() liveness via Image() decode) inside the existing diagnose probe. Wire to the existing failed-resources finding (server.js:107).

topPicks.name: elementFromPoint occlusion gate (CTA covered by overlay / phantom scrim — in-page hit-test sweep)
topPicks.why: High-signal, on-architecture, and partially scaffolded: the element-mode probe already uses document.elementsFromPoint for overlap analysis (inspect.js:549) and getComputedStyle pointerEvents/visibility (line 537) — this generalizes that into a scored sweep over all interactive nodes in the diagnose probe. Catches the #1 AI-compose failure (a decorative gradient hero / leftover modal scrim / ::after band sitting over the primary button) that neither the static regex coherence nor the current overflow/invisible checks can see. Vanilla, zero-dep, runs in the existing probe. Merge with the phantom-scrim variant (invisible-but-pointer-capturing full-viewport layer) as one 'reachability' dimension; guard with the [open] dialog/aria-modal allowlist the candidate notes.

topPicks.name: Font-load gate via FontFaceSet (document.fonts.ready / FontFace.status)
topPicks.why: Confirmed absent and squarely on-architecture. The diagnose probe checks unloaded fonts only by document.fonts.check() on family NAMES it sees in computed styles (inspect.js:481-488) — it never reads FontFace.status, so a 404'd/CORS-failed @font-face that silently falls back to a system font passes 'clean'. That is a textbook slop tell on a vault whose authenticity law is custom typography. Awaiting document.fonts.ready then iterating FontFaceSet for status==='error'/'unloaded' is a few lines inside the existing page.evaluate-equivalent probe, zero deps, Widely-Available API. Distinct from the existing name-only check.
topPicks.license: Web standard (CSS Font Loading) — no license

topPicks.name: Vertical line-clamp truncation gate (scrollHeight > clientHeight on clipped text)
topPicks.why: Genuine gap with high real-world hit rate. The probe's overflow logic is horizontal-only (r.right > W, inspect.js:366) and the layout finding (server.js:110) only fires on page-level hOverflow — both are blind to a heading/paragraph whose back half is eaten by -webkit-line-clamp or a fixed-height card, because the element's WIDTH stays pinned. Pure DOM geometry (el.scrollHeight > el.clientHeight+1 gated on overflow:hidden/clip or line-clamp), cheap in the same per-element sweep, no pixels/baseline. Playwright itself has no native solution (open issue #10686), so this is genuine net-new coverage. Pair with the horizontal-ellipsis variant (scrollWidth>offsetWidth) as one 'truncation' dimension.

topPicks.name: Promote clipped-by-ancestor detection from element-mode into the scored gate
topPicks.why: Lowest-effort high-value item: the algorithm ALREADY EXISTS and is proven (inspect.js:554-563, the overflow-hidden ancestor + rect-spill walk) but runs ONLY in the opt-in single-selector element probe and is NEVER scored. Porting a bounded version into the diagnose per-element sweep with a penalty catches 'absolutely-positioned element chopped by an overflow:hidden parent' and 'fixed-height hero clipping its child' — cases neither hOverflow nor the vertical-clamp check fully cover. Reuses written, tested in-repo code; the work is wiring + a cap, not new infra. Candidate's line cite is essentially correct.

topPicks.name: Vanilla focus-order / keyboard-reachability audit (positive-tabindex trap, DOM-vs-visual order, aria-hidden-focusable)
topPicks.why: The gate's a11y today is contrast/alt/labels/headings/dupe-ids only (inspect.js:329, scored 490-502) — zero focus or tab-order signal. The interactive[] collector with rects already exists (line 404) and the a11y penalty block has an obvious slot, so this is an additive ~40-line in-probe change, not new infra. Catches the single most common keyboard failure in generated UIs (flex/grid/absolute reorder making DOM order != visual order) that a pixel-blind model cannot otherwise see and that axe-core leaves to MANUAL review. Reimplement vanilla — explicitly do NOT pull axe-core (MPL-2.0 file-level copyleft). Use the focus-trap 'tabbable' library only as the selector/visibility correctness reference.

topPicks.name: Visible-focus-indicator audit (el.focus() + getComputedStyle delta in headless)
topPicks.why: Pairs with the focus-order audit as one keyboard-a11y dimension; the gate currently has no :focus-state inspection at all. focus()+getComputedStyle needs no user gesture in headless Chrome, so it slots into the existing probe. WebAIM 2024 found ~78% of home pages have focus-indicator issues — high frequency. Correctness caveat the candidate flags: compare focused vs unfocused computed style (outline AND box-shadow/border/bg substitutes are valid), not just outline==='none'. Note the static audit.js focus-removed lint is CSS-source-only and repo-health, not a page gate — this is the live-DOM complement.

topPicks.name: Reduced-motion compliance probe (getAnimations() still .playing + non-zero transform/opacity transitions after reduce)
topPicks.why: The vault ships heavy motion and many snippets only CLAIM reduced-motion support; nothing verifies a composed page actually obeys the OS setting. getAnimations() is already referenced in the perf probe (inspect.js:223) so the API is proven in this harness — this reuses it in the reduced-motion re-render (driven by the force-flags pick above) to flag leaking essential motion. Vanilla, zero-dep, mirrors existing probe conventions. Strongest when paired with the force-flags pass as its provocation layer; on its own it needs that dark/reduce render to exist.

topPicks.name: Rendered line-measure linter (Range.getClientRects per-line width → true CPL vs 45–75ch band)
topPicks.why: Net-new and on-architecture. The probe has a crude width/(fontSize*0.5) CPL approximation flagging only >95ch (inspect.js:435-437); measuring actual laid-out line geometry via Range.getClientRects is materially more correct (the ch unit is the '0'-glyph width, not real character count, so source max-width:65ch does not prove the rendered measure) and catches BOTH over-wide hero copy and over-narrow columns. Vanilla native APIs in the existing probe. Slightly overlaps the existing approxCh heuristic, so it is an upgrade-in-place rather than a brand-new axis — hence not top-tier.

topPicks.name: Internal <a href> resolution + mixed-content (http://) scan (vanilla static-parse + bounded fetch)
topPicks.why: Real gap: the probe validates only resources the browser FETCHES; it never navigates <a href>, so broken #fragment anchors, dead relative page links, and http:// mixed-content subresources ship silently. The in-page fragment-id resolution and the http:// regex scan are trivially on-architecture and deterministic. Demote the remote-HEAD-liveness half: it adds network nondeterminism and is best gated behind an explicit online mode (the vault favors offline determinism). Reference linkinator (MIT) only for the bounded-pool/fragment patterns — do NOT add it or the stale GPL-flavored broken-link-checker as a dep.
topPicks.license: N/A (vanilla; linkinator MIT as reference only)

topPicks.name: Font-swap reflow / CLS gate via Layout Instability API across document.fonts.ready
topPicks.why: Partially present, so a scoped upgrade not a new axis: the PERF probe already runs a layout-shift PerformanceObserver and reports cls (inspect.js:182-185, 238) — but only in perf mode, not measured ACROSS the font-swap boundary and not gated. Re-using that observer around await document.fonts.ready to attribute font-swap reflow (no size-adjust/ascent-override on the @font-face) is a clean extension that defends typographic polish. The deeper 'deterministic settle + session-window CLS' candidates and fontaine are the natural follow-ons (settle protocol = the accuracy upgrade; fontaine MIT = the precomputed-override FIX baked into the vault's fixed font pairs). Capped at 3 because basic CLS plumbing already exists.
topPicks.license: Web standard (Layout Instability API) — no license

topPicks.name: Spacing-scale conformance gate (cluster rendered gaps → % that are token-scale multiples)
topPicks.why: Net-new rendered-geometry analogue of the static px-radius lint. The frontendmaxxing audit.js only catches border-radius:Npx via source regex (repo-health, not the page gate); nothing measures resolved gaps/margins post-layout, so clamp()/vw values that resolve off-grid (e.g. a stray 25px gap in an 8pt system) are invisible to every current check. Reads getComputedStyle gap/margin + getBoundingClientRect deltas in the existing sweep, emits an adherence % + offenders. Solid craft signal; ranked mid because it is a refinement of the anti-slop coherence dimension rather than a correctness/legibility floor.
topPicks.license: N/A (technique; USWDS spacing doc is public-domain reference)

topPicks.name: axe-core curated rule subset (need()-guarded advisory a11y scan) — INSPIRATION / use-with-care
topPicks.why: The W3C ACT-validated engine would turn the regex coherence heuristic into a real a11y signal, and the curated rule allowlist (image-alt/link-name/svg-img-alt/color-contrast computed on the RENDERED page, region/landmark noise excluded) is the genuinely valuable design decision for imagery-heavy genres. BUT two real caveats drop it below the vanilla picks: (1) MPL-2.0 is file-level copyleft — safe ONLY as an unmodified CDN/standalone load, never forked/patched into the repo (candidate's own critical note); (2) it is a ~550KB external dep behind a need()-guard, whereas the vault's pattern is vanilla in-probe checks + only deliberate perceptual deps (culori). Best framed as an OPTIONAL advisory layer, with the high-value alt/link-name/contrast rules reimplemented vanilla for the hard gate. Most of its unique value (rendered color-contrast) is already the #1 pick done vanilla.
topPicks.license: MPL-2.0 (file-level copyleft — unmodified CDN use only; do not vendor/fork)

topPicks.name: Layout-tree fingerprint + ARIA-snapshot structural gate — INSPIRATION ONLY (wrong-codebase premise)
topPicks.why: Genuinely clever (a color-blind structural baseline that survives the composer's seed-driven palette variance), and the geometry-hash idea is reimplementable vanilla in the existing probe. BUT its whole anti-dup justification is built on infrastructure that DOES NOT EXIST in designbook: there is no render-smoke.mjs, no RENDER-HARNESS-SPEC.md, no pixelmatch screenshot-diff baseline, and the gate is execfile+probe, not Playwright (so page.ariaSnapshot is unavailable without new infra). It solves the OS/GPU-fragile-baseline problem the codebase doesn't actually have. Keep the bounding-box-tree-hash as future inspiration if a cross-run regression baseline is ever added; do not build now.
topPicks.license: ARIA snapshots Apache-2.0 (Playwright, not present); tree-hash own code

categories.trends: The defensible 2024-2026 direction in this category has moved decisively from pixel-diff baselines to RENDERED-FACT verification: read the composited browser truth (computed style + geometry + sampled pixels under glyphs) rather than re-rasterize or compare screenshots. Three threads are current and real: (1) contrast/legibility measured on the ACTUAL painted background — the only correct way to gate text over gradients/images/backdrop-filters, with WCAG2 as the unencumbered enforceable floor and APCA (apca-w3) as advisory-only because its W3-restricted, source-available, web-a11y-scope license bars vendoring into a commercial tool (reimplement-from-spec or skip); (2) keyboard/reachability and occlusion as first-class gates — elementFromPoint hit-testing (the WebDriver 'click intercepted' algorithm reimplemented in-page) plus focus-order/visible-focus/aria-hidden-focusable, the gaps axe-core itself leaves to manual review and that pixel/screenshot gates are blind to by construction; (3) typography integrity — FontFaceSet.status to catch silently-failed webfonts and Layout-Instability CLS across the font-swap boundary, with fontaine's metric-override math (MIT) as the fix. Licensing is the recurring trap: axe-core is MPL-2.0 (file-level copyleft, safe only unmodified-via-CDN), apca-w3 is non-permissive source-available, and the commercial layout-comparison SaaS (LambdaTest SmartUI 2025, Applitools 'Layout' mode) validate the 'color-excluded structural regression' category but are inspiration only. The strongest fits for THIS codebase exploit what already exists — the Chrome-CLI execFile harness, the in-page <script> probe, the half-built WCAG luminance math, and the give-up bgOf() that currently skips contrast on exactly the gradient surfaces the vault generates. The visual-diff engine candidates (blazediff/honeydiff/pixelmatch/blazediff-wasm) and the structural-baseline candidates (tree-hash, ARIA snapshot) argue against a render-smoke.mjs + pixelmatch baseline harness that is NOT present in designbook, so they are premature here regardless of their (permissive MIT) licenses.

Accessibility in 2025-26 has shifted from "audit after the fact" to "verify at build time," which lands perfectly on this vault's moat: its only quality gate (coherence_check) was confirmed to score pure visual slop — hardcoded hex, unblessed durations, px radius, scale(1.0) hovers — with literally zero a11y assertion, and there is no accessibility skill among the 30 skill docs. The live regulatory/spec pressure is WCAG 2.2 (ISO/IEC 40500, Oct 2025), whose three new mechanically-verifiable criteria — 2.5.8 Target Size 24px, 2.5.7 Dragging Movements (drag MUST have a single-pointer alternative, keyboard alone does not satisfy it), and 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured — map cleanly onto a getBoundingClientRect/DOM-geometry gate, which no static snippet library can replicate. On the authoring side the current best practice is substitute-don't-just-disable (reduced-motion should cross-fade or show a poster, not hard-cut — and the vault's scroll-scrub.js was confirmed to freeze at frame 0), inert-based modal background-hiding (now Baseline since 2023), forced-colors: active with CSS system-color keywords replacing the deprecated -ms-high-contrast, prefers-contrast tiers guarded by `and (forced-colors: none)`, and APG-correct roving-tabindex/live-region/focus-trap primitives shared across overlays rather than re-hand-rolled per component (the vault has 5+ overlays each duplicating partial, buggy focus logic). License-wise the one recurring trap is axe-core: it is MPL-2.0 (file-level copyleft), NOT MIT as several briefs claimed — safe only as a test-time CDN-injected engine, never bundled into emitted pages; the WCAG/ARIA-APG algorithms it implements are public spec and freely reimplementable vanilla, which is the cleaner path for most gate rules.

topPicks.name: FocusTrap.init() — shared vanilla focus-trap + inert primitive
topPicks.why: Highest design-impact net-new snippet, verified hard against source: drawer.js literally comments 'focus trap basics' but ships NO Tab trap, no focus restore, no aria; dialog.js does trap+restore but has NO inert so the background stays in the a11y tree; modals.js/command-palette.js/celebration-screen.js each hand-roll partial logic. One ~60-line primitive (inert + re-query-on-Tab + focus restore) retrofits all five, deletes duplicated code, and makes the whole overlay layer pass WCAG 2.4.3 + 2.1.2 by default. inert is Baseline since 2023. Best home: utils/dom.js so every overlay imports it need()-free.
topPicks.license: N/A original vanilla (modeled on focus-trap MIT + inert WHATWG standard) — clean

topPicks.name: target-size verification rule (WCAG 2.2 2.5.8, getBoundingClientRect gate)
topPicks.why: The purest moat extension: a deterministic, screenshot-independent DOM-geometry check that no snippet library can copy, slotting into the existing headless-Chrome gate which (confirmed) does zero geometry — coherence_check is regex-only style scoring. 2.5.8 is brand-new WCAG 2.2 AA (ISO adoption Oct 2025) and the vault has many tiny icon buttons. Mechanically verifiable with the full spacing-exception (24px circle non-intersection) and spec carve-outs (inline targets, native UA controls). Reimplement the algorithm vanilla — do NOT bundle axe-core (MPL-2.0); use its target-size rule only as the reference algorithm to copy the obscured-area/neighbor-intersection edge cases.
topPicks.license: W3C spec reference; gate code own/MIT — clean (reimplement axe's algorithm, never bundle the MPL-2.0 dep)

topPicks.name: axe-core (Deque) — WCAG/APG conformance gate injected at the headless-Chrome verify step
topPicks.why: The industry-standard a11y engine (ACT reference impl, 4B+ downloads), CDN-injectable via addScriptTag into the existing Playwright save-gate; axe.run() returns structured violations[] for the rules the gate completely lacks today: color-contrast (works in real Chromium, not JSDOM), image-alt, label/button-name/link-name, required-attr/required-children (proves combobox/tabs/accordion ARIA), html-has-lang. Scope to the composed-page selector with runOnly high-signal tags so it scores the design not Playwright chrome; map serious/critical->block, moderate/minor->warn; degrade gracefully (skip, never hard-fail) if the CDN is unavailable. CRITICAL LICENSE CORRECTION: it is MPL-2.0 (file-level copyleft), NOT MIT as two briefs claimed — safe ONLY because it is injected read-only at test time and never bundled into emitted pages. This single candidate appeared four times in the pool with contradictory licenses; merged here.
topPicks.license: FLAG: MPL-2.0 (weak/file-level copyleft) — NOT MIT. Safe as test-time CDN-injected engine, never compiled into shipped output; note in the book.

topPicks.name: RovingTabindex controller (vanilla, APG-codified) — RovingTabindex.init()
topPicks.why: Confirmed: tabs.js, dropdown-menu.js, tree-menu.js, select.js, combobox.js each reimplement the arrow-key/tabindex loop inline, while toolbar/radio-group blocks (button-groups.css, radios.css) ship NO keyboard JS at all — so custom toolbars and radio groups are currently keyboard-inaccessible (WCAG 2.1.1 fail). One ~APG controller (orientation/loop/columns/2D-grid) satisfies 2.1.1 across toolbars, menus, radio groups, tablists, listboxes and grids and lets existing components delegate instead of duplicate. APG examples are MIT/W3C — safe to reimplement vanilla. Ship as interactions/roving-tabindex.js.
topPicks.license: MIT (w3c/aria-practices) / W3C Software License — reimplementation, clean

topPicks.name: forced-colors.css — Windows High Contrast Mode / forced-colors token layer
topPicks.why: Verified near-absent (only blocks/buttons-text-fx.css touches forced-colors; grep-confirmed zero page/token-level layer). Directly rescues the gradient/glass/mesh/aurora aesthetic the vault leans on — exactly the properties (background-image, box-shadow, backdrop-filter, background-clip:text) that revert in forced-colors, leaving borderless invisible UI. Drop-in @media(forced-colors:active): map roles to system-color keywords (Canvas/CanvasText/ButtonFace/Field/LinkText/Highlight), re-draw boundaries with 1px solid (browser auto-matches CanvasText), force icon strokes to currentColor, scope forced-color-adjust:none narrowly. Satisfies WCAG 1.4.3/1.4.11 for HCM users. Could be auto-appended by the a11y-codemod tool as a third guard. (Merges the two duplicate forced-colors candidates in the pool.)
topPicks.license: N/A CSS standards pattern (reimplement original) — clean

topPicks.name: a11y.skill.md — accessibility decision-tree skill
topPicks.why: Confirmed gap: 30 skills, ZERO for accessibility; color.skill.md covers only color-PAIR selection (not routing) and reduced-motion exists only as scattered per-snippet 'disable' guards. This is the missing judgment layer that routes the four generation-time choices: (1) content vs decorative image -> alt vs aria-hidden; (2) does a dynamic update need a live region, polite vs assertive; (3) reduced-motion -> remove/substitute/keep + the flat fallback; (4) forced-colors safe? It sits beside taste/color/art-direction skills (which actively produce gradient-heavy pages that fail HCM/contrast) and references the concrete assets above. The primary additive; the snippets/gates are what it points at. (Merges the duplicate accessibility/motion-a11y skill candidates into one canonical skill that the WCAG-2.2 SCs hang off.)
topPicks.license: vault-authored on W3C WAI + MDN prose (original guidance) — clean

topPicks.name: Announcer.polite()/.assertive() — single persistent aria-live region primitive
topPicks.why: Verified: toasts.js renders toasts visually with only an aria-label on the dismiss button — NO aria-live, NO role=status|alert, so screen-reader users never hear a toast; and there is no shared announcer anywhere (only the .sr-only class in skip-link.css). One ~40-line primitive (lazy-created polite + assertive regions, clear-then-set so repeats re-announce, empty-on-load) makes the existing Toast API accessible by routing through it, and is reused by command-palette result counts, celebration/achievement screens, survey 'Question X of N', form validation, and SPA route changes. Reuses the existing .sr-only class. (Three near-identical announcer candidates in the pool merged into this one canonical primitive.)
topPicks.license: N/A original vanilla (MDN ARIA Live Regions pattern; MIT reference impls) — clean

topPicks.name: WCAG 2.4.13 Focus Appearance token layer + C40 two-color ring + forced-colors transparent-outline
topPicks.why: Confirmed: design-system.skill.md names 'focus ring bloom' only as a decoration to animate, with NO focus-token contract or 2.4.13 thresholds; focus-visible only mirrors hover on a few button packs. A single composable token layer (--focus-ring-color/-width/-offset, :focus-visible-only) upgrades every snippet's a11y for free and is mechanically verifiable. Bundle the two high-value companions: W3C C40 two-color ring (light outline + dark box-shadow, >=9:1 inter-ring) which is the specific fix for this vault's aurora/mesh/gradient backgrounds where a single-color ring fails contrast; and the transparent-outline trick (outline:2px solid transparent + box-shadow) so glow-based focus survives forced-colors. Pairs with the focus-appearance gate below.

topPicks.name: Accessible reorder primitive (keyboard + single-pointer move) — interactions/accessible-reorder.js
topPicks.why: Confirmed: sortable.js, kanban-board.js and block-drag-handle.js are ALL drag-only (HTML5 DnD / pointer; tags show reorder/drag, zero key/aria/tabindex/live) — a WCAG 2.2 AA 2.5.7 failure the whole vault currently fails. One wrapper adds the SINGLE-POINTER alternative the SC actually requires (Move up/down buttons — keyboard-only does NOT satisfy 2.5.7), a keyboard grab/move/drop mode over roving-tabindex, and an aria-live 'moved to position 3 of 7' announcement (the pattern GitHub shipped in prod). Retrofits existing files in place. Must avoid deprecated aria-grabbed/aria-dropeffect.
topPicks.license: MIT own implementation (W3C G219 technique) — clean

topPicks.name: APG-conformant 'accessible-component' compose genre (accordion / disclosure / combobox / tabs / dialog / menu)
topPicks.why: Verified that 'compose an accordion/combobox today yields slop': combobox.css/.js expose no role=combobox/aria-expanded/aria-controls/aria-activedescendant; tabs.js sets aria-selected but NO role=tablist/tab/tabpanel nor aria-controls wiring; faq/accordion is pure-CSS with zero ARIA; dialog.js has no inert. A new compose genre that emits APG patterns with roles + states + the full keyboard model baked in turns accessibility into a default OUTPUT (like the saas/deck page archetypes), and is net-new — the engine has page/deck/diagram genres but no correctness-guaranteed interactive-widget archetype. This is the genre that the axe-core/required-attr gate then PROVES on save.
topPicks.license: W3C Software & Document License (APG reference, reimplement vanilla) — clean

topPicks.name: Contrast-audit save-gate (WCAG-2 ratio, real Chromium getComputedStyle)
topPicks.why: Net-new where it counts: the gate has zero contrast check, so a low-contrast text-on-gradient page renders 'clean' today — and the vault's gradient-heavy aesthetic is exactly where this slips through. Walk visible text nodes, read computed color + composited background (the hard part is compositing rgba/opacity/gradient ancestor layers — naive checkers fail here), apply the public-domain WCAG luminance formula, assert AA (4.5:1 / 3:1 large). Implement vanilla in the existing headless-Chrome harness — no dep needed. Optionally report APCA Lc alongside (sRGBtoY+APCAcontrast is ~30 lines, reimplementable, avoids the restricted apca-w3 license). Merges the four overlapping contrast-gate candidates in the pool; note palette-generator.js already has WCAG ratio math and color-tools-pack.css has a live contrast-checker, but NEITHER is wired into the gate — the gate is the net-new piece.
topPicks.license: Formula public-domain W3C; vanilla reimpl avoids restricted apca-w3 — clean

topPicks.name: Semantic-outline + landmark-region gate (vanilla reimpl of axe-core's structural rules)
topPicks.why: Net-new gate capability that catches the most common AI-generation semantic failures mechanically: exactly one h1, no skipped heading levels (the Lighthouse headings-skip-levels algorithm — the single most common slop failure, sections styled by eye not level), exactly one main, content inside landmarks, repeated nav/aside uniquely labeled. coherence_check does zero DOM-semantic checks and skip-link.css is a fix not a verification. axe-core implements these (page-has-heading-one/heading-order/landmark-one-main/region) but is MPL-2.0 — the underlying WCAG 1.3.1/2.4.1/2.4.6 + ARIA-APG rules are public spec, so reimplement vanilla in the headless-Chrome path. Use MIT html-validate's unique-landmark rule as the clean algorithm reference for the trickiest part (accessible-name + header/footer demotion).
topPicks.license: Algorithm reimplemented from public WCAG/ARIA spec; axe-core MPL-2.0 inspiration-only, do NOT bundle — clean

topPicks.name: Focus-appearance verification gate (ring drawn, not clipped, not obscured — 2.4.13 + 2.4.11)
topPicks.why: Highest-leverage because verification is the stated moat and it aligns with the vault's own 'screenshot is truth for did-it-draw' memory principle. For each interactive element: focus it, screenshot, assert (a) a focus indicator actually paints (pixel-delta area approximating the 2px-perimeter rule), (b) the ring isn't clipped by an overflow:hidden ancestor, (c) the focused box isn't covered by any position:sticky/fixed element (2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured). Turns 2.4.13/2.4.11 from authoring advice into a mechanical pass/fail and proves the focus-ring token layer was actually wired. Pairs with the scroll-padding-top fix for 2.4.11.
topPicks.license: N/A in-house diagnostic on existing headless-Chrome harness — clean

topPicks.name: reduced-motion alternatives layer (--motion-ok substitution token) + cross-fade/poster consumers
topPicks.why: Verified gap: taste/motion.css only ZEROS durations under reduced-motion (no substitution); scroll-scrub.js has zero reduced-motion handling and computes from scroll position so reduced-motion users see the START frame (often blank), not a designed poster; transitions hard-cut. The 2025 best practice (Comeau/web.dev/CSS-Tricks/Pope.tech) is substitute-don't-disable. Ship taste/motion-alt.css + .js mirroring the existing motion.css/motion-profiles.js pair: a --motion-ok flag (1 only inside no-preference) + blessed swap classes (.swap-slide -> opacity cross-fade; .swap-parallax -> static poster), plus the consumers: a reducedEffect:'crossfade' branch for transitions and a data-rm-poster convention that seeks scroll/parallax timelines to the final frame. Add blessed swap classes to the cohesion audit like blessed durations. (Bundles the three reduced-motion candidates; the per-snippet posters that DO exist — lottie/model-viewer/audio-viz — confirm there's no reusable convention.)
topPicks.license: MIT-equivalent technique (reimplement vanilla; blog hooks illustrative only) — clean

topPicks.name: prefers-contrast token tier (contrast.css) + [data-contrast] JS override
topPicks.why: Net-new tier on the exact custom-property substrate the vault already uses for dark-mode (responsive/dark-mode.css) and motion ([data-motion]) — confirmed there is a color-scheme tier and a motion tier but ZERO contrast tier. Swaps --text/--border/--bg/--shadow tokens inside @media(prefers-contrast:more) for a ~15:1 high-contrast variant (thicker/solid borders, gradients flattened) and prefers-contrast:less for a softened variant, letting the gradient-heavy house aesthetic degrade gracefully on request with no markup changes. The load-bearing detail is the `and (forced-colors: none)` guard (per Kilian Valkhof, prefers-contrast:more also fires under Windows forced-colors) — that guard is what keeps this tier distinct from forced-colors.css. Pair with a tiny [data-contrast] controller mirroring the proven theme-switch.js/motion-profiles.js localStorage+matchMedia idiom for an explicit in-page toggle.
topPicks.license: N/A CSS technique, vault-authored MIT-compatible — clean

topPicks.name: inert + aria-modal background-hiding upgrade for dialog.js (+ Focus-Not-Obscured scroll-padding-top)
topPicks.why: Targeted vanilla fix to an existing file rather than new surface area: dialog.js (read & confirmed) sets aria-modal=true and re-queries focusables on Tab (its trap is actually decent), but the background stays in the a11y tree / browser find — the APG dialog pattern requires the rest of the page be inert. Adding inert (Baseline) on siblings while open, plus the already-present prevFocus.focus() restore, closes the single most common real-world modal defect. Largely subsumed by the FocusTrap primitive (#1) if that ships with inert built in — keep as the concrete dialog.js retrofit. Bundle the 2.4.11 scroll-padding-top fix (--sticky-header-h drives both header height and scroll-padding-top) so keyboard focus never lands under a sticky header.

topPicks.name: Live-region presence + drag-only-without-alternative verification diagnostics
topPicks.why: Two cheap advisory gate checks that prove the new primitives were actually WIRED, not just imported: (1) detect composed pages that mutate visible status text (toasts/validation/'Saved') but have NO non-empty-on-load aria-live region; (2) flag any element with draggable=true / dragstart that has no sibling clickable move control and no aria-live (a 2.5.7 violation) — directly answers 'does a non-drag path exist?'. Make both soft/advisory (warn, don't hard-fail) to avoid blocking decorative pages. Only meaningful once the Announcer and accessible-reorder primitives exist, so they rank below those. A 24x24 target-size sub-check can ride along (but the dedicated target-size gate above is the rigorous version).
topPicks.license: N/A own diagnostics on existing harness — clean

topPicks.name: CSS contrast-color() auto-legible text token (with OKLCH fallback)
topPicks.why: Net-new small snippet + skill note as the runtime AUTO-FIX companion to the contrast audit gate: color:contrast-color(var(--brand)) returns black/white for guaranteed WCAG-2 readable text, self-correcting failing pairs with no JS. Baseline Newly Available April 2026 (Chrome 147/Firefox 146/Safari 26) with a pure-CSS OKLCH round() fallback for older engines. palette-generator.js has a JS bestText() equivalent but there is no zero-dep CSS auto-contrast token. Caveat to encode: WCAG-2 math only (APCA was pulled from the WCAG3 draft), so it's a fix not an audit — pairs with the gate, doesn't replace it. Lower value: a nice-to-have polish, not a moat piece.

topPicks.name: jakelazaroff/roving-tabindex (web component) — gold reference for the controller
topPicks.why: A zero-dep <roving-tabindex> custom element with APG-correct arrow nav as pure progressive enhancement, MIT, CDN-ready or vendorable as one file. Best used as the gold-standard reference to mirror the imperative RovingTabindex.init() against (and optionally vendored behind a need()-guard for the declarative/markup-first path). Ranked below the imperative controller because shipping both is redundant; mine eBay's archived makeup-roving-tabindex (MIT, but FLAG archived/unmaintained — inspiration only) for edge cases (disabled-skip, dynamic items, autoReset, RTL) to harden whichever ships.

categories.trends: The category is dominated by one watershed event: the W3C Design Tokens (DTCG) Format Module hit its FIRST STABLE spec on 2025-10-28, and it is now read/written by Figma (native import landing ~late 2026), Penpot, Sketch, Framer, Style Dictionary v5, Terrazzo, and Tokens Studio. That makes "emit a .tokens.json" the single highest-leverage, license-clean handoff artifact a token-driven vault can produce — and the vault has the perfect substrate (everything is CSS-var-driven) yet currently emits ONLY a Markdown DESIGN.md, with zero machine-interop path (verified: no $value/$type/tokens.json anywhere). Two real gotchas define current correctness: (1) in 2025.10, color $value is now an OBJECT {colorSpace, components, hex} and dimensions/durations are {value, unit} objects — NOT bare hex/px strings (older guides are stale); (2) "DTCG" is really two dialects — strict 2025.10 (structured objects, single flat file, Resolver Module for themes) vs the Tokens-Studio/Penpot envelope (hex strings, px-string dims, multi-set + $themes.json/$metadata.json), so an exporter must aim per-destination and an importer must read both. Theming is standardizing too: the Resolver Module (sets/modifiers/resolutionOrder) maps almost 1:1 onto the vault's pal-* classes, and on the CSS side light-dark() (Baseline since May 2024) collapses the vault's duplicated light + [data-theme=dark] token tables into one declaration (behind an @supports fallback). Beyond tokens, two adjacent deliverable surfaces are wide open: PDF/print handoff (Chrome 131+ now honors @page + margin-box headers/counters, but silently DROPS string-set/running()/target-counter and won't fetch url() images in @page margin boxes headlessly — base64 only), and email HTML (Cerberus skeletons + VML bulletproof buttons + caniemail data as a lint source). The throughline and the vault's actual moat is VERIFICATION: the existing checkCoherence() gate asserts only hex/duration/radius/slop/imagery, so net-new gates — theme-wide contrast (WCAG + APCA), a Chrome print-readiness linter, a caniemail email-compat score, and a Gmail 102KB clip guard — turn "we rendered something" into "we verified it will hand off correctly," which is exactly where this category compounds. Heavy build tools (Style Dictionary, Terrazzo, Maizzle, Dispersa, VMail, @adobe/token-diff-generator) are correctly inspiration-only: all are Node/Go/WASM CLIs, wrong layer for a zero-dep browser compose runtime — mine their algorithms/output shapes, never bundle. License posture is clean (DTCG = royalty-free W3C; caniemail/Cerberus/Terrazzo/Tokens Studio plugin = MIT; Style Dictionary = Apache-2.0); the only flags are Penpot SERVER (MPL-2.0, a handoff destination not a dep), apca-w3's W3C Software License (avoid wholesale — get APCA via MIT colorjs.io instead), and closed SaaS hosts (Figma, Tokens Studio cloud) which are skill/inspiration targets only.

topPicks.name: export-tokens.js — DTCG 2025.10 round-trip exporter/importer for the vault's CSS-var token set
topPicks.why: The spine of the entire category and the highest design-impact item. Verified net-new: zero .tokens.json/$value/$type anywhere in the vault; the only machine output today is a Markdown DESIGN.md (mcp-server design_system). The vault is the ideal substrate (structure.css + colors/scales.css + colors/palettes.css are all CSS-var-driven) so this single ~150-line vanilla file turns every composed page into a portable artifact that opens in Figma/Penpot/Style Dictionary/Terrazzo/Tokens Studio with ZERO build step. Round-trips: EXPORT walks styleSheets[].cssRules, classifies --* by name into $type, emits 2025.10-correct OBJECT color {colorSpace,components,hex} and {value,unit} dims (the load-bearing interop detail — bare hex fails strict validators), and preserves the vault's scales→palette→semantic indirection as {brace} aliases; IMPORT dereferences with cycle detection back to :root{--x}. Bake in the cross-origin try/catch around cssRules. This is the one find that unlocks all the others.
topPicks.license: MIT (original code); format is royalty-free W3C DTCG spec — unencumbered

topPicks.name: Token-set contrast auditor (theme-wide semantic-pair WCAG + APCA gate)
topPicks.why: Best moat-fit in the category. Verified gap: mcp-server checkCoherence() asserts only hardcoded-hex/duration/px-radius/slop-hover/imagery — NO contrast or a11y dimension at all — and palette-generator.js already has the exact per-pair WCAG contrast()/luminance math plus the vault owns the exact 17-role token contract (--fg/--bg/--surface/--on-accent/--ok/--warn/--danger…). This walks a composed theme's RESOLVED tokens and fails save if any semantic fg/bg pair drops below its tier floor, extending the anti-slop save-gate to accessibility (a contrast-failing theme is itself an AI-slop tell). WCAG path is fully reimplementable from existing code; for APCA pull it via MIT colorjs.io or ship the documented 'APCA-style' Lc estimator — do NOT vendor apca-w3 (its W3C Software License restricts scope and the APCA name).
topPicks.license: Original vault code (reuses palette-generator luminance math); pair APCA via MIT colorjs.io — avoid apca-w3 W3C Software License

topPicks.name: Semantic-intent token tier + [data-brand] multi-brand override convention (3-tier: primitive → intent → component)
topPicks.why: Names and fixes a real architectural gap I verified directly: colors/palettes.css has 0 var() references — every pal-* hardcodes hexes straight into ~17 flat roles with NO indirection, so there is nowhere to express intent (--color-action/--color-critical/--surface-raised) and no way to re-skin across brands without rewriting palettes. Adding a Tier-2 tokens-semantic.css that aliases intent→role + thin [data-brand=x]{--color-action:var(--accent)} override files unlocks true white-labeling and stacked core+brand+sub-brand+campaign theming on top of the existing 61 palettes WITHOUT touching component CSS, and gives the composer a brand= param. Frost's themeable-systems model; concept is CC-BY, implementation original vanilla CSS. Pair with DTCG naming so the intent tier is durable, not ad-hoc.
topPicks.license: Original convention/CSS; concept credit Brad Frost (CC-BY blog) — no code dependency

topPicks.name: print.css — general-purpose @page page-master (Chrome-honored margin-box headers/footers + counters)
topPicks.why: The missing reusable PDF-export primitive, and verified net-new: the only print CSS in the vault is deck.css's deck-only block (break-after:page, no @page/headers/counters) and email-receipt; print-typography.skill.md is a TOOLS decision-tree (Playwright/Paged.js/WeasyPrint/Typst), not a page-master snippet. Built ONLY from what Chrome 131+ actually honors: @page size/margin via vars, :first/:left/:right for covers and mirrored binding, running headers/footers via margin boxes + counter(page)/counter(pages). High design-impact (turns strong screen composition into real paged deliverables) and pairs with the existing headless-Chrome verify (printToPDF, printBackground:true). Bake the author-time facts: position:running()/content:element()/string-set are broken in Chrome (use margin boxes); url() images in @page margin boxes silently fail headless (base64 only).

This is the print analogue of the contrast auditor — it extends the verification moat to a dimension checkCoherence() cannot currently assert, with no new dep. The single best companion to print.css: page-master emits, gate verifies. Reimplementable; sources are MIT/CC-BY docs.

Reimplementing the three Cerberus shells (600px wrapper, MSO ghost tables for Outlook, percentage stacking, mso-table-lspace resets, bulletproof CTA) as a compose-genre lets the engine emit newsletter/announcement/digest as email-safe HTML — a net-new handoff OUTPUT, not a duplicate of the receipt. Each token declared ONCE as light-dark(light,dark) collapses the two maintained token tables into one; three-state toggle is just overriding color-scheme. Given any composed page it renders swatches (hex + WCAG contrast vs --bg/--fg), the fluid type scale as live specimens, the 4/8 spacing ramp, shadow/elevation ladder, radii, and motion-duration chips — the visual+exportable sibling of the markdown tool, reusing docs-layout.css + swatches-palette.css. Best as a short tokens.skill.md decision-tree (flat file vs sets+resolver, brace-alias rules, color-object fidelity, validate-before-save) that documents BOTH the standards-track resolver AND the Tokens-Studio $themes/selectedTokenSets convention, since real-world files use the latter — a robust importer reads both. Pair with the exporter, not standalone. Consolidates the several near-duplicate resolver/mode candidates the researchers submitted.

topPicks.name: Vanilla CSS-inliner (styleSheets → cssRules → specificity merge) for the email genre
topPicks.why: The load-bearing build step that makes the email genre actually send-ready and verified net-new (closest hits, mesh-editor.exportCss / email-pack, are CSS-string export and inbox mockups, not an inliner). ~60-100 lines vanilla: iterate document.styleSheets, querySelectorAll(rule.selectorText), write declarations onto el.style ordered by computed specificity — no juice/premailer/WASM because the browser already parses CSS and matches selectors. Gmail/Outlook strip <style>, so composed email HTML is only deliverable once flattened to style= attributes. Caveats to bake: cross-origin sheets throw on cssRules (try/catch+skip); inline specificity is 1-0-0-0 so source !important needs re-application; preserve a media-query/@font-face <style> for Apple Mail.

topPicks.name: break-control / pagination utilities (cards, tables, figures, orphans/widows)

Confirmed distinct from masonry.css/gallery-pack.css (those use break-inside for multicol layout, not print pagination). Complements print.css; note orphans/widows are hints Chrome may ignore.

topPicks.name: VML bulletproof button (hybrid VML + accessible HTML CTA)
topPicks.why: Fixes the single biggest Outlook break and verified net-new: email-receipt.css ships a plain padded <a> CTA with NO VML fallback, so the button collapses to unpadded text in Outlook desktop (still can't render padded/rounded <a> in 2026; 25-40% of B2B opens). Small self-contained vanilla snippet: <!--[if mso]> v:roundrect (arcsize, fillcolor, w:anchorlock) paired with a semantic styled <a> everywhere else. Zero deps, plugs straight into the Cerberus genre. Public technique, MIT-spirit.

topPicks.name: token-diff verification capability (vanilla DTCG .tokens.json differ + swatch report)
topPicks.why: Operationalizes 'handoff implies versioning' as a save-gate artifact and verified net-new (changelog-popover/roadmap are display UIs; design_system emits CSS-var DESIGN.md, not a token-set differ). Loads two DTCG files, flattens to canonical dotted-path maps, and emits ADDED/REMOVED/CHANGED/TYPE-CHANGED/DEPRECATED/RENAMED plus a visual before/after swatch report. Crucially it must canonicalize ordering first (the spec says property order is NOT significant, so raw text-diff is noisy) and should diff RESOLVED values (resolve aliases/modes before diffing, or a re-pointed alias shows as noise). Optional CIEDE2000 ΔE grades each color change as imperceptible/noticeable/major. Borrow the taxonomy from MIT VMail / Apache-2.0 @adobe/token-diff-generator (both Node/WASM — inspiration only, do not bundle); reimplement vanilla, diff by PATH not UUID so it works on any DTCG file.
topPicks.license: Original vanilla JS; W3C DTCG spec is CC/royalty-free — unencumbered

categories.trends: The 2026 AI-design-tool frontier has split into three races, and the candidate set maps cleanly onto them. (1) The verifier-as-moat race: Lovable/Stitch/Figma-agent now self-validate generated UI in a real browser (axe accessibility, console/network, layout-shift, broken-links) before presenting — turning "did it render" into "is it correct + stable + accessible + on-system." This is where Design Book already leads (it owns a headless-Chrome save-gate that most export tools lack) and where the highest-leverage net-new work sits: the gate already computes rendered contrast, CLS, un-dimensioned images, multi-viewport overflow, and a left-border-card tell, so the durable wins are the diagnostics it genuinely lacks — ARIA/role/focus-order (axe), reduced-motion HONORING (render-twice diff), state-completeness (empty/loading/error coverage on data views), and motion-budget/effect-stacking. (2) The fan-out-with-distinctness race: every builder (Lovable, Magic Patterns, v0, Stitch) now emits N coherent directions from one intent, and the research consensus (Stanford parallel-prototyping; Celestial-style coverage-guided sampling) is that distinctness must be MEASURED, not hoped for — the missing counterpart to Design Book's existing coherence gate. (3) The structured-input + auto-layout race: deck-AI (Gamma/PPTAgent) classifies content-shape→slide-archetype; diagram-AI (Eraser/Excalidraw/Mermaid) parses a tiny DSL then auto-lays-out via Sugiyama/dagre/ELK; sketch-AI (make-real/Sketch2Code) ingests boxes→layout. Design Book composes genre-driven only and has no auto-layout engine, so these are real new front-doors. Cross-cutting: imagery is the #1 "AI slop" tell and the consensus fix is a portfolio (raster heroes + deterministic palette-conditioned SVG scenes/patterns + low-byte placeholder hashes for graceful load), not a single raster model. License-wise the field is mostly permissive (MIT/BSD/Apache) or vanilla-reimplementable; the genuine flags are axe-core/elkjs (file-level weak copyleft — safe as unmodified CDN injects) and APCA's reference impl (restricted — reimplement the formula). Note: several "the vault lacks X" claims are already partly shipped — culori perceptual color distance, CLS, multi-viewport overflow, rendered contrast, and section-level provenance (data-db-ref) all already exist, which reshuffles the ranking below.

topPicks.name: axe-core (ARIA/role/focus-order WCAG audit injected into the existing headless probe)
topPicks.why: Highest moat-fit: turns the existing save-gate from a ~6-check heuristic into a real WCAG audit, injected exactly the way inspect.js already works (drop axe.min.js, call axe.run(), read violations JSON). CRITICAL DEDUPE: this candidate was submitted TWICE and BOTH overstate the gap — verified inspect.js (lib/inspect.js L311-431) ALREADY does rendered color-contrast (getComputedStyle fg/bg + ratio), missingAlt, unlabeledInputs, namelessButtons, headingSkips, duplicateIds. So axe's contrast/alt overlap and must be deduped; its genuinely net-new value is ARIA validity, role-vs-allowed-attribute, required-children/parent roles, accessible-name computation, landmark/list structure, and positive-tabindex focus-order — the single biggest class of bug a pixel-blind agent emits and the gate cannot see. MPL-2.0 is file-level weak copyleft: safe to inject the UNMODIFIED CDN bundle; only forking axe's own files triggers obligations. CDN-load behind a need()-guard, degrade if blocked.
topPicks.license: MPL-2.0 (file-level weak copyleft — safe as unmodified CDN inject; do NOT fork-and-edit)

topPicks.name: State-completeness gate (empty / loading / error variant audit on data views)
topPicks.why: Pure moat with zero new assets needed — the vault ALREADY renders every state (verified: blocks/skeleton-pack.css, components/empty-state.css, empty-states-pack-2.css, loading-pages.css, error-pages.css). What is missing is a verifier that FAILS/WARNS when a composed list/table/feed/dashboard ships without empty+loading+error. Confirmed checkCoherence (mcp-server/server.js L1088-1169) checks only hex/duration/radius/slop-hover/imagery/motion — zero state-coverage logic. NNG-cited 2025 data (92% of AI dashboards no empty state, 78% no error state, 100% bare spinner) makes this the highest-leverage 'last 30%' gate. Turns existing snippets into a guarantee.
topPicks.license: N/A (original gate; pattern from public UX writing + NNG audit)

topPicks.name: prefers-reduced-motion HONORING verification (render-twice diff)
topPicks.why: Cleanly net-new and confirmed absent: grepped lib/inspect.js for emulate/prefers/Media — ZERO media-feature emulation anywhere, while autofix.js already INJECTS a reduced-motion block that nothing verifies. The gap between 'we injected the query' and 'motion actually quiets' is exactly the kind of silent failure the gate exists to catch. Render once normal, once with reduce emulated (page.emulateMediaFeatures or --force-prefers-reduced-motion=reduce), assert animations collapse / getAnimations() empties. Pairs with the existing two-render screenshot path; zero third-party dep, fully headless.

topPicks.name: Coverage-guided mimetic variant sampling — book_variants distinctness gate
topPicks.why: The missing counterpart to the vault's existing coherence gate, and a perfect moat fit. Verified book_variants (designbook/server.js L142-151) today just re-runs composeWithScore over seeds [0,1,2] with NO pairwise-distinctness measurement — so variants can be cosmetic reshuffles. This adds a MEASURED distinctness gate: compute pairwise distance over an axes vector (archetype x palette-mood x density x motion x type-feel) and reject sets where two variants differ only cosmetically. arXiv inspiration, reimplemented vanilla — no code dep. Backed by the Stanford parallel-prototyping divergence principle (separate lower-value candidate). Distinctness MEASURED, not hoped for.

topPicks.name: variant-axes fan-out skill (ranked axes matrix + preset-snap coherence)
topPicks.why: The net-new ORCHESTRATION layer that drives the distinctness gate above. The vault has every ingredient (taste axes, TastePresets in taste/presets.js, structure archetypes, seed semantics) but no skill that deliberately picks N maximally-far-apart points in {archetype x palette-mood x density x motion x type-feel} and SNAPS each to a valid TastePreset so coherence is guaranteed by construction. This is precisely the Lovable/Magic Patterns/Stitch fan-out move, reimplemented MIT-clean (closed-SaaS = inspiration only). Pairs with the gate: skill generates coherent-by-construction directions, gate proves they diverge.
topPicks.license: Lovable docs closed SaaS — pattern only, MIT-clean to reimplement

DEDUPE NOTE: one of the six (colored-left-border cards) is ALREADY detected in the RENDERED taste probe (inspect.js L857 'left-border-cards'), so claim it for the static scanner only and skip the rest of that overlap. The genuinely net-new static signals: emoji-as-icon, centered-text density, gradient-text-on-numbers, identical-feature-card repetition, banned generic-copy cadence. Merge the two near-duplicate submissions (the Developers Digest checklist + qinnovates vibecheck-slop-stopper) into one: vibecheck (MIT) supplies the TOML->regex architecture, HIGH/MED/LOW confidence tiers, and the co-occurrence rule (fire only when 3+ tells share a file) that prevents dinging legit pages. Reimplement vanilla in audit.js — ripgrep is not bundleable into the zero-dep model.

The placement primitives already exist (verified layout/dashboard-grids.css grid-template-areas, structure/structure.css) — so this is a layout-driven path over the SAME ~920 snippets, no vision model, already verifiable by the save-gate. A deterministic classifier that picks a shell from content shape (1 number->big-stat; 2 sets->comparison/2x2; chronology->timeline; pithy line->quote; section start->divider) turns compose-deck from 'caller picks layout' into 'engine picks layout.' topPicks.name: Mobile native-screen-transitions.css + scr-flow.js navigator + executable mobile-flow genre (bundle)
topPicks.why: The motion + runtime half of mobile-design that the static .scr-* shell is missing, and confirmed absent: grepped for Flow.push/scr-flow/fade-through/container-transform across mobile/ and transitions/ — none of the native push-with-parallax-underlay, fade-through, or container-transform exist (every current transition is a WEB page-to-page effect). The shell already encodes the screen graph (tabs=roots, drill=push, task=sheet) but nothing EXECUTES it — .app-flow only lays phones side-by-side. Ship the three together: the CSS keyframe grammar (iOS push/pop + Material 4-pattern, reduced-motion guarded), the zero-dep stack navigator (push/pop/switchTab/present/dismiss), and the compose-genre that wires the 7 documented archetypes into a transition-aware playable graph the verifier can screenshot. Stitch/Uizard are closed-SaaS inspiration. iOS HIG behavior unprotected; Material timing tokens from Apache-2.0 M3 spec.

topPicks.name: Vanilla Sugiyama layered-DAG auto-layout + Mermaid-subset text-to-diagram parser (diagram engine bundle)
topPicks.why: The missing diagram ENGINE, and a confirmed gap: there is NO diagram.css in the vault (grepped — only diagrams.skill.md references it) and no node/edge auto-layout anywhere, so the composer can only fill a fixed SVG template, never lay out an arbitrary graph. Ship the two together because one feeds the other: a ~80-line Mermaid-flowchart-SUBSET parser (direction header, implicit node creation A-->B, labeled/shaped nodes, edge labels, subgraph) gives an LLM-friendly text->nodes/edges input that round-trips to real Mermaid for free, then the classic 4-phase Sugiyama pipeline (~150 lines: cycle-break, layer-assign + dummy nodes, barycenter crossing-reduction, x-assign + edge polylines) lays it out into clean vanilla SVG that already clears the authenticity gate. Both zero-dep, MIT reference impls to port. Reserve elkjs (EPL-2.0, CDN-as-is) / dagre (MIT) as the cdn-snippet fallback for orthogonal/port-heavy cases — pick ONE fallback path, do not ship all three.

topPicks.name: Motion-intent skill + motion-budget anti-effect-stacking gate (bundle)
topPicks.why: Net-new DECISION + ENFORCEMENT layer for motion, confirmed: the vault has motion PRIMITIVES (taste/motion-profiles.js + data-motion 3 numeric levels, animations/stagger.js, animations/spring.js with raw stiffness/damping) but neither an intent->choreography mapping nor a coherence signal for the OPPOSITE failure the static gate misses. Verified checkCoherence has only a binary NO-MOTION tell — no effect-stacking / easing-mixing / stagger-budget signal, even though the vault's own anti-slop rule #5 forbids exactly that stacking. Ship together: the skill maps an intent verb (energetic/calm-premium/editorial/playful/technical/cinematic) to ONE coherent package (reveal cadence + easing role-set + stagger budget), and the gate enforces it (count distinct motion subsystems + easing families + max simultaneous staggered items, penalize over an intent-appropriate budget). M3-Expressive's spatial-vs-effects spring split (Apache-2.0 vocabulary, reimplemented on existing spring.js) is the supporting discipline.
topPicks.license: N/A original skill + vault-authored gate; M3 vocabulary Apache-2.0 (reimplemented)

topPicks.name: get_tokens / get_outline / get_provenance MCP read surface (designbook://tokens/{slug})
topPicks.why: The highest-leverage MCP parity gap with Figma/Subframe, and partly pre-wired: verified the vault ALREADY stamps section-level provenance — data-db-ref="s<i>-<slot>" on every section and scr<i>-<shell> on every screen (lib/vault.js L75-110) — but NO tool surfaces it. Net-new, cheap because the data exists: (1) get_tokens returns a saved page's resolved color/spacing/type/radius/duration table so an agent edits in token-space and stays inside the gate (distinct from frontendmaxxing's pre-defined palette resource); (2) get_outline returns a sparse section tree from the existing data-db-ref markers (the read-before-write 'metadata' tier between book_list_pages and the token-heavy book_get_page); (3) get_provenance maps section->source snippet (Code-Connect analogue, lowest of the three). Folded into one read-surface workstream because they share the provenance/token-resolution plumbing.

topPicks.name: Palette-conditioned seeded SVG scene generator + GeoPattern + ThumbHash (imagery-without-raster bundle)
topPicks.why: Directly answers the documented imagery gap: book_compose's own description says drafts ship as wireframes with grey placeholders and 0 images, and visual-first genres REQUIRE >=1 real image to clear the save-gate. Verified the vault has only FIXED assets (svg/illustrations.js fixed scenes, components/gradient-avatar.js square identicons, backgrounds/patterns-mega.css tileable but NOT content-seeded) — none generate a unique palette-matched full-bleed hero or content-keyed section backdrop, and grep for thumbhash/blurhash found nothing. Ship together as book_autofill_imagery's zero-dep fallback when mflux is unavailable: GenScene.get(seed,{palette,style}) for hero artwork (MIT technique, reimplement via createElementNS), GeoPattern math (BSD-2) for per-section backdrops keyed on heading, and ThumbHash (MIT, ~100-line decode) for palette-true blur-up so first paint is never a gray box. Seed off the page slug to match compose seed semantics. This is the moat agentic builders lack (they lean on one raster model + Unsplash).
topPicks.license: MIT (georgedoescode technique, ThumbHash) + BSD-2 (GeoPattern) — reimplement vanilla

topPicks.name: Realistic-copy generator via @faker-js/faker (seeded) + state-completeness.skill.md
topPicks.why: Confirmed absent (grep for faker/lorem across INDEX/skills/taste returned only stray hits, none in compose) and addresses a top AI-slop tell. faker v10.5.0 (MIT, ESM/CDN, Jun-2026) behind a need()-guard replaces lorem/'you@example.com' with domain-plausible seeded copy (names, finance rows, products); faker.seed(n) is DETERMINISTIC, mirroring compose seed semantics so pages read real reproducibly. Degrade to a built-in name/company array offline. Pair with state-completeness.skill.md — the human-readable decision tree (genre -> required states -> which existing snippet fills each slot + the anti-lorem rule) that makes the state-completeness GATE and this copy layer usable by the composer rather than ad hoc. Both complement, not duplicate, structure.skill.md (which sequences sections but says nothing about state variants or copy realism).

topPicks.name: container-relative fluid type (clamp + cqi/cqw units)
topPicks.why: Cleanly net-new, verified by grep: ZERO cqi/cqw/cqh occurrences across ALL vault CSS, and responsive/ has only fixed-threshold breakpoints.css + viewport.css (no container-queries.css with intrinsic units). cqi-based clamp (font-size: clamp(14px, 10px + 1.33cqi, 20px) on an inline-size container) gives a card that reads correctly in a 280px sidebar AND a 900px hero with no breakpoints — the intrinsic-layout primitive the vault lacks. Small, MIT-reimplementable utility addition. Gotcha to encode: wrap the grid item (don't make it the container); custom props don't work inside @container conditions.

topPicks.name: Blessed spacing scale token set (4/8 geometric --sp-*) + spacing-snap half of token-snap gate
topPicks.why: Confirmed the canonical SET is genuinely missing: grep for --sp-/--space-/--spacing- returned ZERO across all CSS — the vault has blessed MOTION durations (enforced by unblessed-duration) and a fluid type scale but NO discrete spacing scale, so no 'unblessed-spacing' check is even possible today. Add --sp-1..--sp-9 (4/8/12/16/24/32/48/64/96) as an additive token layer (like the motion tokens), then a checkCoherence pass can snap 13px->12px / 30px->32px and flag off-grid values. IMPORTANT DEDUPE: the COLOR half of the proposed token-snap gate and the standalone 'OKLCH ΔE nearest-token matcher' are LARGELY ALREADY SOLVED — verified lib/color-audit.js already imports culori (differenceCiede2000 + oklch converter) and runs perceptual ΔE2000 flat-gradient + OKLCH-hue rainbow analysis folded into the gate. So culori IS a designbook dep and a 'vanilla color-distance utility the vault lacks' is unnecessary; the real net-new is the SPACING scale + snapping action, not color distance. Scope this to spacing.
topPicks.license: Public domain (USWDS reference) — original token values

topPicks.name: Focus-visible presence + positive-tabindex / focus-order probe
topPicks.why: Lightweight dependency-free a11y checks the existing probe genuinely lacks: inspect.js checks nameless buttons / unlabeled inputs but never whether focus is VISIBLE or ORDERED. Detect outline:none/0 with no :focus-visible replacement (the #1 'keyboard users can't see where they are' regression) and flag positive tabindex. Good standalone if axe is deemed too heavy; if axe IS adopted, keep ONLY the focus-visible-presence half (axe doesn't reliably check for a visible focus indicator since that needs computed-style inspection) and drop the positive-tabindex overlap. Net-new, runs in the same single headless pass.

topPicks.name: Region-role recognition vocabulary + Region-IoU layout-fidelity gate (make-real / Sketch2Code, paired with the box front-door)
topPicks.why: The parser and the closed-loop verifier for the box-layout front-door above — only valuable WITH it, hence ranked just under it. The vocabulary skill (box-with-X=image, sidebar+main=grid, squiggly=body text, red=annotation-strip, rectify-hand-drawn) is the missing wireframe-glyph->role decision tree complementing structure.skill's genre->section map. The Region-IoU gate (Sketch2Code's 7 region classes scored by intersection-over-union of rendered getBoundingClientRect vs requested boxes) extends the existing headless save-gate with a spec-conformance dimension it lacks — fail the save if header/hero/grid drifted out of their requested cells. getBoundingClientRect + IoU is trivially vanilla in the harness; academic-paper methods, free to reimplement, no dep.
topPicks.license: make-real prompt MIT; Sketch2Code arXiv method free to reimplement — no dep

topPicks.name: Broken internal-link / dead-anchor gate (vanilla fragment + placeholder-href resolver)
topPicks.why: Real moat gap, confirmed: the harness hard-fails on requestfailed / HTTP>=400 for FETCHED resources (CSS/JS/CDN) but never exercises a composed page's <a href> nav links or in-page #fragment anchors — a dead href='#pricing' or an href='#'/'' placeholder slop link ships clean today. Build the high-value 80% vanilla in the headless harness: resolve every #fragment against getElementById, flag placeholder hrefs (an AI-generation tell), HEAD-probe same-origin links in ONLINE mode only. lychee (Rust, MIT/Apache) stays inspiration for full external-URL crawl — do NOT ship a Rust binary in the gate. Lower than the top gates because fragment/placeholder breakage is rarer than a11y/state gaps, but it's clean, dep-free, and named in the brief.
topPicks.license: Vanilla fragment/placeholder check = no dep; reference tool lychee MIT/Apache-2.0

categories.trends: 2026 web-design taste is splitting along three live axes, and these candidates cluster on all three. (1) The anti-AI-slop / tactile-print reaction: editorial-spread track-grids, true baseline grids (initial-letter + the newly-shipped text-box-trim), risograph overprint, pure-CSS halftone, deckle edges, and the neo-brutalist token system are the codifiable answers to "gradients + no-images = AI tell" — physical, human, anti-flat surfaces that no amount of generic gradient-mesh produces. (2) Spatial depth on flat screens via the now-stable CSS scroll-driven-animations stack (animation-timeline: view()/scroll(), named timelines + timeline-scope, scroll-state container queries) plus progressive-blur DoF and atmospheric perspective — zero-JS, off-main-thread replacements for whole classes of GSAP/rAF scroll handlers. (3) Verification and interoperability as the actual moat: the W3C DTCG 2025.10 token format (with its structured color object replacing flat hex) is the first real interchange standard worth implementing, and the headless-Chrome harness can be extended into measurable a11y/typography gates (widow/orphan detection, 45-80ch measure floor/ceiling, semantic reduced-motion, contrast + forced-colors emulation) — capabilities competitors cannot copy by pasting a snippet. Cross-cutting: native CSS keeps eating JS (light-dark(), contrast-color(), scroll-state(), CSS carousels), and a11y has graduated from advice to baseline (WAI-ARIA APG behaviors, WCAG 2.2.2 in-page motion toggle, forced-colors, live-region announcers) — the vault ships visuals but is thin on the headless-behavior layer and on enforced (not hand-claimed) accessibility. License posture is clean across the top picks (web standards, MIT, OFL, vault-native); the only flags are SaaS-closed inspiration sources (Mobbin/Savee ToS forbid scraping; MotionScore is paywalled) and the support caveats on bleeding-edge CSS (text-box-trim/scroll-state/CSS-carousels are Chromium-ahead, must ship behind @supports with graceful fallback).

topPicks.name: Contrast + forced-colors render VERIFICATION gate
topPicks.why: Highest moat-fit item in the set: a diagnostic, not a copyable snippet. Confirmed gap — book_inspect's diagnose mode surfaces contrast/a11y FACTS but there is no save-FAILING contrast (WCAG AA / APCA Lc) gate, and zero forced-colors anywhere in the vault. color.skill claims palettes are 'contrast-checked' BY HAND; this makes it enforced and automated, fully buildable in the existing headless-Chrome harness via Emulation.setEmulatedMedia. Turns accessibility from advice into a measurable gate, exactly the vault's differentiator.
topPicks.license: n/a (built on existing harness + open WCAG/APCA math)

topPicks.name: Widow/orphan + short-last-line detector (Range.getClientRects)
topPicks.why: Net-new gate with no library needed; the Range-rects line-reconstruction is the only reliable post-render way to count visual lines + measure the last line. Confirmed: vault verification (book_inspect taste mode + inspect.js) collects type-scale ratio and long-lines>95ch but has ZERO widow/orphan logic. Author (Rutter/clagnut) explicitly notes CSS text-wrap:pretty/balance does NOT eliminate widows — so a JS verifier is the correct tool, and this is precisely the AI/template tell the moat should catch. Cheap fix (pretty/balance or nbsp), high signal.

topPicks.name: reduced-motion compliance gate (audit.js upgrade: semantic, not string-presence)
topPicks.why: Pure moat: upgrades a crude substring check (passes if 'prefers-reduced-motion' appears even in a comment, never checks JS) into a real gate that confirms the reduce block actually neutralizes the SAME animated selectors, flags the animate-from-hidden-stays-invisible footgun, and requires a matchMedia guard in any rAF/WAAPI/setInterval JS. The vault ships 920+ motion snippets and claims every one has a guard — this is the capability that FAILS the ones that don't. Reuses existing --strict plumbing. Verification = the thing nobody can paste.

topPicks.name: DTCG export/import skill (W3C Design Tokens Format Module 2025.10)
topPicks.why: The vault's FIRST interchange format and a clean role-token 1:1 map (bg/fg/accent/semantic -> color; motion -> duration; density -> dimension; fontPair -> fontFamily; +{alias} curly references). design-system.skill already speaks 'tokens' as CSS vars and get_palette returns role-token JSON, but NEITHER is W3C-DTCG-shaped or importable — confirmed. Makes vault taste interoperable with Figma/Tokens Studio/Style Dictionary/Penpot/Framer instead of a closed CSS-var island. The one real conversion (structured {colorSpace,components,hex} object, not flat hex) is well-scoped. Permissive W3C CG report.

topPicks.name: Scroll-stacking cards section (sticky pin + scale-down stack)
topPicks.why: The single most-shipped 2025-26 award 'how it works / feature reveal' archetype and a verified gap: card-stack.css is a timed rotating deck and sticky-scroll-reveal.css is a 2-col text/image-swap — NEITHER is the pinned-and-scaling vertical stack. structure.skill has no such slot in any archetype. Vault has the ingredients (scroll-pin.js, container-scroll.js, aos-lite) but not THIS composed section. Implementable as vanilla animation-timeline:view() with IntersectionObserver fallback; degrades to a plain list under reduced-motion. High realism payoff, deterministic, composer-ready.

topPicks.name: Neo-brutalist taste preset + token layer (data-aesthetic="brutalist")
topPicks.why: Direct fit for the 'taste folder coordinates type+motion+spacing' model and the documented 6-aesthetic gap (vault has exactly 6: minimal/editorial/energetic/luxury/playful/technical — no brutalist). The raw atoms are confirmed scattered (shadows.css .shadow-brutal, buttons-extra .xbtn-brutalist, section-frames grid-guides, fonts mono-technical) with NO coordinating preset/aesthetic. The net-new is pure coordination: a [data-aesthetic=brutalist] block + presets.js row + --nb-* token group + a decision-tree skill. Directly counters the MEMORY 'gradients+no-images=AI tell' law by being a flat, raw, anti-perfection system. Vault-native, no dep.
topPicks.license: vault-native (neobrutalism.dev spec is editorial reference only)

topPicks.name: Structured-data / JSON-LD injector + verify gate (FAQPage, Product, Organization, BreadcrumbList)
topPicks.why: Confirmed total gap — zero schema.org/JSON-LD across 931 snippets, yet every real marketing page ships it. Maps to the verification moat (derive JSON-LD from composed sections + a gate asserting it's valid AND matches visible content) not just a snippet. Critical 2026 nuance correctly flagged: Google REMOVED FAQ rich results (May 2026), so the value is GEO/AEO (citation by AI Overviews/ChatGPT/Perplexity), not rich snippets. Open vocab (schema.org/JSON-LD W3C). Degrades to nothing if absent. Slightly below the pure-a11y gates only because content-match validation is fiddlier.

topPicks.name: Scroll-driven variable-font weight (animation-timeline on font-variation-settings)
topPicks.why: Confirmed precise gap: vault uses animation-timeline exactly ONCE (headers-pro solidify), NEVER on font axes; its variable-font kinetics are hover/keyframe (variable-fonts.css) or cursor-DISTANCE lerp (variable-font-cursor.js) — scroll-POSITION axis morphing is absent. Pure CSS, @supports-guarded, trivially reduced-motion-safe (animation:none). High leverage for editorial/landing heroes and on-trend kinetic type. The documented overflow:clip-not-hidden gotcha makes it a non-obvious snippet worth codifying.

topPicks.name: editorial-spread (compose genre + vanilla CSS layout set)
topPicks.why: A genuinely net-new compose archetype with four addable primitives the vault lacks: baseline-grid snap (vertical-rhythm.css only approximates via margins), the 3-track full/body/margin grid, marginalia toggle, and balanced multi-column text flow. Confirmed adjacent-only: quote-blocks pull-quote (1 of 8), heading-pack editorial-serif, grid-systems generic 12-col, masonry CSS-columns-for-cards. Vanilla, zero-dep, deterministic and verifier-safe. Strong differentiation as the print-magazine counter to bento. Just below the top tier because it's a larger build and leans on the baseline-kit candidate to truly 'measure like print'.

topPicks.name: Pure-CSS halftone (radial-gradient + multiply + contrast)
topPicks.why: Confirmed gap: the ONLY vault halftone is shaders/halftone.glsl.js (WebGL, needs three.js/GL); dot-pattern is a uniform fixed background, not luminance-driven. Ana Tudor's ~3-declaration technique gives a true content/luminance-driven print halftone with NO GPU, degrade-friendly, usable on text/heroes/over images. Reuses existing duotone palettes for a duotone-halftone variant. Centerpiece of the tactile-print trend, trivially reimplementable, credit-only license.
topPicks.license: technique / blog tutorial (freely reimplementable; credit Ana Tudor)

topPicks.name: Risograph misregistration / overprint effect (layer-offset + mix-blend-mode: multiply)
topPicks.why: Confirmed biggest hole in the tactile-print vocabulary: step-risograph is only a banded gradient palette and halftone.glsl is a shader — NEITHER produces the channel-split/overprint-on-real-content riso look. 100% vanilla (stacked tinted copies + multiply + feTurbulence speckle), pairs with existing noise-grain.css. Directly counters AI-flatness per the MEMORY authenticity law. Reimplement-from-primitives (the studio-ity URL is a closed SaaS reference only).
topPicks.license: public CSS technique (reimplement; URL is closed-SaaS reference)

topPicks.name: a11y.js — shared vanilla a11y primitives (Radix-behavior layer, reimplemented)
topPicks.why: High-leverage moat primitive that raises ~10 components at once and fixes a real correctness bug: dialog.js caches first/last focusable ONCE at open (confirmed via search — focus-trap is per-component, breaks when DOM changes), tabs.js hardcodes roving-tabindex, and there is NO inert/scroll-lock anywhere. Reimplement the Radix-spec'd (WAI-ARIA APG, vendor-neutral) getFocusable/trapFocus that re-queries, rovingTabindex, and inert+scroll-lock as one vanilla utils file. Radix is React = inspiration only, correctly flagged. The kind of foundational layer the visuals-heavy vault lacks.
topPicks.license: MIT (Radix = behavior reference, not a dep)

topPicks.name: Adaptive theming engine: color-scheme + light-dark() + contrast-color()
topPicks.why: Confirmed: responsive/dark-mode.css duplicates every token across light/dark/@media blocks (3x maintenance) with no auto-contrast; NONE of light-dark(), contrast-color(), or color-scheme three-state exist. light-dark() halves the CSS and contrast-color() makes the 61 palettes self-correct.
